2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200113143120/https://indianexpress.com/article/cities/chandigarh/chandigarh-caa-is-essentially-anti-poor-says-former-ias-officer-kannan-gopinathan-6202416/|url-status=live}} They are also concerned that all citizens will be affected by the bureaucratic exercise of the NRC where they will have to prove their citizenship for inclusion in the registry.{{Cite web|url=https://www.news18.com/news/india/its-not-just-about-muslims-all-indians-will-have-to-queue-up-to-prove-they-are-indians-owaisi-2432283.html|title=It's Not Just About Muslims, All Indians Will Have to Queue Up to Prove They are Indians: Owaisi|website=News18|date=22 December 2019|access-date=17 January 2020|quote="Why are we protesting? We are protesting because, in the country, in the name of religion, preparation is being made to make us not just second-class citizens but stateless," [Owaisi] claimed, adding it is a loss for everyone.|archive-date=19 January 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200119221021/https://www.news18.com/news/india/its-not-just-about-muslims-all-indians-will-have-to-queue-up-to-prove-they-are-indians-owaisi-2432283.html|url-status=live}}{{Cite web|url=https://scroll.in/article/948969/the-nrc-is-a-bureaucratic-paper-monster-that-will-devour-and-divide-india|title=The NRC is a bureaucratic paper-monster that will devour and divide India|last1=Mathur|first1=Nayanika|website=Scroll.in|date=14 January 2020|access-date=14 January 2020|archive-date=14 January 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200114221037/https://scroll.in/article/948969/the-nrc-is-a-bureaucratic-paper-monster-that-will-devour-and-divide-india|url-status=live}} The protesters have raised voices against authoritarianism and the police crackdown in universities to suppress protests. Protesters in [[Assam]] and other [[Northeast India|northeastern states]] do not want [[Indian citizenship]] to be granted to any refugee or immigrant, regardless of their religion, as they fear it would alter the region's demographic balance, resulting in a loss of their political rights, culture, and land. They are also concerned that it will motivate further [[Human migration|migration]] from [[Bangladesh]] that could violate the [[Assam Accord]] which was a prior agreement reached with the central government on migrants and refugees.{{cite news|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/explained/assam-protests-citizenship-amendment-bill-nrc-northeast-bandh-5543785/|title=Explained: Why Assam, Northeast are angry|last1=Saha|first1=Abhishek|date=20 January 2019|work=The Indian Express|quote=Protesters have expressed fears that the prospect of citizenship will encourage migration from Bangladesh. They have cited several grounds for opposing this. Demography: This will change across Northeastern states, protesters say, as has already been happening in Assam and Tripura over decades of migration (see graphs). "Assamese could become the second language. Then there is also the question of loss of political rights and culture of the indigenous people," said former Chief Minister Prafulla Mahanta, who was the face of the Assam Movement (1979–85) against illegal immigration, and one of the signatories to the Assam Accord at the culmination of the movement. (...) Protesters say the Bill goes against the Assam Accord and negates the ongoing update of the National Register of Citizens (NRC).|access-date=16 December 2019|archive-date=18 January 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190118005323/https://indianexpress.com/article/explained/assam-protests-citizenship-amendment-bill-nrc-northeast-bandh-5543785/|url-status=live}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-india-citizenship-idUSKBN1YF0QI|title=India's parliament passes citizenship law, protests flare|date=11 December 2019|work=Reuters|access-date=17 January 2020|quote=The bill will take away our rights, language and culture with millions of Bangladeshis getting citizenship (...) people in Assam and surrounding states fear that arriving settlers could increase competition for land and upset the region's demographic balance|archive-date=24 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191224233534/https://www.reuters.com/article/us-india-citizenship-idUSKBN1YF0QI|url-status=live}} The protests started in Assam on 4 December 2019, after the bill was introduced in parliament. Later on, protests erupted in [[Northeast India]], and subsequently spread to the major cities of India. On 15 December, major protests took place near [[Jamia Millia Islamia]] in New Delhi and [[Aligarh Muslim University]]. As the protests broke out, mobs burnt and destroyed public as well as private properties and several railway stations were vandalised.{{Cite web|url=https://www.livemint.com/news/india/caa-violence-arson-in-south-delhi-as-protesters-torch-four-buses-two-injured-11576412295374.html|title=CAA: Violence, arson in south Delhi as protesters torch four buses; two injured|date=15 December 2019|website=Livemint|access-date=17 January 2020|quote=Four buses were set ablaze by a mob and two fire officials were injured in stone pelting as the protests against the newly enacted Citizenship Act" (...) "The situation turned critical when a bus was burned by the protestors and police got into action|archive-date=24 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191224094526/https://www.livemint.com/news/india/caa-violence-arson-in-south-delhi-as-protesters-torch-four-buses-two-injured-11576412295374.html|url-status=live}}{{Cite web|url=https://theweek.com/5things/884376/violent-protests-over-citizenship-law-continue-india|title=Violent protests over citizenship law continue in India|website=The Week|access-date=17 January 2020|quote=Protesters set fire to buses and vandalized railroad stations|archive-date=24 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191224094525/https://theweek.com/5things/884376/violent-protests-over-citizenship-law-continue-india|url-status=live}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.business-standard.com/article/pti-stories/no-bullet-fired-by-police-during-protests-at-jamia-mha-officials-119121700271_1.html|title=Delhi cops didn't fire during Citizenship Act protests at Jamia: Officials|date=17 December 2019|work=Business Standard India|access-date=17 January 2020|quote=Several vehicles, including buses, were burnt down and public properties destroyed during the protests against the Citizenship Amendment Act, which was passed by Parliament last week.|archive-date=1 January 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200101143435/https://www.business-standard.com/article/pti-stories/no-bullet-fired-by-police-during-protests-at-jamia-mha-officials-119121700271_1.html|url-status=live}} Police [[2019 Jamia Milia Islamia attack|forcibly entered]] the campus of Jamia, used batons and [[tear gas]] on the students, and more than 200 students were injured while around 100 were detained overnight in the police station. The police action was widely criticised and resulted students across the country protesting in solidarity.{{Cite web|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/supreme-court-declines-to-take-up-police-action-against-jamia-protesters-says-it-is-a-law-and-order-problem/story-WlBa5J75IkHiW3iMbLffnK.html|title=High courts can inquire, says Supreme Court on plea over police action against protesters|date=17 December 2019|website=Hindustan Times|access-date=17 January 2020|archive-date=16 January 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200116100426/https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/supreme-court-declines-to-take-up-police-action-against-jamia-protesters-says-it-is-a-law-and-order-problem/story-WlBa5J75IkHiW3iMbLffnK.html|url-status=live}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2019/dec/17/india-protests-students-condemn-barbaric-police|title=India protests: students condemn 'barbaric' police|last1=Ellis-Petersen|first1=Hannah|date=17 December 2019|work=The Guardian|access-date=17 December 2019|issn=0261-3077|archive-date=19 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191219194220/https://www.theguardian.com/world/2019/dec/17/india-protests-students-condemn-barbaric-police|url-status=live}} The protests resulted in thousands of arrests and 27 deaths as of 27 December 2019.{{Cite web |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/anti-citizenship-amendment-bill-assam-protest-1627464-2019-12-11 |title=1,000 detained as anti-Citizenship Amendment Bill protests intensify in Assam |date=11 December 2020 |author=Hemanta Kumar Nath |website=India Today |access-date=27 February 2020 |archive-date=24 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230224183207/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/anti-citizenship-amendment-bill-assam-protest-1627464-2019-12-11 |url-status=live }} Two 17-year-old minors were among those reported to have been killed due to police firing during a live ammunition on protesters in Assam.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/citizenship-amendment-act-caa-protests-guwahati-minor-boys-dead-1628160-2019-12-13|title=2 minor boys killed in police firing during anti-CAB protests in Guwahati|last1=Kumar Nath|first1=Hemanta|date=13 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=16 December 2019|archive-date=13 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191213220437/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/citizenship-amendment-act-caa-protests-guwahati-minor-boys-dead-1628160-2019-12-13|url-status=live}} On 19 December, the police issued a complete ban on protests in several parts of India. As a result of defying the ban, thousands of protesters were detained.{{Cite web |date=2019-12-19 |title=Indian police ban protests amid citizenship law outrage |url=https://www.cnbc.com/2019/12/19/indian-police-ban-protests-amid-citizenship-law-outrage.html |access-date=2022-06-06 |website=CNBC |language=en |archive-date=25 September 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925101633/https://www.cnbc.com/2019/12/19/indian-police-ban-protests-amid-citizenship-law-outrage.html |url-status=live }} == Background == [[File:A child taking part in an anti-CAB NRC protest.jpg|thumb|A child taking part in an anti-CAB NRC protest with [[Jamia Millia Islamia]] students and locals.]] ===Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019=== {{Main|Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019}} [[The Citizenship (Amendment) Bill, 2019]] (CAB) was introduced by the Home Minister, [[Amit Shah]] on the floor of the Parliament of India on 9 December 2019 in response to the exclusion of 1.9 million people, predominantly Hindus and Muslims{{Cite web|url=https://www.livemint.com/mint-lounge/features/focus-assam-s-complicated-tryst-with-caa-11577418654545.html|title=FOCUS: Assam's complicated tryst with CAA|last=Samrat|date=27 December 2019|website=Livemint|access-date=27 February 2020|archive-date=27 March 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200327160838/https://www.livemint.com/mint-lounge/features/focus-assam-s-complicated-tryst-with-caa-11577418654545.html|url-status=live}} in the [[National Register of Citizens for Assam]].{{Cite news|agency=Press Trust of India|url=https://www.business-standard.com/article/pti-stories/nrc-must-for-national-security-will-be-implemented-shah-119100101129_1.html|title=Hindu, Buddhist, Sikh, Jain refugees won't have to leave India: Amit Shah|date=1 October 2019|work=Business Standard India|access-date=27 February 2020|archive-date=4 March 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200304222617/https://www.business-standard.com/article/pti-stories/nrc-must-for-national-security-will-be-implemented-shah-119100101129_1.html|url-status=live}} The Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019 (CAA) was passed by the [[Parliament of India]] on 11 December. It amends the [[Indian nationality law|Citizenship Act of 1955]] to grant a swifter path to Indian citizenship under the assumption of [[religious persecution]] to any individual belonging to the specific minorities of [[Hindus]], [[Sikhs]], [[Buddhist]]s, [[Jain]]s, [[Parsis]] and Christians from [[Afghanistan]], [[Bangladesh]] and [[Pakistan]], who entered India on or before 31 December 2014.{{Cite web|url=https://pib.gov.in/newsite/PrintRelease.aspx?relid=195783|title=Parliament passes the Citizenship (Amendment) Bill 2019|website=pib.gov.in|access-date=21 December 2019|archive-date=16 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191216092712/https://pib.gov.in/newsite/PrintRelease.aspx%3Frelid%3D195783|url-status=live}} The Act also seeks to relax the requirement of residence in India for citizenship by [[naturalisation]] from 11 years to 5 years for migrants covered under the Act.{{Cite web|url=https://www.cnn.com/2019/12/11/asia/india-citizenship-amendment-bill-intl-hnk/index.html|title=India passes controversial citizenship bill that excludes Muslims|first1=Helen|last1=Regan|first2=Swati|last2=Gupta|first3=Omar|last3=Khan|date=11 December 2019|publisher=CNN|access-date=4 March 2020|archive-date=15 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191215224043/https://www.cnn.com/2019/12/11/asia/india-citizenship-amendment-bill-intl-hnk/index.html|url-status=live}} However, the Act does not mention [[Muslims]] and does not offer the same eligibility benefits to Muslim immigrants or immigrants belonging to other religions from those countries.{{Cite web|url=https://www.vox.com/future-perfect/2019/12/12/21010975/india-muslim-citizenship-bill-national-register|title=India just redefined its citizenship criteria to exclude Muslims|last1=Samuel|first1=Sigal|date=12 December 2019|website=Vox|access-date=27 February 2020|archive-date=16 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191216034906/https://www.vox.com/future-perfect/2019/12/12/21010975/india-muslim-citizenship-bill-national-register|url-status=live}} The Act also does not mention any benefits for various other refugees which form the bulk of the refugees living in India, such as [[Sri Lankan Tamils in India|Sri Lankan Tamil refugees]] who faced persecution during the [[Sri Lankan Civil War]],{{cite news|last1=Shaji|first1=K.A|url=https://www.huffingtonpost.in/entry/citizenship-amendment-act-sri-lankan-tamil-refugees_in_5dff246ce4b05b08bab6aff2|title=CAA: Sri Lankan Tamil Refugees Say The New Law Has Killed Their Hopes Of Citizenship|date=23 December 2019|work=HuffPost|access-date=28 December 2019|archive-date=24 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191224164100/https://www.huffingtonpost.in/entry/citizenship-amendment-act-sri-lankan-tamil-refugees_in_5dff246ce4b05b08bab6aff2|url-status=live}} [[Rohingya Refugee Crisis|Rohingya refugees]] who were victims of the [[Rohingya genocide]], [[Bhutanese refugees|Nepali refugees]] who faced [[ethnic cleansing in Bhutan]], and [[Tibetan refugees in India|Tibetan Buddhist refugees]] who faced persecution in China.{{Cite news|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2019/12/13/indias-new-law-may-leave-millions-muslims-without-citizenship/|title=India's new law may leave millions of Muslims without citizenship|last1=Chaudhry|first1=Suparna|date=13 December 2019|newspaper=The Washington Post|access-date=18 December 2019|archive-date=16 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191216141007/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2019/12/13/indias-new-law-may-leave-millions-muslims-without-citizenship/|url-status=live}} According to the [[Intelligence Bureau (India)|Intelligence Bureau]], the immediate beneficiaries of the new law will be 25,447 Hindus, 5,807 Sikhs, 55 Christians, 2 Buddhists and 2 Parsis.{{cite news|last1=Saha|first1=Abhishek|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/explained/assam-protests-citizenship-amendment-bill-nrc-northeast-bandh-5543785/|title=Explained: Why Assam, Northeast are angry|date=20 January 2019|work=The Indian Express|quote=From its records, the IB gave a count of 31,313 (25,447 Hindus, 5,807 Sikhs, 55 Christians, 2 Buddhists and 2 Parsis) who have been given Long Term Visas on their claim of religious persecution in the three countries.|access-date=16 December 2019|archive-date=18 January 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190118005323/https://indianexpress.com/article/explained/assam-protests-citizenship-amendment-bill-nrc-northeast-bandh-5543785/|url-status=live}} ===Response=== The passage of the Act sparked massive protests in India. Protesters in [[Assam]] and other [[Northeast India|northeastern states]] oppose the grant of [[Indian citizenship]] to any refugee or immigrant, regardless of their religion, because they fear it would alter the region's demographic balance. They have campaigned since the 1970s against all refugees, and they fear that the new law will cause a loss of their political rights, culture and land. They are also concerned that it will trigger more migration from [[Bangladesh]] as well as violate the [[Assam Accord]], which was a prior agreement reached with the central government on migrants and refugees. After the act was passed, protests in the northeastern region turned violent. Authorities have arrested over 3000 protesters as of 17 December 2019,{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-protests-assam-congress-worker-arrested-1628876-2019-12-17|title=Assam violence: Congress worker, 190 more arrested|website=India Today|access-date=18 December 2019|date=17 December 2019|archive-date=29 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220929011149/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-protests-assam-congress-worker-arrested-1628876-2019-12-17|url-status=live}} and some news outlets have described these protests as [[riots]].{{Cite web|url=https://news.yahoo.com/assams-sons-soil-cherish-protest-symbol-073729856.html|title=Assam's 'sons of the soil' cherish new protest symbol|publisher=Yahoo! News|access-date=18 December 2019|archive-date=18 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191218084920/https://news.yahoo.com/assams-sons-soil-cherish-protest-symbol-073729856.html|url-status=live}} Protesters say that the Act violates Clause 5 and Clause 6 of the 1985 [[Assam Accord]].{{cite news |last1=Dutta |first1=Prabhash K. |title=What is the Assam Accord of 1985 and how the amended citizenship law challenges it? |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/news-analysis/story/what-is-assam-accord-of-1985-and-how-amended-citizenship-law-challenges-it-1627965-2019-12-13 |access-date=19 December 2019 |work=India Today |date=13 December 2019 |ref=Assam Accord |archive-date=21 January 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210121100616/https://www.indiatoday.in/news-analysis/story/what-is-assam-accord-of-1985-and-how-amended-citizenship-law-challenges-it-1627965-2019-12-13 |url-status=live }} Critics have stated that the amendment Act is unconstitutional.{{Cite web|url=https://globalnews.ca/news/6301241/india-protests-citizenship-bill-explainer/|title=Here's why people are protesting India's citizenship bill|website=Global News|access-date=27 February 2020|archive-date=23 March 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230323191411/https://globalnews.ca/news/6301241/india-protests-citizenship-bill-explainer/|url-status=live}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2019/dec/11/india-to-bring-in-law-denying-citizenship-to-muslim-migrants|title=Indian citizenship law discriminatory to Muslims passed|date=11 December 2019|website=The Guardian|access-date=17 December 2019|archive-date=16 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191216012044/https://www.theguardian.com/world/2019/dec/11/india-to-bring-in-law-denying-citizenship-to-muslim-migrants|url-status=live}}{{cite news |url=https://apnews.com/article/ebcc80d2f7e4bf0d911168d4e25d1f97 |title=India's Parliament passes contentious citizenship bill |work=Associated Press |date=11 December 2019 |access-date=10 February 2021 |archive-date=28 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220128094841/https://apnews.com/article/ebcc80d2f7e4bf0d911168d4e25d1f97 |url-status=live }} The major opposition political parties state that it violates Constitution's Article 14, one that guarantees equality to all. They allege that the new law seeks to make Muslims second-class citizens of India, while preferentially treating non-Muslims in India.{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/et-explains/citizenship-amendment-bill-what-does-it-do-and-why-is-it-seen-as-a-problem/articleshow/72436995.cms|title=Citizenship (Amendment) Bill: What does it do, why is it seen as a problem|date=16 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=16 December 2019|archive-date=29 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231029090124/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/et-explains/citizenship-amendment-bill-what-does-it-do-and-why-is-it-seen-as-a-problem/articleshow/72436995.cms|url-status=live}} Critics of the Act have also stated that due to the [[National Register of Citizens of India|National Register of Citizens (NRC)]], Muslims could be made [[Statelessness|stateless]], while the Citizenship Amendment Act would be able to shield people with Hindu, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi or Christian identity as a means of providing them with Indian citizenship even if they fail to prove that they were citizens of India under the stringent requirements of the NRC. Some critics allege that it is a deliberate attempt at [[Disfranchisement|disenfranchising]] and segregating Muslims in line with the [[ethnonationalist]] [[Hindutva]] ideology of the ruling [[Bharatiya Janata Party]] (BJP).{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/news-analysis/story/jamia-violence-amended-citizenship-act-and-speculation-over-nationwide-nrc-1628621-2019-12-16|title=Jamia violence, amended Citizenship Act and speculation over nationwide NRC|date=16 December 2019|first=Prabhash K.|last=Dutta|website=India Today|access-date=17 December 2019|archive-date=29 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220929021357/https://www.indiatoday.in/news-analysis/story/jamia-violence-amended-citizenship-act-and-speculation-over-nationwide-nrc-1628621-2019-12-16|url-status=live}}{{Cite web|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/opinion/columns/redefining-the-republic-citizenship-amendment-bill-6162526/|title=Citizenship law, proposed nation-wide NRC will revise conception of group rights in India|date=12 December 2019|website=The Indian Express|access-date=17 December 2019|archive-date=27 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191227230644/https://indianexpress.com/article/opinion/columns/redefining-the-republic-citizenship-amendment-bill-6162526/|url-status=live}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.asianage.com/india/all-india/161219/orwellian-nightmare-how-cab-uses-the-law-to-discriminate.html|title=Orwellian nightmare: How CAB uses the law to discriminate|last1=Sreenivasa|first1=Baawa Prasad Kunale and Vinay Korragayala|date=16 December 2019|website=The Asian Age|access-date=17 December 2019|archive-date=28 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220928031249/https://www.asianage.com/india/all-india/161219/orwellian-nightmare-how-cab-uses-the-law-to-discriminate.html|url-status=live}} [[Tavleen Singh]] described the Act as India's first [[Nuremberg Laws|Nuremberg Law]].{{cite news|url=https://thewire.in/video/watch-karan-thapar-interview-tavleen-singh|title=Watch 'Anti-Muslim Prejudice is in Modi's DNA': Karan Thapar Interviews Tavleen Singh|first1=Karan|last1=Thapar|date=17 February 2020|access-date=18 February 2020|work=[[The Wire (India)|The Wire]]|archive-date=18 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200218173304/https://thewire.in/video/watch-karan-thapar-interview-tavleen-singh|url-status=live}} The Act was criticised by various NGOs, students bodies, and liberal, progressive, and socialist organisations across the country, with the [[Indian National Congress]] and other major political parties announcing their staunch opposition. Protests led by these groups are concerned that the new law discriminates against Muslims, and believe that Indian citizenship should also be granted to Muslim refugees and immigrants. The states of [[Rajasthan]], West Bengal, [[Kerala]], Maharashtra, [[Madhya Pradesh]], Jharkhand{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/elections/jharkhand/wont-implement-caa-nrc-in-jharkhand-hemant-soren/article30322078.ece|title=Won't implement CAA, NRC in Jharkhand: Hemant Soren|last1=Barik|first1=Satyasundar|date=16 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=10 January 2020|issn=0971-751X|archive-date=13 January 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200113161816/https://www.thehindu.com/elections/jharkhand/wont-implement-caa-nrc-in-jharkhand-hemant-soren/article30322078.ece|url-status=live}}{{subscription required}} and [[Chhattisgarh]] – all ruled by political parties that had poor relations with the BJP in the year 2020 – announced that they would not implement either the [[National Register of Citizens]] (NRC) or the Citizenship Amendment Act. The states of [[Bihar]], Andhra Pradesh and [[Odisha]] have however refused to only implement the NRC, while the state of [[Punjab, India|Punjab]] and the union territories of Delhi and [[Puducherry (union territory)|Puducherry]] have refused to implement the Act while only expressing disapproval of the NRC.{{Cite web|url=https://www.livemint.com/politics/news/president-gives-assent-to-cab-5-states-refuse-to-implement-it-11576262342021.html|title=President gives assent to CAB, 5 states refuse to implement it|last1=Varma|first1=Anuja and Gyan|date=14 December 2019|website=Livemint.com|access-date=16 December 2019|archive-date=16 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191216113941/https://www.livemint.com/politics/news/president-gives-assent-to-cab-5-states-refuse-to-implement-it-11576262342021.html|url-status=live}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/cong-govts-in-punjab-mp-rajasthan-chhattisgarh-puducherry-won-t-implement-caa-harish-rawat-1630219-2019-12-20|title=Cong govts in Punjab, MP, Rajasthan, Chhattisgarh, Puducherry won't implement CAA: Harish Rawat|first1=Hemanta|last1=Kumar Nath|date=20 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=21 December 2019|archive-date=21 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191221041916/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/cong-govts-in-punjab-mp-rajasthan-chhattisgarh-puducherry-won-t-implement-caa-harish-rawat-1630219-2019-12-20|url-status=live}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.freepressjournal.in/india/from-bengal-to-punjab-10-states-which-have-refuses-to-implement-caa-nrc|title=From Bengal to Punjab: 10 states which have refuses to implement CAA-NRC|website=LaFree Press Journal|access-date=25 December 2019|archive-date=25 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191225124641/https://www.freepressjournal.in/india/from-bengal-to-punjab-10-states-which-have-refuses-to-implement-caa-nrc|url-status=live}} The states of [[West Bengal]] and [[Kerala]] have also put a hold on all activities relating to the preparation and update of the [[National Population Register]] which is necessary for the Census as well as the implementation of the [[National Register of Citizens]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-narc-protest-after-west-bengal-kerala-too-puts-hold-npr-work-1630230-2019-12-20|title=After West Bengal, Kerala too puts on hold NPR work|date=20 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=21 December 2019|archive-date=21 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191221005557/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-narc-protest-after-west-bengal-kerala-too-puts-hold-npr-work-1630230-2019-12-20|url-status=live}} Although some of the states have opposed the Act, the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (India)|Union Home Ministry]] clarified that states lack the legal power to stop the implementation of CAA. The Ministry stated that "The new legislation has been enacted under the Union List of the 7th Schedule of the Constitution. The states have no power to reject it."{{cite news|url=https://www.outlookindia.com/newsscroll/sporadic-protests-in-mp-against-caa/1689856|title=Sporadic protests in MP against CAA|date=19 December 2019|work=outlookindia.com|access-date=21 December 2019|archive-date=21 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191221120347/https://www.outlookindia.com/newsscroll/sporadic-protests-in-mp-against-caa/1689856|url-status=live}} The [[Indian Union Muslim League]] and various other bodies have also petitioned the [[Supreme Court of India]] to strike down the Act as illegal and unconstitutional.{{cite news |title='Anti-Muslim' citizenship law challenged in Indian court |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-50739593 |access-date=17 December 2019 |work=BBC |date=12 December 2019 |archive-date=15 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191215095658/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-50739593 |url-status=live }} == Chronology == === December 2019 === * '''4 December''' :*The Citizenship (Amendment) Bill, 2019 was cleared by the [[Union Council of Ministers|Union Cabinet]] for introduction in the parliament.{{cite web |title=Controversial Citizenship (Amendment) Bill to Be Tabled in Lok Sabha on Monday |url=https://thewire.in/government/citizenship-amendment-bill-to-be-tabled-in-lok-sabha-on-monday |website=The Wire |access-date=8 December 2019 |archive-date=8 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191208204513/https://thewire.in/government/citizenship-amendment-bill-to-be-tabled-in-lok-sabha-on-monday |url-status=live }} :*After the bill was cleared, violent protests erupted in Assam, especially in [[Guwahati]], and other areas in the state.{{Cite news |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/anti-cab-protests-in-assam-police-opens-fire-on-protestors/articleshow/72487251.cms |title=Anti-CAB stir: People defy curfew, police |url-status=live }} :*In [[Dispur]], several thousands of protesters broke down police barricades to protest in front of the [[Assam Legislative Assembly]] building.{{Cite web |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2019/12/india-northeast-protesters-rally-citizenship-bill-191210111006001.html |title=In India's northeast, protesters rally against citizenship bill |publisher=Al :*Demonstrations were held in [[Agartala]].{{Cite web |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/agartala/anti-cab-protests-turn-violent-in-tripura/articleshow/72472009.cms |title=Anti-CAB protests people died and fifty people were injured in the protests against the Act.{{cite news |title=Assam: Death toll rises to 5 in protests against citizenship act |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/videos/news/assam-death-toll-rises-to-5-in-protests-against-citizenship-act/videoshow/72666788.cms |access-date=15 December 2019 |date=15 December 2019 |url-status=live }}{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-50805092|title=India PM plea for calm as citizenship unrest rages|date=16 December 2019|access-date=16 December 2019|archive-date=29 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191229143926/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-50805092|url-status=live}} * '''9 December''' :*The bill was introduced in the [[Lok Sabha]] by the Minister of Home Affairs, [[Amit Shah]].{{Cite web |url=https://liveupdates.hindustantimes.com/india/parliament-winter-session-live-updates-citizenship-amendment-bill-in-lok-sabha-21575867624874.html |title=Citizenship Bill gets Lok Sabha nod, Rajya |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191211202337/https://liveupdates.hindustantimes.com/india/parliament-winter-session-live-updates-citizenship-amendment-bill-in-lok-sabha-21575867624874.html |archive-date=11 December 2019 |access-date=12 December 2019}} * '''10 December''' :*The bill was passed with 311 members voting in favour and 80 against.{{Cite web |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/will-citizenship-bill-pass-in-rajya-sabha-1626919-2019-12-10 |title=Citizenship Bill has smooth sail in Lok Sabha, will Amit Shah clear Rajya Sabha test? |date=10 December 2019 |website=India Today |access-date=10 December 2019 |archive-date=10 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191210102243/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/will-citizenship-bill-pass-in-rajya-sabha-1626919-2019-12-10 |url-status=live }}{{Cite web '''11 December''' :*The bill was subsequently passed by the [[Rajya Sabha]] with 125 votes in favour and 105 votes against it. Those parties that had voted in favour include BJP allies such as the [[Janata Dal (United)]] and the [[All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam|AIADMK]] and non-aligned parties such as the [[Biju Janata Dal]].{{Cite news |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/parliament-clears-citizenship-amendment-bill/article30279517.ece |title=After a heated debate, Rajya Sabha clears Citizenship (Amendment) Bill |last1=Nath |first1=Damini |date=11 December 2019 |work=The Hindu |access-date=11 December 2019 |last2=Singhfirst2=Vijaita |issn=0971-751X |archive-date=14 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191214115506/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/parliament-clears-citizenship-amendment-bill/article30279517.ece |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} * '''12 December''' :*After receiving assent from the [[President of India]], the bill assumed the status of an act.{{Cite news |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/citizenship-amendment-bill-gets-presidents-assent-becomes-act/articleshow/72498176.cms |title=Citizenship (Amendment) ll gets |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191217100441/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/citizenship-amendment-bill-gets-presidents-assent-becomes-act/articleshow/72498176.cms |url-status=live }} The act would come into force on 10 January, chosen by the Government of India, and would be notified as such.{{Cite web |url=http://egazette.nic.in/WriteReadData/2019/214646.pdf |title=The Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019 |date=12 December 2019 |website=[[The Gazette of India]] |access-date=14 December 2019 |archive-date=14 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191214081252/http://egazette.nic.in/WriteReadData/2019/214646.pdf |url-status=live }} :*Dipanjal Das and Sam Stafford were killed in police firing during a protest in Guwahati.{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/anti-caa-stir-violent-protests-rock-jamia-amu-bengal-boils-uneasy-calm-in-assam/articleshow/72698734.cms|title=Anti-CAA stir: Violent protests rock Jamia, AMU; Bengal boils, uneasy calm in Assam|date=15 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=21 December 2019|archive-date=10 March 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200310225833/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/anti-caa-stir-violent-protests-rock-jamia-amu-bengal-boils-uneasy-calm-in-assam/articleshow/72698734.cms|url-status=live}} :*[[Akhil Gogoi]] was taken in preventive custody. [[File:A poster trying to depict the CAA process at Shaheen Bagh protests New Delhi.jpg|thumb|A poster at Shaheen Bagh protests New Delhi]] * '''13 December''' :* UK, US, France, Israel and Canada issued [[travel warning]]s for their citizens visiting India's north-east region, where the protests were mainly taking place, asking them to "exercise caution".{{Cite web |url=https://in.usembassy.gov/travel-alert-for-u-s-citizens-protests-in-northeastern-states-121319/ |title=Travel Alert for U.S. Citizens: Protests in Northeastern States |date=13 December 2019 |website=U.S. Embassy & Consulates in India |access-date=14 December 2019 |archive-date=14 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191214183505/https://in.usembassy.gov/travel-alert-for-u-s-citizens-protests-in-northeastern-states-121319/ |url-status=live }} :*The Chief Ministers of the Indian states of West Bengal, Punjab, Kerala, Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh had stated that they would not implement the act.{{Cite news |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/anti-citizenship-act-protests-us-uk-france-israel-issue-travel-advisories/article30304634.ece |title=Anti-Citizenship Act protests: U.S., U.K., France, Israel issue travel advisories |date=14 December 2019 |work=The Hindu |access-date=14 December 2019 |issn=0971-751X |archive-date=16 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191216103701/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/anti-citizenship-act-protests-us-uk-france-israel-issue-travel-advisories/article30304634.ece |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} * '''14 December''' :*Ishwar Nayak was killed in police firing at a protest in Assam. :*Thousands of people protested against the law at the [[Jantar Mantar, New Delhi|Jantar Mantar]] in New Delhi. * '''15 December''' :*In Assam, Abdul Alim, a protester, died due to police firing in a protest the previous day.{{cite web |url=https://www.sentinelassam.com/north-east-india-news/assam-news/kin-of-anti-citizenship-amendment-act-agitation-martyr-awaits-justice-515153 |title=Kin of anti-Citizenship Amendment Act agitation |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210125115646/https://www.sentinelassam.com/north-east-india-news/assam-news/kin-of-anti-citizenship-amendment-act-agitation-martyr-awaits-justice-515153 |url-status=live }} :*In Jamia Nagar, Delhi, three [[Delhi Transport Corporation]] buses were torched as protests took a violent turn.{{Citation|date=15 December 2019|title=Delhi: Anti-CAA protest turns violent as 3 buses burnt in Jamia Nagar|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/delhi-anti-caa-protesters-set-fire-on-bbuses-jamia-millia-islamia-students-blamed/videoshow/72683068.cms|access-date=14 March 2020|archive-date=19 April 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230419183916/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/delhi-anti-caa-protesters-set-fire-on-bbuses-jamia-millia-islamia-students-blamed/videoshow/72683068.cms|url-status=live}} :*A group of artists in [[Guwahati, Assam|Guwahati]] staged a concert in protest against the Citizenship Amendment Act 2019.{{Cite news|url=https://www.aninews.in/videos/national/assamese-artists-hold-concert-against-caa/|title=Assamese artists hold concert against CAA|date=15 December 2019|work=ANI News|access-date=22 December 2019|archive-date=22 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191222122731/https://www.aninews.in/videos/national/assamese-artists-hold-concert-against-caa/|url-status=live}} :*Police forcefully entered the campus of [[Jamia Millia Islamia|Jamia Milia Islamia university]] and detained students. According to video footage, the police used batons and tear gas on the students. More than two hundred students were injured and around a hundred were detained. The police action was widely criticised, and resulted in protests across the country and abroad. There were also allegations that the police attacked students who were not part of the protests.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/night-of-horrors-death-on-other-side-inside-jamia-millia-when-it-was-stormed-by-police-1629328-2019-12-18|title=Night of horrors, death on other side: Inside Jamia Millia when it was stormed by police|date=18 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=25 December 2019|archive-date=18 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191218120923/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/night-of-horrors-death-on-other-side-inside-jamia-millia-when-it-was-stormed-by-police-1629328-2019-12-18|url-status=live}} :*Protests were held outside the campus of the [[Aligarh Muslim University]]. In the evening, police officers forcefully entered the campus and attacked students. At least 80 students were injured in the attacks.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/jamia-protests-anti-citizenship-amendment-act-caa-injured-hospital-1629100-2019-12-17|title=80 students undergo treatment at Delhi hospital after violence at Jamia|date=17 December 2019|first1=Mausami|last1=Singh|website=India Today|access-date=19 December 2019|archive-date=18 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191218184240/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/jamia-protests-anti-citizenship-amendment-act-caa-injured-hospital-1629100-2019-12-17|url-status=live}} :*In West Bengal, violent protests occurred and five trains were set on fire by protesters in [[Lalgola railway station|Lalgola]] and [[Krishnapur railway station]]s in [[Murshidabad district]]. * '''16 December''' :*In Lucknow, police prevented around 300 students of [[Nadwa University]] from staging a peaceful protest outside the campus; clashes ensued later.{{cite news |title=After Jamia and AMU, Nadwa in Lucknow erupts in protest over citizenship law |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/after-jamia-and-amu-nadwa-in-lucknow-erupts-in-protest-over-citizenship-law/story-CVUhv5DKHst9CDwgf3slQK.html |access-date=19 December 2019 |url-status=live }} Around 15 to 20 students were injured and around 30 students were charged by the police for attempt to murder and violence.{{cite news |title=New citizenship law: Lucknow's Nadwa College students forced to retreat protest march, 2 FIRs filed |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/india/new-citizenship-law-lucknows-nadwa-college-students-forced-to-retreat-protest-march-2-firs-filed-6171034/ |access-date=28 December 2019 |work=The Indian |url-status=live }} :*[[Priyanka Gandhi]] led a silent protest at the [[India Gate]] along with about three hundred [[Indian National Congress]] workers to show solidarity with the students of Jamia Milia Islamia after the previous day's incidents. :*A [[Satyagraha]] was organised by the [[All Assam Students' Union]] across [[Assam]], which would continue till 18 December. :*In West Bengal thousands of people joined a demonstration led by Chief Minister [[Mamata Banerjee]] and her ruling [[All India Trinamool Congress|Trinamool Congress]] party. :*Prime Minister [[Narendra Modi]] appealed for calm on Twitter and clarified that CAA was for immigrants.{{Cite web|url=https://twitter.com/narendramodi/status/1206492850378002432|title=I want to unequivocally assure my fellow Indians that CAA does not affect any citizen of India of any religion. No Indian has anything to worry regarding this Act. This Act is only for those who have faced years of persecution outside and have no other place to go except India.|last1=Modi|first1=Narendra|date=16 December 2019|website=@narendramodi|access-date=16 December 2019|archive-date=17 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191217100438/https://twitter.com/narendramodi/status/1206492850378002432|url-status=live}}{{Primary source inline|date=May 2020}} * '''17 December''' :*Violent clashes occurred in Delhi's [[Seelampur subdivision|Seelampur]] area. Police retaliated with tear gas and batons against the stone throwing protesters. Several protesters and officers were injured. A police station was set on fire and buses were vandalised in the area. :*A dawn-to-dusk hartal (shutdown) was observed in Kerala by the [[Welfare Party of India]], [[Bahujan Samaj Party]], [[Social Democratic Party of India]] and 30 other organisations against the Citizenship Amendment Act and the police violence against students at various universities across the country, in relation to anti-CAA protests.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/anti-caa-stir-kerala-ksrtc-buses-stoned-1628905-2019-12-17|title=Anti-CAA stir: KSRTC buses stoned, roads blocked during hartal * '''18 December''' :*The [[Supreme Court of India]] heard 60 petitions challenging the Act and declined to stay implementation of CAA. 22 January 2020 was set as the next date of hearing on the constitutional validity of the act.{{cite news |last1=Bagriya |first1=Ashok |title=Supreme Court refuses stay on Citizenship Amendment Act, issues notice to Centre |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/supreme-court-refuses-stay-on-citizenship-amendment-act-issues-notice-to-centre/story-hXDK5Qx27bmX9AtLORBVYP.html |access-date=18 December 2019 protesters (2 in [[Mangalore]] and 1 in [[Lucknow]]) were killed in police firing.{{cite news |title=Violent CAA protests take 3 lives in 2 states, Internet shutdown, restrictions send ripples in Delhi: Highlights |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/violent-caa-protests-take-3-lives-internet-shutdown-mangaluru-deaths-highlights-1629878-2019-12-19 |access-date=19 December 2019 |url-status=live }} :*Various administrative authorities imposed bans against public gatherings, especially in [[Bharatiya Janata Party|BJP]] ruled states such as [[Karnataka]], Uttar Pradesh and parts of Delhi, where the police comes under the BJP-ruled central government. Access to [[Mobile broadband|mobile-based internet]] was shut down in certain places in Delhi and Bangalore. :*Internet was suspended in certain regions of Uttar Pradesh including the state capital, [[Lucknow]] where data and text services were restricted till noon of 21 December 2019. Internet services were also suspended in Sambhal, Aligarh, Mau, Ghaziabad, and Azamgarh districts, as well as [[Dakshina Kannada]] district in Karnataka.{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/rights/internet-shutdown-lucknow-karnataka-caa|title=Internet Shutdown: Lucknow Till December 21, Dakshina Kannada for 48 Hours|website=The Wire|access-date=21 December 2019|archive-date=21 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191221062005/https://thewire.in/rights/internet-shutdown-lucknow-karnataka-caa|url-status=live}} :*Protest meetings were held defying bans in Delhi's [[Red Fort]] and Bengaluru. Tens of thousands of people protested in Hyderabad, Patna, Chandigarh, Mumbai and other cities. :*[[Bhim Army]] Chief [[Chandrashekhar Azad Ravan|Chandrashekhar Azad]] conducted a protest march at the [[Jama Masjid, Delhi|Jama Masjid]] in Delhi. * '''21 December''' :*Chandrashekhar Azad was |access-date=4 January 2020 |work=Reuters |date=4 January 2020 |archive-date=4 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200104170440/https://www.reuters.com/article/us-india-citizenship-protests/tens-of-thousands-march-in-southern-india-to-protest-citizenship-law-idUSKBN1Z30DK |url-status=live }} :*In Bangalore hundreds of protesters participated in a rally and accused the [[Modi government]] of attempting to divide India on the basis of religion, and distracting people from the issues of economic slowdown and unemployment in the country. * '''5 January''' :*A masked mob, allegedly composed of [[Saffron terror|Hindu extremists]] from the [[Akhil Bharatiya Vidyarthi Parishad]], armed with rods and sticks attacked the campus of [[Jawaharlal Nehru University, Delhi]] and injured more than 40 students and teachers. The attack was described as an attempt to suppress [[student activism]] during the Citizenship Amendment Act protests.{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-51004204|title=Indian students rally after campus attack|date=6 January 2020|access-date=7 January 2020|archive-date=6 January 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200106061131/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-51004204|url-status=live}} * '''6 January''' :*50,000 women held an "all women's rally" in [[Malegaon]], Maharashtra.{{Cite web|url=https://www.thecitizen.in/index.php/en/NewsDetail/index/9/18141/Last-Week-in-Protest-Small-Towns-Miss-the-Headlines-But-Not-the-Crowds|title=Last Week in Protest: Small Towns Miss the Headlines But Not the Crowds|date=10 January 2020|website=The Citizen|access-date=1 February 2020|archive-date=1 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200201215704/https://www.thecitizen.in/index.php/en/NewsDetail/index/9/18141/Last-Week-in-Protest-Small-Towns-Miss-the-Headlines-But-Not-the-Crowds|url-status=dead}} * '''7 January''' :*Women in Kolkata started a sit-in protest at the grounds of Park Circus in Kolkata.{{cite news |last1=Doulatramani |first1=Chandni |title=Shaheen Bagh inspires a women-led CAA protest in Kolkata's Park Circus |url=https://caravanmagazine.in/politics/kolkata-caa-protest-park-circus-women |access-date=16 January 2020 |work=The Caravan |date=16 January 2020 |archive-date=16 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200116145708/https://caravanmagazine.in/politics/kolkata-caa-protest-park-circus-women |url-status=live }} * '''8 January''' :*PM Modi cancelled his visit to Assam, while the CAA protests continued. AASU had planned huge protests during Modi's visit.{{cite news |title=Amidst anti-CAA protests, Narendra Modi's Assam visit cancelled |url=https://www.newindianexpress.com/nation/2020/jan/08/amidst-anti-caa-protests-narendra-modis-assam-visit-cancelled-2086899.html |access-date=8 January 2020 |work=The New Indian Express |date=8 }}{{cite news |title=Amid Anti-CAA Protests, PM Modi To Skip Khelo India Youth Games in Assam |url-status=live }} :*Thousands of people joined the anti-CAA protests at Dibrugarh, Guwahati and other parts of Assam.{{cite news |title=Protests unabated across Assam |url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/north-east/protests-unabated-across-assam/cid/1734119 |access-date=8 January 2020 |work=The Telegraph |location=Kolkota |date=8 January 2020 |archive-date=8 March :*Students of JNU attempted to march towards [[Rashtrapati Bhavan]], the official residence of the [[President of India]] while blocking [[Janpath]]. Police detained around 100 students when the march was blocking Janpath.{{cite news |title=JNU students stopped by police from marching towards Rashtrapati Bhavan, detained |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/jnu-students-stopped-by-police-from-marching-towards-rashtrapati-bhavan-detained/article30524569.ece |access-date=9 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=9 January 2020 |archive-date=9 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200109162114/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/jnu-students-stopped-by-police-from-marching-towards-rashtrapati-bhavan-detained/article30524569.ece |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} :*The Chief Justice of India dismissed a petition by a lawyer demanding that the CAA be declared constitutionally valid, stating that "There is anyway a presumption of constitutionality".{{cite news |title=Chief Justice Sharad Bobde scoffs at plea to declare CAA 'constitutional' |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/chief-justice-sharad-bobde-scoffs-at-plea-to-declare-caa-constitutional/article30521922.ece |access-date=9 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=9 January 2020 |archive-date=9 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200109102043/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/chief-justice-sharad-bobde-scoffs-at-plea-to-declare-caa-constitutional/article30521922.ece |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} :* Asom Jatiyatabadi Yuba Chatra Parishad (AJYCP) along with All Assam Students’ Union (AASU), the Krishak Mukti Sangram Samiti (KMSS) and 30 other organisations and artist communities organised protests against CAA in [[Guwahati]]. They demanded "corruption-free, foreigner-free, terrorism-free and pollution-free Assam" from the Chief Minister of Assam, [[Sarbananda Sonowal]] rather than implementing CAA-NRC. * '''10 January''' :*The Citizenship Amendment Act came into force.{{cite news |last1=Singh |first1=Vijaita |title=Citizenship Amendment Act comes into effect from Jan. 10 |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/citizenship-amendment-act-comes-into-effect-from-jan-10/article30537592.ece |access-date=10 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=10 January 2020 |archive-date=10 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200110200039/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/citizenship-amendment-act-comes-into-effect-from-jan-10/article30537592.ece |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} :*The [[Delhi High Court]] dismissed a plea to remove the [[Shaheen Bagh protests|protesters occupying Shaheen Bagh]].{{cite news |title=Delhi HC dismisses plea seeking removal of anti-CAA protesters at Shaheen Bagh |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/cities/Delhi/delhi-hc-dismisses-plea-seeking-removal-of-anti-caa-protesters-at-shaheen-bagh/article30531944.ece |access-date=11 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=10 January 2020 |archive-date=24 May 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240524134753/https://www.thehindu.com/news/cities/Delhi/delhi-hc-dismisses-plea-seeking-removal-of-anti-caa-protesters-at-shaheen-bagh/article30531944.ece |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} * '''11 January''' :*PM Modi visited Kolkata on a two day official visit. Hundreds of people protested against CAA at the Kolkata airport.{{cite news |title=Modi welcomed to Kolkata with anti-CAA protests |url=https://www.rediff.com/news/report/tmc-cpi-m-begin-protest-pms-kolkata-visit/20200111.htm |access-date=11 January 2020 |work=Rediff |date=11 January 2020 |archive-date=11 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200111114047/https://www.rediff.com/news/report/tmc-cpi-m-begin-protest-pms-kolkata-visit/20200111.htm |url-status=live }} :*Several parties and student unions launched a protest at the Dorina Crossing at Esplanade in Kolkata. SFI leader stated that the protests would continue till Sunday, until PM Modi (who had been visiting the city) was in Kolkata.{{cite news |title=Left-leaning Students Protest Against Mamata for Meeting PM, Say it Will 'Dilute Fight' Against CAA |url=https://www.news18.com/news/politics/narendra-modi-west-bengal-live-updates-kolkata-mamata-banerjee-tmc-left-front-anti-caa-protest-rallies-2454155.html |access-date=11 January 2020 |work=News18 |date=11 January 2020 |archive-date=11 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200111221058/https://www.news18.com/news/politics/narendra-modi-west-bengal-live-updates-kolkata-mamata-banerjee-tmc-left-front-anti-caa-protest-rallies-2454155.html |url-status=live }} :*A "[[Flag of India|Tiranga]] Rally" against the CAA was held in Hyderabad, with thousands{{Cite web|url=https://telanganatoday.com/watch-thousands-participate-in-tiranga-rally-against-caa-in-hyderabad|title=Thousands participate in Tiranga Rally against CAA in Hyderabad|website=Telangana national flag.{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/telangana/hyderabad-turns-into-a-sea-of-tricolour/article30537135.ece|title=Hyderabad turns into a sea of Tricolour|last1=Mohammed|first1=Syed|date=10 January 2020|work=The Hindu|access-date=12 January 2020|issn=0971-751X|archive-date=11 January required}} Police officers were seen [[caning]] peaceful protesters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.deccanchronicle.com/nation/current-affairs/120120/hyderabad-police-caned-protesters-at-8-places-during-tiranga-rally.html|title=Hyderabad police caned protesters at 8 places during :*[[Kirit Pradyot Deb Barman|Pradyot Manikya Debbarma]], the royal scion of the [[Manikya dynasty]], led the largest protest in Tripura as of 11 January 2020 consisting of thousands of people.{{cite news |title="No Space To Accommodate Any More": Tripura Royal Scion On Citizenship |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/no-space-to-accommodate-any-more-tripuras-pradyot-manikya-debbarma-on-citizenship-act-2162788 |access-date=12 January 2020 |work=NDTV.com |date=12 January 2020 |archive-date=12 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200112113049/https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/no-space-to-accommodate-any-more-tripuras-pradyot-manikya-debbarma-on-citizenship-act-2162788 |url-status=live }} Debbarma stated that beneficiaries of CAA will not be allowed to settle in Tripura. He added that the state has already accommodated many migrants from [[East Pakistan]] and further immigration due to CAA will endanger the threatened indigenous residents of Tripura. * '''12 January''' :*In Kolkata, the protesters outside the venue of PM Modi's speech were detained by the police.{{cite news |last1=Bagchi |first1=Suvojit |title=Citizenship Amendment Act: Narendra Modi's remarks upset Ramakrishna Mission members |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/pms-caa-remarks-upset-mission-members/article30551410.ece |access-date=12 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=12 January 2020 |archive-date=12 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200112162356/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/pms-caa-remarks-upset-mission-members/article30551410.ece |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} :*Thousands of people join the anti CAA protests at Jogeshwari in Mumbai. Slogans such as "I Am From Gujarat, My Documents Burned in 2002", "No CAA, Boycott NRC, Stop Dividing India, Don't Divide us", "Save Constitution", were displayed on the banners. :*The Indian National Congress demanded the withdrawal of CAA, and stopping of the process to update NPR. It claimed that the NPR was a 'disguised NRC'. * '''13 January''' :*The [[List of Indian parliamentary committees#Department-related standing committees|Parliamentary panel on Home Affairs]] criticised the Delhi Police for the violent crackdown on Jamia and JNU and asked them not to be harsh.{{cite news |title=CAA protests LIVE Updates: Parliamentary panel comes down hard on Delhi Police over Jamia, JNU crackdown |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-protest-news-live-updates-congress-meet-rahul-gandhi-amit-shah-delhi-opposition-1636317-2020-01-13 |access-date=13 January 2020 |work=India Today |date=13 January 2020 |archive-date=13 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200113063013/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-protest-news-live-updates-congress-meet-rahul-gandhi-amit-shah-delhi-opposition-1636317-2020-01-13 |url-status=live }} :*A meeting of 20 opposition parties in Delhi released a statement demanding a revocation of the CAA and asked all the Chief Ministers who have refused to implement the NRC in their states to stop the work of updating the [[National Population Register]], as it is the foundation of NRC.{{cite news |title=Stop NPR: Opposition's Message To Chief Ministers Against Citizens' List |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/national-population-register-oppositions-message-to-chief-ministers-against-citizens-list-stop-of-2163428 |access-date=13 January 2020 |work=NDTV.com |date=13 |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/citizen-amendment-act-january-12/liveblog/73210547.cms |access-date=13 January 2020 |work=The Times of India |date=13 January 2020 |archive-date=12 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200112121533/https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/citizen-amendment-act-january-12/liveblog/73210547.cms |url-status=live }} :*At Jamia Millia Islamia, several student groups protested outside the office of Vice-Chancellor Najma Akhtar asking to reschedule the exam dates, filing a case against Delhi Police and ensuring the safety of students. The VC announced in the afternoon, that a case will be filed against police on 14 January. * '''14 January''' :*The [[Kerala government]] approached the Supreme Court to challenge the CAA under Section 131 of the Constitution and became the first state to do so.{{cite news |last1=Yamunan |first1=Sruthisagar |title=As Kerala challenges CAA in the Supreme Court, here are possible ways the case could play out |url=https://scroll.in/article/949822/as-kerala-challenges-caa-in-the-supreme-court-here-are-possible-ways-the-case-could-play-out |access-date=14 January 2020 |work=Scroll.in |date=14 }}{{cite news |title=Kerala will remain in forefront to defend Constitution: CM {{!}} India News – Times of India |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/kerala-will-remain-in-forefront-to-defend-constitution-cm/articleshow/73253428.cms |access-date=14 January 2020 |work=The Times of India |agency=Press :*[[Mani Shankar Aiyar]] of the Indian National Congress joined the anti CAA protesters at Shaheen Bagh.{{cite news |title=Live: Cong's Mani Shankar Aiyar joins protest against CAA & NRC, in Shaheen Bagh |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/caa-news-live-updates-jan-14-jan-15/liveblog/73238479.cms |access-date=14 January 2020 |work=The Times of India |date=14 January 2020 |url-status=live }} :*5000 women staged a sit-in protest at Mansoor Ali park in [[Prayagraj]], Uttar Pradesh.{{cite news |title=Citizenship Act Protests: Protests In Delhi, Prayagraj, Mumbai |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/citizenship-act-caa-protests-live-updates-women-protest-in-delhis-shaheen-bagh-prayagrajs-old-town-2163921 |access-date=14 January 2020 |work=NDTV.com |date=14 |url-status=live }} :*In Mumbai, a group of students attended the India v/s Australia match at Wankhede stadium and peacefully protested by wearing T-shirts with the message "No NPR, NO NRC and NO CAA". :*In Gujarat, people celebrated [[Makar Sankranti]] by flying kites with slogans for and against CAA.{{Cite web|url=https://www.firstpost.com/india/battle-over-caa-reaches-skies-kites-with-messages-for-and-against-amended-citizenship-law-flown-in-gujarat-on-makar-sankranti-7906331.html|title=Battle over CAA * '''15 January''' :*More than 200,000 people joined the Anti-CAA protests in Mangalore, where hundreds of people came in boats carrying Indian flags. Activists Harsh Mander and former [[IAS officer]] Kannan Gopinathan gave speeches during the protests. The protests ended peacefully with the singing of National anthem.{{cite news |title=Watch: Hundreds Travel By Sea To Protest Citizenship Law In Mangaluru |url=https://www.ndtv.com/karnataka-news/caa-protests-in-mangaluru-karnataka-hundreds-travel-by-sea-to-protest-caa-citizenship-amendment-act-2164622 |access-date=16 January 2020 |work=NDTV.com |date=15 January 2020 |archive-date=16 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200116101025/https://www.ndtv.com/karnataka-news/caa-protests-in-mangaluru-karnataka-hundreds-travel-by-sea-to-protest-caa-citizenship-amendment-act-2164622 |url-status=live }} :*Women conducted sit-in protests at Patna's [[Sabzibagh]] and Kolkata's [[Park Circus]], Allahabad's Roshan Bagh, Kanpur's [[Chaman Ganj, Kanpur|Chaman Ganj]], Gaya's Shanti Bagh along with Delhi's Shaheen Bagh.{{cite news |last1=Ameen |first1=Faruqh |title=Shaheen Bagh inspires many protests across the country |url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/india/shaheen-bagh-inspires-many-protests-across-the-country/cid/1736089 |access-date=19 January 2020 |work=The Telegraph Calcutta |date=15 January 2020 |archive-date=16 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200116050448/https://www.telegraphindia.com/india/shaheen-bagh-inspires-many-protests-across-the-country/cid/1736089 |url-status=live }} :*A petition was filed in Gujarat High court asking its intervention to allow citizens to hold Anti-CAA protests in Ahmedabad. The petitioners stated that the Gujarat Police did not grant them permission for peaceful protests but granted permission to 62 programmes held by the ruling BJP to support the CAA. The court asked the police to decide properly on the protesters' applications.{{cite news |title=CAA-NRC Live Updates: Gujarat HC Asks Police to Take 'Timely Call' on Protests |url=https://thewire.in/rights/caa-nrc-live-updates-gujarat-hc-asks-police-to-take-timely-call-on-protests |access-date=15 January 2020 |work=The Wire |date=15 January 2020 |archive-date=15 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200115210937/https://thewire.in/rights/caa-nrc-live-updates-gujarat-hc-asks-police-to-take-timely-call-on-protests |url-status=live }} :*A Delhi court granted bail and released Bhim Army chief Chandrashekhar Azad aka "Raavan", who had been arrested for his protest in December against CAA at [[Jama Masjid, Delhi]].{{cite news |title=Bail For Bhim Army Chief Chandrashekhar Azad After CAA Protest; Can't Be In Delhi For 4 Weeks |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/bhim-armys-chandrashekhar-azad-granted-bail-by-delhi-court-he-was-arrested-for-protest-against-citiz-2164526 |access-date=15 January 2020 |work=NDTV.com |date=15 January 2020 |archive-date=15 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200115134405/https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/bhim-armys-chandrashekhar-azad-granted-bail-by-delhi-court-he-was-arrested-for-protest-against-citiz-2164526 |url-status=live }} :*Aligarh Muslim University (AMU) postpones all exams due to the ongoing CAA protests.{{cite news |title=AMU Postpones All Exams in Wake of Ongoing Anti-CAA Protests on Campus |url=https://www.news18.com/news/india/amu-postpones-all-exams-in-wake-of-ongoing-anti-caa-protests-on-campus-2459477.html |access-date=15 January 2020 |work=News18 |date=15 January 2020 |archive-date=15 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200115170652/https://www.news18.com/news/india/amu-postpones-all-exams-in-wake-of-ongoing-anti-caa-protests-on-campus-2459477.html |url-status=live }} * '''16 January''' :*Police in [[Chennai]] banned all protests in the city for a period of 15 days. Five women and one man (including a physically disabled person) were detained by the police for protesting against CAA.{{cite news |title=Amid CAA protests, Chennai Police Commissioner bars protests in city for 15 days |url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/amid-caa-protests-chennai-police-commissioner-bars-protests-city-15-days-116187 |access-date=16 January 2020 |work=thenewsminute.com |date=16 January 2020 |archive-date=16 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200116122822/https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/amid-caa-protests-chennai-police-commissioner-bars-protests-city-15-days-116187 |url-status=live }} * '''17 January''' :* Inspired by the Shaheen Bagh protest, a massive anti-CAA-NRC-NPR protest was started in Mumbai. Around 10,000 women gathered at the YMCA ground in Mumbai to protest in the evening. The protest was organised by a Non-governmental organisation named Mumbai Citizen Quorum.{{cite news |last1=Iyer |first1=Rudrika |title=Mumbai: 10,000 women lend their voice to anti-CAA protest |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/mumbai/mumbai-10000-women-lend-their-voice-to-anti-caa-protest/articleshow/73350759.cms |access-date=19 January 2020 |work=The Times of India |date=18 :*Bihar Police detained CPI leader [[Kanhaiya Kumar]] before he could start his planned month-long "Jan-Gana-Man Yatra" which was organised at [[Champaran]] on [[Mahatma Gandhi]]'s death anniversary.{{cite news |title=Kanhaiya Kumar Taken Into Custody in Champaran as Police Halts Protest March Against CAA, NRC |url=https://www.news18.com/news/politics/kanhaiya-kumar-taken-into-custody-from-champaran-as-police-cancels-nod-for-protest-march-against-caa-nrc-2479291.html |access-date=31 January 2020 |work=The News18 |date=30 January 2020 |archive-date=31 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200131214720/https://www.news18.com/news/politics/kanhaiya-kumar-taken-into-custody-from-champaran-as-police-cancels-nod-for-protest-march-against-caa-nrc-2479291.html |url-status=live }} :*Seven protesters who were protesting at the Ghantaghar (Clock Tower) in Lucknow. They were arrested for taking out a candle march, which UP Police said was a violation of section 144 of the CrPC which was imposed in the area.{{cite news |title=CAA-NRC protest: 7 arrested for taking out candle march without permission in Lucknow |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-nrc-protest-7-arrested-for-taking-out-candle-march-without-permission-in-lucknow-1642151-2020-02-01 |access-date=31 January 2020 |work=The India Today |date=1 February 2020 |archive-date=31 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200131214723/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-nrc-protest-7-arrested-for-taking-out-candle-march-without-permission-in-lucknow-1642151-2020-02-01 |url-status=live }} * '''31 January''' :*Leaders from 14 opposition parties, led by the Indian National Congress, wore black armbands and vacated the front row seats to protest against the new law, the NRC and the NPR during President [[Ram Nath Kovind]]'s address to the [[Joint Session of the Parliament of India]] for [[2020 Union budget of India|2020 budget]] session. The Leader of the Opposition in the [[Rajya Sabha]], [[Ghulam Nabi Azad]] said that the Union Government misused the President's office and it was "shameful" that the government had included the CAA in the President's speech as an achievement.{{cite news |title=Budget Session 2020: Congress says President's speech used to legitimise government's errors |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/budget-session-2020-congress-says-presidents-speech-used-to-legitimise-governments-errors/article30703736.ece |access-date=31 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=31 January 2020 |archive-date=31 January 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200131221154/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/budget-session-2020-congress-says-presidents-speech-used-to-legitimise-governments-errors/article30703736.ece |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} === February 2020 === * '''1 February''' :* After the JMI firing incident on 30 January, another Hindu fundamentalist fired bullets in the air during the [[Shaheen Bagh protests]].{{Cite web |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/shaheen-bagh-shooting-anti-caa-protest-violence-delhi-police-protesters-1642353-2020-02-01 |title=Shaheen Bagh firing: Shooter shouts sirf Hinduon ki chalegi, detained |date=1 February 2020 |first=Arvind |last=Ojha |website=India Today |access-date=1 February 2020 |archive-date=1 February 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200201141904/https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/shaheen-bagh-shooting-anti-caa-protest-violence-delhi-police-protesters-1642353-2020-02-01 |url-status=live }} The man chanted the slogans "''Hindu Rashtra Zindabad''" (Long live the [[Hindu nationalism|Hindu Rashtra]]) and "''Sirf Hinduo ki chalegi''" (Only Hindus will rule in this country).{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/cities/Delhi/man-opens-fire-in-shaheen-bagh-area-no-casualty-reported/article30712788.ece|title=Man opens fire in Shaheen Bagh area, no casualty reported|last1=Trivedi|first1=Saurabh|date=1 February 2020|work=The Hindu|access-date=1 February 2020|issn=0971-751X|archive-date=1 February required}} :* [[Amnesty International]] informed US lawmakers that the CAA clearly violates the Constitution of India and [[International human rights law]] and "legitimises discrimination" on the basis of religion.{{cite news |title=CAA in 'clear violation' of Indian Constitution and international human rights law |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/caa-in-clear-violation-of-indian-constitution-and-international-human-rights-law-amnesty/articleshow/73838887.cms |access-date=2 February 2020 |work=The |url-status=live }} :*Thousands of LGBT rights activists protest raised slogans against the CAA at the [[Queer Azaadi Mumbai|Queer Azaadi Pride Parade]] in Mumbai.{{Cite web|url=https://www.firstpost.com/india/mumbai-pride-2020-thousands-stand-in-solidarity-with-queer-muslims-amid-caa-nrc-chaos-urge-govt-to-amend-draconian-transgender-act-7991111.html|title=Mumbai '''6 February''' :* In [[Bidar]], a mother and a school principal were arrested after the mother's daughter participated in a play against the CAA.{{Cite web|url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2020/02/india-mother-school-principal-arrested-anti-caa-play-200206055353254.html|title=India: Mother, school principal arrested over anti-CAA play|publisher=Al February''' :* The Bidar police interrogated 85 school-children studying in classes 4–6 for participating in an anti-CAA play.{{Cite web |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/bengaluru/karnataka-cops-quiz-85-school-kids-for-anti-caa-play/articleshow/73997470.cms |title=Praveen Sood: Karnataka cops quiz 85 school kids for |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200208201717/https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/bengaluru/karnataka-cops-quiz-85-school-kids-for-anti-caa-play/articleshow/73997470.cms |url-status=live }} :* Ahead of the [[2020 Delhi Legislative Assembly election]], two bike-borne men allegedly opened fired in the air near an anti-Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) protest site in Northeast Delhi's Jafrabad. The police, however, suspected personal enmity.{{Cite web |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/delhi-assembly-polls-2020/story/two-men-bike-fire-near-anti-caa-protest-site-jafrabad-police-1644316-2020-02-07 |title=Ahead of Delhi election, 2 men on bike fire in air near anti-CAA protest site |date=7 February 2020 |agency=Press Trust of India |website=India Today |access-date=9 February 2020 |archive-date=8 February 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200208165530/https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/delhi-assembly-polls-2020/story/two-men-bike-fire-near-anti-caa-protest-site-jafrabad-police-1644316-2020-02-07 |url-status=live }} * '''8 February''' :* A Muslim BJP councillor in [[Indore]] resigned from the party, accusing it of spreading "politics of hatred".{{Cite web|url=https://www.news18.com/news/politics/bjps-muslim-face-in-indore-resigns-over-caa-accuses-party-of-indulging-in-politics-of-hatred-2492941.html|title=BJP's Muslim Face in Indore Resigns Over CAA, Accuses Party of Indulging in Politics of Hatred|website=News18|date=8 February 2020|access-date=9 February 2020|archive-date=9 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200209141344/https://www.news18.com/news/politics/bjps-muslim-face-in-indore-resigns-over-caa-accuses-party-of-indulging-in-politics-of-hatred-2492941.html|url-status=live}} * '''11 February''' :*[[Jamia Millia Islamia|Jamia Milia Islamia]] students staged a march to the [[Parliament of India|Parliament]] against the CAA and NRC. The march was stopped by the Delhi police who detained protesters.{{Cite web|url=https://scroll.in/latest/952754/delhi-more-than-10-jamia-students-injured-as-police-stop-caa-protest-march-to-parliament|title=Delhi: More than 10 Jamia students injured as police stop CAA protest march to Parliament|website=Scroll.in|date=10 February 2020|access-date=12 February 2020|archive-date=11 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200211211629/https://scroll.in/latest/952754/delhi-more-than-10-jamia-students-injured-as-police-stop-caa-protest-march-to-parliament|url-status=live}} The police allegedly sexually assaulted the protesters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.news18.com/news/india/hit-on-private-parts-some-choked-jamia-students-delhi-cops-clash-as-anti-caa-march-to-parl-turns-violent-2496175.html|title='Hit on Private Parts, Some * '''16 February''' :*[[Mohammad Imran Pratapgarhi|Imran Pratapgarhi]], an Indian National Congress politician was fined {{INRConvert|1.04|c|-3|lk=c|to=USD|year=2020}} for violating Section 144 by addressing protestors, participating in an anti-CAA protest in [[Moradabad]], Uttar Pradesh, and allegedly instigating protesters at the Idgah ground.{{Cite news|last1=Kumar|first1=Anuj|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/caa-protests-1-crore-fine-slapped-on-up-poet-politician/article30834459.ece|title=Anti-CAA protests: ₹1 crore fine slapped on 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200217211118/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/caa-protests-1-crore-fine-slapped-on-up-poet-politician/article30834459.ece|url-status=live}} * '''17 February''' :*Over 150 prominent citizens including former judges, bureaucrats, army officers and academicians wrote to President Ram Nath Kovind, claiming that the anti-CAA protests were based on a false narrative, and urged the centre to look into the ongoing protests with all seriousness, safeguard the nation's democratic institutions and take stern action against the people behind them.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/caa-former-judges-army-officers-write-to-president-against-protesters-2181594|title=Former Judges, Army Officers Write To President Against Anti-CAA news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/154-prominent-citizens-back-caa-npr-nrc-say-protests-are-motivated/article30846782.ece|title=154 prominent citizens back CAA, NPR, NRC; say protests are motivated|date=18 February 2020|work=The Hindu|access-date=18 February 2020|agency=Press Trust of India|issn=0971-751X|archive-date=17 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200217234142/https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/154-prominent-citizens-back-caa-npr-nrc-say-protests-are-motivated/article30846782.ece|url-status=live}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/154-eminent-citizens-write-to-president-in-favour-of-caa-nrc-and-npr/story-RKjQMwrwHL9zntVwLvx7ZK.html|title=154 eminent citizens write to President in favour of CAA, NRC and NPR|date=17 February 2020|website=Hindustan Times|access-date=18 February 2020|archive-date=17 February :* [[Ramon Magsaysay Award|Magsaysay Award]] recipient [[Sandeep Pandey]] was arrested by the Lucknow police for attempting to protest. He was released on bail after being produced in the District Magistrate's court.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/magsaysay-award-winner-arrested-on-way-to-anti-caa-march-in-uttar-pradesh-2181478|title=Magsaysay Award Winner Arrested On Way To Anti-Citizenship Act March In Uttar Pradesh|website=NDTV.com|access-date=18 February 2020|archive-date=17 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200217164428/https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/magsaysay-award-winner-arrested-on-way-to-anti-caa-march-in-uttar-pradesh-2181478|url-status=live}} * '''18 February''' :* The Uttar Pradesh government informed the [[Allahabad High Court]] that 22 had been killed in the protests and a total of 883 people had been arrested in connection with violence during the protests.{{Cite web|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/22-killed-during-anti-caa-stir-322-still-in-jail-up-government-tells-allahabad-high-court/story-FwFSEDAUWTjQoGqqRE1TNM.html|title=22 killed during anti-CAA stir, 322 still in jail, UP government tells Allahabad high court|date=18 February 2020|website=Hindustan Times|access-date=18 February 2020|archive-date=17 February * '''19 February''' :* [[Secretary-General of the United Nations]] [[António Guterres]] expressed concerns over the possibility of millions being rendered [[stateless nation|stateless]] due to the CAA and NRC.{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/external-affairs/un-chief-antonio-guterres-caa-nrc|title='Necessary to Prevent Statelessness': UN Chief Antonio Guterres on CAA, NRC|website=The Wire|access-date=24 February 2020|archive-date=24 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200224142746/https://thewire.in/external-affairs/un-chief-antonio-guterres-caa-nrc|url-status=dead}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.nationalheraldindia.com/india/un-chief-antonio-guterres-expresses-concern-over-caa-says-risk-of-statelessness-for-two-million-muslims|title=UN Chief Antonio Guterres expresses concern over CAA, says risk of statelessness for two million Muslims|website=National Herald|date=19 February 2020|access-date=24 February 2020|archive-date=24 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200224152559/https://www.nationalheraldindia.com/india/un-chief-antonio-guterres-expresses-concern-over-caa-says-risk-of-statelessness-for-two-million-muslims|url-status=live}} :* In [[Chennai]], a confederation of Muslim groups led a massive rally towards the [[Secretariat (administrative office)|Secretariat]] of Tamil Nadu in a demonstration against the CAA.
