news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-51004204|title=Indian students rally after campus attack|date=6 January 2020|access-date=6 January 2020}} ===Delhi=== [[File:Locals in New Delhi protest against CAA CAB NRC.jpg|thumb|Locals in New Delhi protest against CAA]] [[File:Graffiti on police barricades no nrc caa road blocked for protesters who have been sitting for over three weeks in background New Delhi 7 Jan 2020.jpg|thumb|A major road in New Delhi has been blocked by anti CAA protests for over three weeks in the [[Shaheen Bagh protests]]. (pic taken on 7 January 2020).{{cite news|url=https://scroll.in/article/948440/the-art-of-resistance-ringing-in-the-new-year-with-anti-caa-protesters-at-shaheen-bagh|title=The Art of Resistance: Ringing in the new year with CAA protesters at Delhi's Shaheen Bagh|last1=Venkataramakrishnan|first1=Rohan|date=1 January 2020|work=[[Scroll.in]]|access-date=7 January 2020}}]] On 14 December 2019, thousands of agitators packed into [[Jantar Mantar, New Delhi|Jantar Mantar]] Road, filling up a space estimated to be half the size of a football ground, as multiple demonstrations occurred against the CAA in Delhi.{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/india/different-protest-voices-at-jantar-mantar/cid/1727006|title=Different protest voices at Jantar Mantar |website=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota |access-date=16 December 2019}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/massive-protests-at-delhis-jantar-mantar-over-citizenship-act/article30306676.ece|title=Massive protests at Delhi's Jantar Mantar over Citizenship Act|date=14 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=16 December 2019|agency=Press Trust of India|issn=0971-751X}}{{subscription required}} The next day in Delhi near New Friends Colony, three [[Delhi Transport Corporation]] buses were torched. On 16 December, [[Priyanka Gandhi]] led a silent protest at the [[India Gate]] along with about three hundred congress workers to show solidarity with the students of Jamia Millia Islamia.{{cite news|url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/priyanka-gandhi-vadra-leads-congress-protest-at-india-gate-against-police-crackdown-on-delhi-student-2149694|title=Priyanka Gandhi Leads Protest At India Gate Against Crackdown On Students|publisher=NDTV|access-date=16 December 2019}} On 17 December 2019, Delhi's Seelampur area had stone-throwing crowds face off against the police. The police retaliated with tear gas and batons, in which, according to local reports, several protesters and officers were injured. There were also reports of a police station being set on fire. According to police, buses were vandalised in the area.{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-50818192|title=Fresh violence in Delhi over citizenship law|date=17 December 2019|access-date=17 December 2019}} On 19 December 2019, the administrative authorities imposed a ban against public gatherings in parts of Delhi. 20 metro stations were closed to prevent the movement for protests. At least 700 flights were delayed and more than 20 cancelled due to traffic jams caused by police closing the roads to stifle protests. Protest meetings were held defying the ban in Red Fort and [[Mandi House]]. Access to the mobile internet was restricted in certain places in Delhi. Digital rights activists, who accessed and studied the order suspending internet in several areas in Delhi on 19 December, stated that it was not issued from the "right channels" and, therefore, was "illegal".{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/delhi/delhi-internet-shutdown-did-not-come-via-right-channel-digital-right-activists/articleshow/72896669.cms|title=Delhi internet shutdown did not come via right channel: Digital right activists |date=20 December 2019|first1=Anam|last1=Ajmal|website=The Times of India|access-date=20 December 2019}} Politicians [[Yogendra Yadav]], [[Sitaram Yechury]], [[Nilotpal Basu]], [[Brinda Karat]], [[Ajay Maken]], [[Brinda Karat]], [[Prakash Karat]], [[Sandeep Dikshit]], [[Umar Khalid]] and [[D. Raja]] along with around 1,200 protesters were detained by the police.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/anti-citizenship-amendment-act-stir-continues-in-kerala-1629786-2019-12-19|title=Anti-Citizenship Amendment Act stir continues in Kerala|date=19 December 2019 |website=India Today|access-date=22 December 2019}} On 20 December 2019, two Delhi Metro stations – [[Jamia Millia Islamia metro station|Jamia Millia Islamia]] and [[Jasola Vihar Shaheen Bagh metro station|Jasola Vihar Shaheen Bagh]] were closed. Amid nationwide crackdown because of CAA, [[Bhim Army]] Chief [[Chandrashekhar Azad (social activist)|Chandrashekhar Azad]]'s permission for the march from Jama Masjid to [[Jantar Mantar]] had been denied by [[Delhi Police]]. In spite of the denial of permission and the imposition of [[Section 144]], a protest march was held where Azad was able to escape after being detained by the police.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/delhi-news/bhim-armys-chandrashekhar-azad-leads-massive-protest-at-delhis-jama-masjid-2151809|title=Bhim Army's Chandrashekhar Gives Cops The Slip At Delhi's Jama Masjid|publisher=NDTV|access-date=20 December 2019}}{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/bhim-army-chief-chandrashekhar-azad-escapes-from-police-after-being-caught/articleshow/72902014.cms|title=Bhim Army chief Chandrashekhar Azad escapes from police after being caught|date=20 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=20 December 2019}} The protests were peaceful throughout the day, but in the evening a car was torched in Daryaganj after which the police attacked the protesters with water cannons and lathi charge. On the aftermath, Chandrashekhar Azad accused the [[Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh]] of being responsible for the violence and sought for the resignation of the [[Union Home Minister]], Amit Shah.{{Cite web|url=https://caravanmagazine.in/amp/politics/chandrashekhar-azad-bhim-amit-shah-resign-rss-violence|title=Chandrashekhar Azad seeks Amit Shah's resignation, holds RSS responsible for violence at CAA protests|website=caravanmagazine.in|access-date=25 December 2019}} On 21 December, Chandrashekhar Azad was arrested along with 27 people and three FIRs were registered for the violent incidents on 20 December at Delhi Gate and Seemapuri. On 10 January 12 of those arrested were released on bail.{{cite news |title=Anti-CAA protests: Delhi court grants bail to 12 people in Seemapuri violence case |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/anti-caa-protests-delhi-court-grants-bail-to-12-people-in-seemapuri-violence-case/article30534583.ece |access-date=10 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=10 January 2020 }}{{subscription required}} On 15 January, a Delhi court granted bail and released Chandrashekhar Azad aka "Raavan". However, when presented in the Delhi Tis Hazari court on 14 January 2020, the court questioned the public prosecutor about what is wrong with protesting since many people who protested in past are sitting chairpersons in the present government.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/is-jama-masjid-in-pakistan-delhi-court-asks-police-on-chandrashekhar-azad-arrest-1636705-2020-01-14|title=Is Jama Masjid in Pakistan, what's wrong in protesting: Delhi court on Chandrashekhar Azad's arrest|first1=Aneesha|last1=Mathur|first2=Poonam|last2=Sharma|date=14 January 2020 |website=India Today|access-date=16 January 2020}} Protests were conducted by the journalists against the police brutality on the journalists covering the Anti-CAA protests especially in the states of Delhi, Uttar Pradesh and Karnataka. In the protests, [[Sitaram Yechury]] stated that only the states ruled by the [[Bharatiya Janata Party]] were experiencing violence. He further added that the other states were peaceful and hinted that the differences between the situation in the states show the who were encouraging violence.{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/media/caa-protests-kerala-journalists|title=CAA Protests: Journalists Take Out Rally Against Mistreatment by Law Enforcement|website=The Wire|access-date=22 December 2019}} On 23 December 2019, protests were held at multiple locations. 93 other students protesting outside Assam Bhawan and demanding the release of RTI activist Akhil Gogoi were detained by the police. The students alleged that the police used violence on the protesters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/india/students-activists-hit-the-streets/cid/1729671|title=Students, activists hit the streets|website=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota|access-date=24 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/caa-protests-students-in-up-bhawan-demanding-yogi-adityanath-resignation-over-up-crackdown-detained-2153043|title=Students Demanding Yogi Adityanath's Resignation Over UP Crackdown Detained|publisher=NDTV|access-date=24 December 2019}} On 24 December 2019, Police imposed a ban on gatherings in central Delhi's [[Mandi House]] near the Lutyens' Zone to prevent the protest march of students from multiple universities. The anti CAA-NRC protest march was to start from Mandi House to [[Jantar Mantar]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/large-gatherings-banned-near-mandi-house-in-central-delhi-ahead-of-citizenship-act-protests-2153559|title=Large Gatherings Banned Near Delhi's Mandi House Amid Protest|publisher=NDTV|access-date=24 December 2019}}{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/anti-caa-protest-students-from-various-universities-across-delhi-join-march/articleshow/72953893.cms|title=Anti-CAA protest: Students from various universities across Delhi join march|date=24 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=24 December 2019}} On 27 December, the Delhi Police used [[facial recognition software]] by recording a video of the protester and checking it with the database of criminals maintained by them.{{cite news |title=Delhi Police film protests, run its images through face recognition software to screen crowd |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/india/police-film-protests-run-its-images-through-face-recognition-software-to-screen-crowd-6188246/ |access-date=28 December 2019 |work=The Indian Express |date=28 December 2019}} On 14 January, [[Supreme Court of India|Supreme Court]] lawyers conducted a protest march from the Supreme Court to Jantar Mantar to protest against the CAA, NRC and NPR.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatvnews.com/news/india/supreme-court-lawyers-protest-march-caa-nrc-npr-delhi-jantar-mantar-579487|title=SC to Jantar Mantar: Supreme Court lawyers hold protest march against CAA, NRC, NPR|last1=Taneja|first1=Nidhi|date=14 January 2020|website=IndiaTVNews|access-date=14 January 2020}} On 19 January, in Delhi, hundreds of protesters joined a protest march holding lighted candles from Jamia University to Shaheen Bagh. Some of the protesters had dressed up as [[Mahatma Gandhi]], [[BR Ambedkar]] and other revolutionaries of the [[Indian Independence movement]]. A model of detention camp was also paraded in the march. ====North East Delhi riots==== {{Main|2020 Delhi riots}} [[File:Delhi Riots 2020.jpg|thumb|Many Cars burnt by Mob in Shiv Vihar]] [[File:North East Delhi Riots 2020 (1).jpg|thumb|The burnt Gym at Shiv Vihar.]] On 24 February, [[North East Delhi riots|violent clashes]] occurred at [[Jaffrabad, Delhi|Jaffrabad]] and Maujpur in which one police officer and a protester were killed. The pro-CAA demonstrators indulged in stone pelting with the anti-CAA protesters and vandalised houses, vehicles and shops. The police personnel used [[tear gas]] and [[lathicharge]] against the protestors.{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/head-constable-killed-during-clashes-over-caa-in-northeast-delhi-police/articleshow/74283695.cms|title=Head constable killed during clashes over CAA in northeast Delhi: Police|date=24 February 2020|work=The Economic Times|access-date=24 February 2020}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/maujpur-protest-stone-throwing-violence-as-citizenship-law-supporters-protesters-clash-near-delhis-j-2184831|title=Cop Killed In Delhi Clashes Over CAA, Trump Due At 7:30 pm: 10 Points|website=NDTV.com|access-date=24 February 2020}} Later, it was reported that four protestors also died during the violence.{{Cite news|last1=Trivedi|first1=Saurabh|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/cities/Delhi/pro-anti-caa-groups-clash-in-northeast-delhis-maujpur/article30901937.ece|title=Policeman among 5 killed in Delhi violence over CAA|date=24 February 2020|work=The Hindu|access-date=25 February 2020|last2=Bhandari|first2=Hemani|issn=0971-751X}} The [[Ministry of Home Affairs (India)|Ministry of Home Affairs]] stated that the violence appeared orchestrated because of U.S. President [[Donald Trump]]'s visit to India.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/videos/city/delhi/anti-caa-protests-in-delhi-entire-violence-appears-to-be-orchestrated-says-mha/videoshow/74286979.cms|title=Anti CAA protests in Delhi: Entire violence appears to be orchestrated, says MHA|date=24 February 2020|website=The Times of India|access-date=25 February 2020}} On 26 February, The [[United States Commission on International Religious Freedom]] (USCIRF) condemned the violence in New Delhi.{{Cite web|url=https://www.uscirf.gov/news-room/press-releases-statements/uscirf-condemns-violence-in-india-s-capital-city|title=USCIRF Condemns Violence in India's Capital City|website=uscirf.gov|date=26 February 2020|access-date=28 February 2020}} USCIRF Chair [[Tony Perkins (politician)|Tony Perkins]] said, "We urge the Indian government to make serious efforts to protect Muslims and others targeted by mob violence."{{Cite web|date=2020-02-27|title=US Commission on Religious Freedom condemns mob violence in Delhi, urges Centre to ensure safety of all citizens including Muslims|url=https://www.firstpost.com/india/us-commission-on-religious-freedom-condemns-mob-violence-in-delhi-urges-centre-to-ensure-safety-of-all-citizens-including-muslims-8091511.html|access-date=2021-07-11|website=Firstpost}} The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has also condemned the New Delhi violence. It stated that steps must be taken to prevent the incidents of "[[Islamophobia]]" in the country. The OIC statement further went on to add that it "condemns the recent and alarming violence against Muslims in India, resulting in the death and injury of innocent people and the arson and vandalism of mosques and Muslim-owned properties."{{Cite web|date=2020-04-22|title=The OIC Statement on Indian Muslims|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/blogs/blunt-frank/the-oic-statement-on-indian-muslims/|access-date=2021-07-11|website=Times of India Blog|language=en-US}} The Government of India (GOI) has reacted by describing the criticism from the OIC as "inaccurate, selective, and misleading." The GOI also described USCIRF's criticism as "factually inaccurate and misleading, and appear to be aimed at politicising the issue." {{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/delhi-violence-india-slams-misleading-irresponsible-remarks-by-international-bodies/articleshow/74335934.cms|title=Delhi violence: India slams 'misleading, irresponsible' remarks by international bodies|website=[[The Times of India]] |date=27 February 2020|access-date=28 February 2020}} ==== Shaheen Bagh ==== {{Main|Shaheen Bagh protest}} [[File:Shaheen Bagh on the eve of New Year.jpg|thumb|Shaheen Bagh on New Year's Eve.]] Since 14 December, a continuous 24/7 sit in protest is being conducted. The protest began in the afternoon of 14 December with just 15 local women, and went on to gather thousands of protesters with crowds reaching as high as 100,000 on Sundays.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/unfazed-by-cold-200-women-stage-sit-in-at-shaheen-bagh-police-seek-to-end-protest-1631797-2019-12-26|title=Unfazed by cold, 200 women stage sit-in at Shaheen Bagh, police seek to end protest|date=26 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=14 January 2020}}{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/rights/watch-women-homemakers-lead-protests-against-caa-at-delhis-shaheen-bagh|title=Women, Homemakers Lead Protests Against CAA at Delhi's Shaheen Bagh|website=The Wire|access-date=14 January 2020}} On New Year's Eve, thousands of protesters camping at the site sung the [[Indian national anthem]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/delhi-news/protesters-usher-in-new-year-2020-at-delhis-shaheen-bagh-with-national-anthem-2157039|title=Protesters Usher In New Year At Delhi's Shaheen Bagh With National Anthem|publisher=NDTV|access-date=14 January 2020}} They were joined in by celebrities and activists on 31 December.{{Cite web|url=http://www.millenniumpost.in/delhi/celebrities-activists-join-protesters-at-shaheen-bagh-to-celebrate-new-year-393726|title=Celebrities, activists join protesters at Shaheen Bagh to celebrate New Year|last1=Abbas|first1=Zafar|date=31 December 2019|website=Millennium Post|access-date=14 January 2020}} But in a turn of events, the protest took a turn for the worse when anti India slogans were shouted at Shaheen Bagh with reference to Jinnah and a call out to try and break India (''Jinnah wali Azadi''), where many miscreants with their own agenda had joined.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/delhi/bjp-man-tweets-video-with-people-chanting-jinnah-wali-azadi/articleshow/73199840.cms|title=BJP man tweets video with people chaanting 'Jinnah wali azadi'|last1=TOI|author=|date=11 January 2020|website=[[The Times of India]] |access-date=14 January 2020}} The day was also recorded to have been the coldest in Delhi in the past 100 years.{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/rights/shaheen-bagh-anti-caa-protest|title=Organiser 'Calls Off' Shaheen Bagh Anti-CAA Protest, Locals Continue Dharna|website=The Wire|date=2 January 2020 |access-date=14 January 2020}} On 10 January, the [[Delhi High Court]] rejected a plea to shift the Shaheen Bagh protesters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/hc-turns-down-plea-on-shifting-protesters-from-shaheen-bagh-1635649-2020-01-10|title=HC turns down plea on shifting protesters from Shaheen Bagh|date=10 January 2020|website=India Today|access-date=14 January 2020}} The area has been covered with protest artwork.{{Cite web|url=https://scroll.in/article/948440/the-art-of-resistance-ringing-in-the-new-year-with-anti-caa-protesters-at-shaheen-bagh|title=The Art of Resistance: Ringing in the new year with CAA protesters at Delhi's Shaheen Bagh|last1=Venkataramakrishnan|first1=Rohan|website=Scroll.in|date=January 2020 |access-date=14 January 2020}} === West Bengal === On Saturday, 14 December 2019, violent protests occurred in West Bengal as the protesters attacked railway stations and public buses. Five trains were set on fire by the protesters in [[Lalgola railway station|Lalgola]] and [[Krishnapur railway station]]s in [[Murshidabad district]]; railway tracks were also damaged in Suti.{{cite news |title=Anti-Citizenship Act protests turn violent in West Bengal, Assam situation eases |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/other-states/protests-against-citizenship-law-continue-in-bengal-road-and-rail-blockade-at-several-places/article30304513.ece |access-date=14 December 2019 |work=The Hindu |date=14 December 2019 }}{{subscription required}} On Monday, 16 December 2019, tens of thousands of people joined a protest march led by Chief Minister [[Mamata Banerjee]] and her ruling [[All India Trinamool Congress|Trinamool Congress]] party.{{cite news |title=India PM plea for calm as citizenship unrest rages |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-50805092 |access-date=16 December 2019 |date=16 December 2019}} Mamata Banerjee stated that the NRC and CAA would not be implemented in West Bengal state as long as she was alive. She appealed people not to resort to violence, while accusing people from outside the state and members of the [[Bharatiya Janata Party|BJP]] of engaging in arson.{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/caa-nrc-over-my-dead-body-mamata-banerjee-ldf-and-udf-on-one-platform/articleshow/72781193.cms|title=CAA, NRC over 'my Dead Body': Mamata Banerjee; LDF and UDF on one platform|date=16 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=17 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/bengal-cm-mamata-banerjee-warns-of-strict-action-amid-violent-protests-over-citizenship-law/story-7Z3LiomT8prIICIXoiHcFJ.html|title=Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee warns of strict action amid violent protests over citizenship law|date=15 December 2019|website=Hindustan Times|access-date=17 December 2019}} On Tuesday, 17 December 2019, protests that included road and rail blockades continued in parts of West Bengal including the districts of [[South 24 Parganas]], [[North 24 Parganas district|North 24 Parganas]] and [[Nadia district|Nadia]].{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/govt-firm-on-caa-protests-continue-opposition-petitions-president-top-developments-and-reactions/articleshow/72857200.cms|title=Govt firm on CAA, protests continue, opposition petitions President: Top developments and reactions |date=17 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=17 December 2019}} On 19 December 2019, a crowd with thousands of protesters gathered at Moulali in central Kolkata to peacefully object CAA and NRC. Mamata Banerjee held a second rally in Kolkata and stated that the Central Government was trying to project the CAA Protests as though it was a Hindu vs Muslim fight. She also stated that "BJP is buying skull caps for its cadres who are wearing them while vandalising properties to malign a particular community,".{{cite news |title=Anti-CAA rally in Kolkata: BJP buying skull caps to disturb peace, says Mamata Banerjee |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/anti-caa-rally-in-kolkata-bjp-buying-skull-caps-to-disturb-peace-says-mamata-banerjee-1629755-2019-12-19 |access-date=19 December 2019 |work=India Today |date=19 December 2019 }} Earlier, on 18 December, a young [[BJP]] worker along with five associates, wearing [[lungi]] and [[Taqiyah (cap)|skullcap]] were seen by the local residents throwing stones on a train engine. The arsonists were caught by the locals who handed them to the [[Murshidabad]] police.{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/west-bengal/stone-gang-in-fake-skullcap-held-by-murshidabad-police/cid/1728657|title=Stone gang in fake skullcap held by Murshidabad police|website=The Telegraph|access-date=20 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/communalism/murshidabad-bjp-lungi-skullcap |title=BJP Worker, Supporters 'in Skullcaps' Held for Pelting Stones on Train, Says Report |website=The Wire|access-date=20 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.freepressjournal.in/india/identify-them-by-their-clothes-bjp-worker-dons-skull-cap-before-throwing-stones-in-bengal |title=Identify them by their clothes? 'BJP worker' dons skull cap before throwing stones in Bengal |website=The Free Press Journal|access-date=20 December 2019}} On 21 December 2019, a protest march that the police estimated to be of 10,000 people was held in [[Kolkata]] from [[Shaheed Minar, Kolkata|Shahid Minar]] till [[Mahajati Sadan]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/west-bengal/protest-floodgates-open-in-a-flash-in-calcutta/cid/1729195|title=Protest floodgates open, in a flash|website=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota|access-date=21 December 2019}} Between 13 and 17 December 2019, multiple incidents of violence were reported during the protests. According to the police, as of 21 December, more than 600 people had been arrested for allegedly being involved in the violence. On 23 December 2019, Governor [[Jagdeep Dhankhar]] was stopped by the students at [[Jadavpur University]], from attending the university convocation ceremony. The vice-president of the BJP West Bengal unit raised questions that why the Muslims were excluded from the amendment if it was not about religion.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/protests-in-west-bengal-against-citizenship-act/articleshow/72950947.cms|title=Protests in West Bengal against Citizenship Act|date=24 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=24 December 2019}} On 24 December 2019, Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee lead a protest march in Kolkata from [[Swami Vivekananda]] statue at [[Bidhan Sarani]]. She accused PM Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah of making contradictory statements. She stated "The prime minister is saying there has been no discussion or proposal on NRC. But a few days ago, BJP president and Union Home Minister Amit Shah had said NRC exercise would be carried out across the country. Both the statements are contradictory. We wonder who is speaking the truth. They are trying to create confusion,".{{Cite web|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/india/citizenship-amendment-act-protests-live-updates-mamata-banerjee-west-bengal-new-delhi-6182255/|title=Citizenship Act protests LIVE updates: CM's who opposed NRC should stop NPR process as well, says CPI(M)|date=24 December 2019|website=The Indian Express|access-date=24 December 2019}} On 3 January, the radical Islamic organisation [[Popular Front of India]] (PFI) had planned an anti CAA protest for 5 January, but the West Bengal police denied permission for it.{{cite news |title=Popular Front of India rally denied permission by West Bengal Police |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/other-states/popular-front-of-india-rally-denied-permission-by-west-bengal-police/article30471382.ece |access-date=3 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=3 January 2020}}{{subscription required}} Since 7 January, women had been conducting a sit in protest at the grounds of Park Circus in Kolkata. On 11 January, PM Modi visited Kolkata on a two-day official visit. Hundreds of people protested against CAA at the Kolkata airport. Several parties and student unions launched a protest at the Dorina Crossing at Esplanade in Kolkata. SFI leader stated that the protests would continue till Sunday, until PM Modi (who had been visiting the city) was in Kolkata. On 22 January, a 5 kilometre long procession against CAA was led by CM Mamta Bannerjee in [[Darjeeling]], West Bengal. The participants wore ethnic dresses and also played musical instruments. The participants chanted anti CAA slogans and carried banners and posters. On 26 January (Republic Day), thousands formed parallel 11 km long human chains from [[Shyambazar]] to Golpark in Kolkata.{{Cite web|url=https://scroll.in/latest/951174/kolkata-thousands-form-11-km-human-chain-to-protest-against-citizenship-law-and-nrc|title=Kolkata: Thousands form 11-km human chain to protest against citizenship law and NRC|date=26 January 2020|website=Scroll.in}} === Punjab === Protests begun in [[Malerkotla|Malerkotta]], [[Patiala]] and [[Ludhiana]] with the support of [[Khalsa Aid]], Alliance of Sikh Organisations and various Dalit organisations.{{Cite web|url=https://www.thecitizen.in/index.php/en/NewsDetail/index/9/18011/Punjab-Protests-CAB-Big-Rallies-Across-the-State-|title=Punjab Protests CAB, Big Rallies Across the State|last1=khanna|first1=rajeev|website=The Citizen|date=15 December 2019 |access-date=14 January 2020}} By 17 December, protests were being held in [[Amritsar]], Malerkotla, [[Machhiwara]], [[Bathinda]] and [[Ludhiana]]. Students of [[Panjab University]], [[Punjabi University]] and [[Central University of Punjab]] took a leading role in the protests backed by the Association of Democratic Rights. [[Razia Sultana (politician)|Razia Sultana]], the only Muslim MLA and a Minister of the [[Second Amarinder Singh ministry|Punjab Cabinet]] organised a large protest in Malerkotla.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/amritsar/students-take-lead-as-caa-protest-spreads-across-state/articleshow/72783273.cms|title=Students take charge as CAA protests spread across Punjab|date=17 December 2019|first1=Neel|last1=Kamal|website=The Times of India|access-date=14 January 2020}} A cabinet meeting in Punjab decided to proceed as per the decision of the state assembly. A statement was released stating, "The ministers also expressed concern over the implications of the blatantly unconstitutional and divisive CAA, NRC and NPR," They also expressed concern on the violence over CAA stating, the issue "threatened to rip apart the secular fabric of the nation". === Uttar Pradesh === {{Main|CAA protests in Uttar Pradesh}} Protests were held in [[Aligarh]], [[Kanpur]], [[Bareilly]], [[Varanasi]] and [[Lucknow]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/lucknow/protests-against-citizenship-amendment-act-continue-in-up/story-xFxDfPruuMyjIVPrsjTo6I.html|title=Protests against Citizenship Amendment Act continue in UP|date=13 December 2019|website=Hindustan Times|access-date=16 December 2019}} [[Banaras Hindu University]] students also protested the police action targeting AMU and Jamia students.{{cite news |title=UP to Kerala, Bengal to Gujarat: How India Vented Out Anger Against Police Crackdown in Jamia, AMU |url=https://www.news18.com/news/india/citizenship-amendment-bill-protests-live-updates-jamia-millia-islamia-citizenship-act-2019-amu-assam-west-bengal-2425443.html |access-date=17 December 2019 |work=News18 |date=17 December 2019}} However, they also held a rally in support of the CAA and NRC.{{Cite news|url=http://www.uniindia.com/bhu-students-hold-rally-in-support-of-caa-and-nrc/north/news/1825380.html|title=BHU students hold rally in support of CAA and NRC|date=17 December 2019|work=United News of India|access-date=28 February 2020}} On 19 December, the administration banned public assembly all over the state to prevent further protests in the state. Access to the internet was restricted in [[Azamgarh district]] for 2 days, after protest continued for 2 days in the area. In Lucknow, several buses, cars, media vans and motorbikes were torched. [[Uttar Pradesh]] Chief Minister [[Yogi Adityanath]] stated that the authorities would seize the properties of those who indulge in violence in the state.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-protests-will-seize-properties-of-those-who-indulge-in-violence-says-yogi-adityanath-1629778-2019-12-19|title=CAA protests: Will seize properties of those who indulge in violence, says Yogi Adityanath|date=19 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=21 December 2019}} On 20 December 2019, six protesters are killed in police firing in UP. According to the [[Press Trust of India]], the death toll from Friday's protests in Uttar Pradesh's 13 districts has risen to 11.{{Cite web|url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2019/12/modi-summons-ministers-india-protests-death-toll-increases-191221063611978.html|title=8-year-old boy among people killed as protests continue in India|publisher=Al Jazeera|access-date=21 December 2019}} On 21 December 2019, violent protests along with alleged police brutality were reported from several districts across the state. Access to the internet was restricted. The number of fatalities in the state increased to 16. Multiple clashes causing injuries to several people were reported. According to the police, 263 policemen were injured, of which 57 were firearm injuries. NDTV later reported that they could only find one policeman with bullet wound and the police did not share the list of 57 policemen with bullet injuries. Police arrested 705 people in the state, with 102 arrested for making allegedly objectionable remarks or social media posts.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/lucknow/anti-caa-stir-15-die-in-protests-in-uttar-pradesh-705-arrested/articleshow/72916185.cms|title=UP News: CAA protests in Uttar Pradesh; 15 killed in UP, 705 arrested {{!}} Lucknow News |date=21 December 2019|first1=Pathikrit|last1=Chakraborty|website=The Times of India|access-date=21 December 2019}} According to the UP Police, as of 21 December, a total of 218 people had been arrested in Lucknow. On 24 December, [[Uttar Pradesh Police]] stated that 21,500 people were charged in 15 FIRs for violent incidents in Kanpur.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/21-500-booked-for-violence-in-kanpur-1631048-2019-12-24|title=21,500 booked for violence in Kanpur|website=India Today|date=24 December 2019 |access-date=25 December 2019}} In [[Muzaffarnagar]] the government, sealed 67 shops. Chief Minister, Adityanath had threatened the protesters that his government would auction the properties of the rioters to recover the losses due to the damage made to the property.{{Cite web|url=https://www.timesnownews.com/india/article/shocking-up-police-caught-vandalising-property-in-muzaffarnagar-during-anti-caa-protests-watch/531626|title=Shocking! UP Police caught vandalising property in Muzaffarnagar during anti-CAA protests|website=Times Now|date=25 December 2019 |access-date=25 December 2019}} On 26 December, In Sambhal, UP, the government sent notices to 26 people for their alleged involvement in damaging properties during protests and asked them to explain their position or pay for the loss due to damage of property. The losses to the property were assessed to be {{INRConvert|11.66|l|lk=on}}. Earlier, on 22 December, the UP government had created a panel to assess the damage to the property and to recover the losses by seizing the property of the alleged protesters. The social activists accused the government of intimidating the protesters.{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/other-states/police-framing-false-charges-to-crack-down-on-anti-caa-protests-in-up-activists/article30401119.ece|title=Anti-CAA protests: Reign of terror in Uttar Pradesh, assert activists|date=26 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=27 December 2019|issn=0971-751X}}{{subscription required}} On 28 December, the ''Indian Express'' reported that with 19 killed and 1,246 people arrested based on 372 FIRs lodged in the state, UP was the worst affected state with the biggest police crackdown in India. Even though the official figure of number of deaths so far in UP was 19, opposition parties claimed that the actual figure was higher. The UP police had maintained that the deaths of protesters were not the result of police firing, but later on they admitted that some deaths were indeed caused by the police but attributed those incidents to shots fired in self defence.{{cite news |title=Anti-CAA protests All deaths during protests in U.P. caused by police bullets, says Akhilesh Yadav |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/anti-caa-protests-all-deaths-during-protests-in-up-caused-by-police-bullets-says-akhilesh-yadav/article30485029.ece |access-date=16 January 2020 |work=The Hindu|date=5 January 2020}}{{subscription required}} On 17 January in Lucknow, around 500 women along with their children started a sit in protest at 2 pm near the Clock Tower.{{cite news |last1=Rashid |first1=Omar |title=Watch U.P. Police accused of 'stealing' blankets of women CAA protesters |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/other-states/up-police-accused-of-stealing-blankets-of-women-caa-protesters/article30598986.ece |access-date=19 January 2020 |work=The Hindu|date=19 January 2020}}{{subscription required}} The police tried to convince them to end the protest but the protesters refused to move. On the night of 18 January, Uttar Pradesh police cracked down on the CAA protesters and snatched their blankets, utensils and food items.{{cite news |title=In an Unusual Crackdown, UP Police Snatch Blankets of Anti-CAA Protesters at Lucknow's Clock Tower |url=https://www.news18.com/news/india/in-an-unusual-crackdown-up-police-snatch-blankets-of-anti-caa-protesters-at-lucknows-clock-tower-2463773.html |access-date=19 January 2020 |work=News18 |date=19 January 2020}} The video of policemen carrying away the blankets was recorded and shared on social media. The conduct of UP police was criticised by the users and the phrase "KAMBALCHOR_UPPOLICE" (blanket thief UP Police) became the top trend on Twitter in India. The police responded saying that they had confiscated the blankets following due procedure. The protesters alleged that police also cut the electricity connection to the ground, locked the public toilet nearby and poured water on the bonfire in the winter night.{{cite news |title=Anti-CAA protests: Seized blankets from protesters to disperse 'outsiders', says Lucknow police |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/cities/lucknow/anti-caa-protests-in-lucknow-up-police-seize-blankets-from-protesters-6224460/ |access-date=19 January 2020 |work=The Indian Express |date=19 January 2020}} The police had issued a prohibition on assembly in Lucknow, and stated that they will prosecute the protesters for violating it. On 21 January, police registered cases against 160 women for violation of the ban on assembly and protesting against CAA in Lucknow. On 16 December, in response to the police crackdown at Jamia Millia University in Delhi and Aligarh Muslim University over the Citizenship Amendment Act, clashes between the police and protesters occurred in Dakshintola area of [[Mau, Uttar Pradesh|Mau]], Uttar Pradesh. On 17 December, students of [[Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur|IIT Kanpur]] assembled in a peaceful protest against the CAA and to express solidarity with the students of [[Jamia Millia Islamia]].{{cite news |title=IIT Kanpur constitutes panel to decide if Faiz's poem hurts Hindu sentiment |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/iit-knp-constitutes-panel-to-decide-if-faizs-poem-hurts-hindu-sentiment/articleshow/73064163.cms |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200309074419/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/iit-knp-constitutes-panel-to-decide-if-faizs-poem-hurts-hindu-sentiment/articleshow/73064163.cms |url-status=dead |archive-date=9 March 2020 |access-date=4 January 2020 |work=The Economic Times |date=3 January 2020 }} === Karnataka === On 16 December, protests against the Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) were held in various parts of [[Karnataka]]. In [[Mysore]], hundreds of protestors marched on the street, raising slogans and took out bike rallies. The police imposed a [[Section 144|ban on public assembly]] in Mysore. In [[Bangalore]], the [[Indian Institute of Science|IISc]] students organised silent protest in the campus in solidarity with the students of Delhi and other parts of India. In [[Shimoga]], former [[Minister of Legislative Assembly|MLA]] [[K. B. Prasanna Kumar|K B Prasanna Kumar]] was leading the protests near Gandhi Park in the city. The police detained him alleging that the protests were turning violent. Protests also occurred in [[Bellary]], [[Bidar]], [[Gulbarga]], [[Kodagu]] and [[Udupi]]. In [[Raichur]] protests were held after the announcement of the CAA, as the protestors had concerns that approximately 5,000 of the 20,000 Bangladeshi immigrants in the [[Sindhanur]] camp would get Indian citizenship.{{Cite web|url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/protests-erupt-across-karnataka-against-citizenship-amendment-act-114066|title=Protests erupt across Karnataka against Citizenship Amendment Act|date=14 December 2019|website=The News Minute}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/karnataka/bangla-camp-in-raichur-district-celebrates-citizenship-act/article30299585.ece|title=Bangla camp in Raichur district celebrates Citizenship Act|first1=Sharath S.|last1=Srivatsa|date=13 December 2019 |newspaper=The Hindu}} On 21 January more than 300,000 people join an anti-CAA protest rally at [[Kalaburagi]] in Karnataka. The rally was held 13-acre Peer Bangali ground, where people waving national flags had occupied the ground and the nearby roads. [[Sitaram Yechury]], general secretary of [[Communist Party of India (Marxist)]] and [[M. Mallikarjun Kharge]], general secretary of the [[All-India Congress Committee]], activists [[Swamy Agnivesh]] and former IAS officer Sasikanth Senthil gave speeches in the event. Yechury asked the people to conduct a peaceful civil disobedience movement with aim to defy the CAA, NPR, and NCR to "save the country from Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah". ==== Bangalore ==== On 19 December, hundreds of protesters, including historian [[Ramachandra Guha]], were detained by the [[Bangalore Police]] from [[Bangalore Town Hall|Town Hall]].{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/caa-protests-police-detain-hundreds-of-protesters-in-bengaluru/articleshow/72882489.cms|title=CAA protests: Police detain hundreds of protesters in Bengaluru|last1=M|first1=Akshatha|date=19 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=19 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/section-144-be-imposed-bengaluru-and-other-parts-karnataka-thursday-114335 |title=Section 144 to be imposed in Bengaluru and other parts of Karnataka from Thursday |website=thenewsminute.com |access-date=18 December 2019|date=18 December 2019 }} On 20 December, Karnataka High Court asked the state government to explain the prohibitions on the assembly in the state.{{Cite web|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/cities/bangalore/will-check-legality-of-imposing-144-in-bengaluru-karnataka-hc-6176535/|title='Are you going to ban each and every protest?': Karnataka HC raps govt over Bengaluru detentions|date=20 December 2019|website=The Indian Express|access-date=20 December 2019}} On 23 December, over 100,000 protesters gathered around the town hall in Bangalore.{{Cite web|url=https://www.livemint.com/news/india/over-1-lakh-people-gather-in-bengaluru-to-protest-caa-nrc-11577099363409.html|title=Over 1 lakh people gather in Bengaluru to protest CAA,NRC|last1=Poovanna|first1=Sharan|date=23 December 2019|website=Livemint|access-date=14 January 2020}} On 4 January in Bangalore hundreds of protesters participated in a rally and accused [[Modi government]] of attempts to divide India on the basis of religion, and distracting people from the issues of economic slowdown and job losses in the country. Respecting Section 144 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, in force in the city until the midnight of 21 December, around 60 students from the [[Indian Institute of Management Bangalore]] came out in turns at the campus gate and left blank placards and their footwear at the campus gate, letting their footwear represent them.{{cite news |last1=Ahmad |first1=Samreen |title=Citizenship Act unrest: IIM-B students let their shoes protest for them |url=https://www.business-standard.com/article/current-affairs/citizenship-act-unrest-iim-b-students-let-their-shoes-protest-for-them-119122100026_1.html |website=[[Business Standard]] |date=21 December 2019 |access-date=12 May 2021}} As of 14 January 2020, 82 separate protest occasions had taken place in the city. The number was observed to be the highest number of protests in a month in the city's history. The protests were primarily held in Town Hall and [[Freedom Park, Bangalore|Freedom Park]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.newindianexpress.com/states/karnataka/2020/jan/14/bengaluru-sees-record-82-protests-in-just-one-month-on-caa-and-nrc-2089262.html|title=Bengaluru sees record 82 protests in just one month on CAA and NRC|website=The New Indian Express|date=14 January 2020 |access-date=14 January 2020}} On 25 January, more than thousand women staged a sit in protest at Frazer Town in Bangalore for 48 hours.{{cite news |title=Bengaluru women's sit-in protest against CAA extended to 48 hours |url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/bengaluru-womens-sit-protest-against-caa-extended-48-hours-116769 |access-date=25 January 2020 |work=thenewsminute.com |date=25 January 2020}} ====Mangalore==== According to ''India Today'', on 19 December seven CCTV recorded the arrival of a mob near a Mangalore police station along with a tempo carrying gunny bags full of stones. They then made an organised attempt to attack a police station, block all roads to the station, stone the police personnel and steal firearms. This was followed by the police firing on the mob, killing two people. The family of the deceased stated that the police used excessive force and should have tried to disperse the crowd instead.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/mangaluru-violence-cctv-footage-december-19-police-protesters-1631125-2019-12-24|title=CAA protest: Was Mangaluru violence pre-planned? CCTV footage reveal how events unfolded on Dec 19|first1=Saurabh|last1=Vaktania|website=India Today|date=24 December 2019|access-date=17 January 2020}} In [[Mangalore]] 38 protesters from [[Campus Front of India]] who were marching towards the [[Deputy commissioner]] residence were arrested by the police arrested on charges of blocking traffic on Balmatta Road.{{cite news |title=CAA: Protesters block road; 38 arrested |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/cities/Mangalore/caa-protesters-block-road-38-arrested/article30323653.ece |access-date=16 December 2019 |work=The Hindu |date=16 December 2019 }} On 19 December, a curfew was imposed in Mangalore until 20 December, while protesters marched on the streets defying prohibitory orders. Two people died with gunshot wounds after police fired on the crowd. The police in [[riot gear]] then entered the Highland Hospital where the two people with gunshot wounds were brought. The police were accused of beating up patients and their relatives. The incident was caught on [[CCTV Cameras]], in which the policemen were seen banging on the hospital's [[Intensive care unit|ICU]] doors. Home Minister of Karnataka [[Basavaraj Bommai]] accused the people from [[Kerala]] for the violence in Mangalore.{{Cite web | url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/people-from-kerala-behind-mangaluru-violence-karnataka-home-minister-1629887-2019-12-20 |title = People from Kerala behind Mangaluru violence: Karnataka Home Minister| date=20 December 2019 }} Karnataka Police restricted the entry of people from Kerala to Mangalore at the [[Thalappady, Kasaragod|Thalappady]] state border and detained more than 50 people without identity cards.{{cite news |title=Aftermath of anti-CAA deaths, entry clampdown at Kerala-Mangalore border – OrissaPOST |url=https://www.orissapost.com/aftermath-of-anti-caa-deaths-entry-clampdown-at-kerala-mangalore-border/ |access-date=21 December 2019 |date=20 December 2019}} On 20 December, the mobile phones of several journalists in Mangalore (many from Kerala) were confiscated and the journalists were detained.{{Cite web|url=https://www.deccanherald.com/national/kerala-cm-condemns-detention-of-journalists-in-mluru-787219.html|title=Kerala CM condemns detention of journalists in M'luru|date=20 December 2019|website=Deccan Herald|access-date=20 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://english.mathrubhumi.com/news/kerala/police-claim-malayalee-journalists-arrested-in-mangaluru-were-fake-1.4378100|title=Police claim Malayalee journalists arrested in Mangaluru were fake|website=Mathrubhumi|date=20 December 2019 |access-date=20 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.theweek.in/news/india/2019/12/20/mangaluru-journalists-arrested-minister-blames-people-from-kerala-for-violence.html|title=Mangaluru: Journalists arrested; BJP leader says 50 goondas came from Kerala|website=The Week|access-date=20 December 2019}} The Chief Minister of Kerala, [[Pinarayi Vijayan]] who condemned the action as an attack on [[media freedom]] and intervened after which the journalists were released.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/thiruvananthapuram/cm-leaders-protest-ktaka-police-action-against-scribes/articleshow/72909461.cms|title=Kerala CM, leaders protest Karnataka police action against scribes|date=21 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=21 December 2019}} On 22 December, the Karnataka government announced a compensation of {{INRConvert|10|l|lk=on}} each to the families of the two men killed in violent protests in Mangalore on 19 December. The compensation was later withheld and an enquiry was initiated on the killed men. On 15 January, more than 200,000 people joined the anti-CAA protests in Mangalore, hundreds of whom came to the venue in boats carrying Indian flags. Activists Harsh Mander and Kannan Gopinathan gave speeches during the protests. An organiser said that they considered the CAA, an anti-constitutional law that will be affecting not just Muslims but all religions. The event ended peacefully with the singing of National anthem. ===Tamil Nadu=== On 16 December, protests against CAA were organised by Muslim organisations and political parties in [[Tirupathur district]] of [[Tamil Nadu]]. Effigies of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah were burnt, after which more than a hundred people were detained by the police.{{cite news |title=Citizenship Act stir: 100 protesters in Tamil Nadu held after burning effigies of Modi, Shah |url=https://www.newindianexpress.com/states/tamil-nadu/2019/dec/16/citizenship-act-stir-100-protesters-in-tamil-nadu-held-after-burning-effigies-of-modi-shah-2076998.html |access-date=16 December 2019 |work=The New Indian Express}} Demonstrations protesting against the CAA and the attack on students in Delhi were also held by the students of Government Law College at [[Katpadi]] and Government Arts College in [[Tiruvannamalai]]. On 20 December, actor [[Siddharth (actor)|Siddharth]], singer [[T. M. Krishna]] and 600 others were detained for anti-CAA protests in Valluvar Kottam In Chennai.{{cite news |title=Photos of actors Parvathy and Sidharth taking part in CAA protests goes viral |url=https://english.mathrubhumi.com/movies-music/movie-news/photos-of-actors-parvathy-and-sidharth-taking-part-in-caa-protests-goes-viral-1.4378296 |access-date=20 December 2019 |work=Mathrubhumi |date=20 December 2019 |archive-date=20 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191220165224/https://english.mathrubhumi.com/movies-music/movie-news/photos-of-actors-parvathy-and-sidharth-taking-part-in-caa-protests-goes-viral-1.4378296 |url-status=dead }} On 23 December [[Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam]] along with allied parties held a "mega rally" as an anti-CAA protest in Chennai.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/chennai/caa-dmk-allies-to-take-out-rally-in-chennai-bjp-leader-appeals-to-students-not-to-fall-prey-to-deceitful-words-of-oppn-parties/articleshow/72867369.cms|title=CAA Protest Chennai: DMK, allies to take out rally in Chennai; BJP leader appeals to students not to fall prey to 'deceitful words' of oppn parties|last1=Sivakumar|first1=B.|date=18 December 2019|first2=Shanmughasundaram|last2=J.|website=The Times of India|access-date=21 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.firstpost.com/india/opposition-steps-up-protests-against-caa-dmk-leads-massive-rally-in-chennai-congress-holds-satyagraha-at-rajghat-uttar-pradesh-pfi-chief-arrested-7818831.html|title=Opposition steps up protests against CAA: DMK leads massive rally in Chennai, Congress holds 'satyagraha' at Rajghat; Uttar Pradesh PFI chief arrested|website=Firstpost|date=23 December 2019|access-date=25 December 2019}} Protests were also continued to be held in various parts of Tamil Nadu including [[Salem, Tamil Nadu|Salem]] and [[Krishnagiri]].{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/citizenship-amendment-act-protests-top-developments/articleshow/72952429.cms|title=Citizenship Amendment Act protests: Top developments|date=24 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=25 December 2019}} In Chennai, eight anti-CAA activists, including five women, were arrested for drawing ''[[kolam]]s'' criticising the CAA and NRC.{{cite news |last1= Khanna |first1= Rohit |date= 2 January 2020 |title= Bengal finds new protest in Kolam |url= https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/kolkata/bengal-finds-new-protest-tool-in-kolam/articleshow/73062530.cms |work= The Times of India |location= Kolkata |access-date= 4 January 2020 }} Following their arrest, the opposition DMK officially supported the ''kolam'' protest, and anti-CAA and anti-NRC ''kolams'' were drawn outside the houses of its leaders and members.{{cite news |last1= Shivakumar |first1= B |date= 30 December 2019 |title= Kolam protest against CAA and NRC spreads in Tamil Nadu |url= https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/chennai/kolam-protest-against-caa-and-ncr-spreads-in-tamil-nadu/articleshow/73024170.cms |work= The Times of India |location= Chennai |access-date= 4 January 2020 }} On 16 January, police in [[Chennai]] banned all protests in the city for a period of 15 days. 5 women and 1 man (including a physically disabled person) were detained by the police for protesting against CAA. ===Gujarat=== On 16 December, around 50 people protesting outside the [[Indian Institute of Management Ahmedabad]] and [[CEPT University]] were detained by the [[Gujarat Police]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/protest-outside-iim-a-in-support-of-jamia-students-50-held/article30321091.ece|title=Protest outside IIM-A in support of Jamia students; 50 held|date=16 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=21 December 2019|agency=Press Trust of India|issn=0971-751X}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.deccanherald.com/national/west/caa-protest-iim-ahmedabad-prof-roughed-up-by-police-786045.html|title=CAA protest: IIM-Ahmedabad prof roughed up by police|date=16 December 2019|website=Deccan Herald|access-date=21 December 2019}} On 17 December, five students were arrested by the [[Gujarat police]] for allegedly creating a [[graffiti]] against Citizenship Act. According to the police, the students (all in their early twenties) of the [[Maharaja Sayajirao University]]'s Fine Arts department had made a graffiti that said "no CAB Modi", but instead of the letter 'o' in the phrase the students made a '[[swastika]]' sign. The graffiti was made at locations that included police headquarters, Kala Ghoda Circle, the Fatehgunj pavilion wall and the wall of a hostel near Rosary School. A police complaint was registered on 16 December for "using provocative and humiliating words to hurt sentiments of the people from one community and cause violence, and also damaging public property". The Police alleged that "They used slogans with certain symbols in their graffiti to intentionally hurt religious sentiments of a particular community and affect public peace and cause riots against members of a community. They also damaged public property." The police has already arrested five and two students who were absconding were being searched. On 19 December 2019, peaceful protest march were carried out in several parts of [[Ahmedabad]]. The peaceful protest march in [[Shah-e-Alam's Roza]] area turned violent after police intervened to disperse the crowd. Police used tear gas to counter [[stone pelting]] while trying to disperse a crowd of 2000 protesters. Protest rallies and strikes were also organised in several cities of Gujarat.{{Cite news|url=https://www.gujaratsamachar.com/news/madhya-gujarat/protests-rally-in-gujarat-against-nrc-and-caa|title=NRC અને CAAના વિરોધમાં ગુજરાતમાં ઠેર ઠેર રેલી- પ્રદર્શનો|date=20 December 2019|work=[[Gujarat Samachar]]|access-date=20 December 2019|language=gu|trans-title=Rallies and protests in several places in Gujarat against CAA and NRC}} On 19 December 2019, videos surfaced allegedly showing protestors attacking policemen in Ahmedabad and Banaskantha.{{cite news |title= Mob launches murderous attack on cops in Ahmedabad, locals rescue them |url= https://www.indiatoday.in/india/video/watch-mob-launches-murderous-attack-on-cops-in-ahmedabad-locals-rescue-them-1629834-2019-12-19 |work= India Today |date= 19 December 2019 |access-date= 31 December 2019 }}{{cite news |title= Gujarat: Anti-CAA protestors attack police jeep in Banaskantha |url= https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/gujarat-anti-caa-protestors-attack-police-jeep-in-banaskantha/videoshow/72900862.cms |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20200306171708/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/gujarat-anti-caa-protestors-attack-police-jeep-in-banaskantha/videoshow/72900862.cms |url-status= dead |archive-date= 6 March 2020 |work= The Economic Times |location= Gujarat |date= 20 December 2019 |access-date= 31 December 2019 }} On 25 December 2019, many activists alleged that [[Nirma University]] in Ahmedabad tried to "intimidate" its students who took part in protests against the Citizenship (Amendment) Act and also advised their parents to "counsel" them. The SMS sent by the university to the protesting students' parents read thus: " It has come to our knowledge that your ward was involved in protest against recent issues. The Police and Intelligence Bureau-IB have taken details of your ward from us... This is also to inform you that if your ward continues to participate in the protest, the police might create a record against him."{{cite web |title=Activists accuse Nirma University of intimidating students for taking part in anti-CAA protest |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/activists-accuse-nirma-university-of-intimidating-students-for-taking-part-in-anti-caa-protest-1631472-2019-12-25 |website=[[India Today]] |date=25 December 2019 |access-date=12 May 2021}} On 15 January 2020, a petition was filed in Gujarat High Court asking for the court's intervention to allow the petitioners to hold Anti-CAA protests in Ahmedabad. The petitioners stated that police did not grant them permission for peaceful anti-CAA protests but granted permission to 62 programmes held by the BJP to support the CAA. The petitioners said that the administration keeps denying the permission to Anti-CAA protesters and called it a violation of fundamental rights of the citizens. The Court asked the police to decide timely on the protests application. === Kerala === {{See also|Human chain in Kerala, 2020}}[[File:People's_Long_March_against_CAA_&_NRC_at_Ernakulam_on_23-12-2019_165422.jpg|thumb|Protests at [[Ernakulam]], c. 23 December 2019.]] On 16 December, the ruling coalition, the [[Left Democratic Front (Kerala)|Left Democratic Front (LDF)]], and the opposition coalition, the [[United Democratic Front (Kerala)|United Democratric Front (UDF)]], organised a joint [[hunger strike]] in the [[Thiruvananthapuram]] Martyr's Square. Chief Minister [[Pinarayi Vijayan]] stated that Kerala will "stand together to fight against the evil designs of the Modi government undermining the secular credentials of India", terming the Citizens Amendment Act "anti-constitutional and anti-people". Leader of the Opposition [[Ramesh Chennithala]] observed that another struggle for Independence is required to save the constitution from "[[fascist]] forces".{{cite news|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/kerala-s-ruling-left-and-congress-led-opposition-stage-joint-protest-against-caa-1628612-2019-12-16|title=Kerala's ruling Left and Congress-led Opposition stage joint protest against CAA|last1=Jacob|first1=Jeemon|date=16 December 2019|work=India Today|access-date=17 December 2019}} On 17 December, A dawn-to-dusk hartal (shutdown) was observed in Kerala by [[Welfare Party of India]], [[Bahujan Samaj Party]], [[Social Democratic Party of India]] and other 30 organisation against the Citizenship (Amendment) Act and the police violence against students at various universities across the country, in relation to anti-CAA protests. The Kerala police detained 233 people in connection with the hartal, including 55 people in Ernakulam, 51 in Thrissur and 35 in Idukki. In Palakkad 21 people have been detained, while 13 were detained in Kannur, 12 in Kottayam and 8 in Wayanad.{{Cite web|url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/hartal-against-caa-sporadic-violence-kerala-more-200-detained-114240|title=Hartal against CAA: Sporadic violence in Kerala, more than 200 detained|date=17 December 2019|website=The News Minute|access-date=17 January 2020}} On 23 December, multiple protests in Kochi was organised and took place.{{Cite web|url=https://www.newindianexpress.com/states/kerala/2019/dec/23/citizenship-act-stir-multiple-protests-in-kochi-organised-today-2079727.html|title=Citizenship Act stir: Multiple protests in Kochi organised today|website=The New Indian Express|date=22 December 2019 |access-date=14 January 2020}} People's long march, which started from [[Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium (Kochi)|Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium]] premises and ended near [[Cochin Shipyard]]. Cultural activists led another march from the Gandhi Square to Vasco De Gama Square at [[Fort Kochi]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/kerala/hundreds-join-march-against-caa-nrc/article30382963.ece|title=Hundreds join march against CAA, NRC|date=23 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=14 January 2020|issn=0971-751X}} Another march led by CPI state secretary [[Kanam Rajendran]] was also held at the same time started from [[Kalamassery]] and ended in Rajendra Maidanam.{{Cite web|url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/malayalam-artistes-cultural-activists-march-kochi-protesting-caa-nrc-114663|title=Malayalam artistes, cultural activists march in Kochi protesting CAA, NRC|date=24 December 2019|website=The News Minute|access-date=14 January 2020}}{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/kochi/anti-caa-stir-kochi-hosts-star-studded-rallies/articleshow/72945589.cms|title=Anti-CAA stir: Kochi hosts star-studded rallies|date=24 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=14 January 2020}} On 24 December, [[Disability|Differently-abled]] Come together against CAA and NRC at [[Marine Drive, Kochi]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.thequint.com/photos/differentially-abled-caa-nrc-protest-kerala-kochi|title=Differently-abled Come Together Against CAA and NRC in Kochi|date=24 December 2019|website=The Quint|access-date=14 January 2020}} On 28 December, several delegates attending the [[Indian History Congress]] held at [[Kannur University]] protested during Kerala Governor [[Arif Mohammad Khan]]'s speech. Some of them were detained by police, but were later released following an intervention by Kannur University.{{cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/kerala/students-stage-protest-during-kerala-governors-speech-at-indian-history-congress/article30419272.ece|title=Students stage protest during Kerala Governor's speech at Indian History Congress|date=28 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=30 December 2019}} On 31 December, [[Kerala Legislative Assembly]] passed a resolution demanding scrapping of the Act.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/kerala-news/kerala-chief-minister-tables-resolution-against-citizenship-act-in-assembly-2156658|title=Kerala Assembly Passes Resolution Against Citizenship Act|publisher=NDTV}} On 1 January 2020 in [[Kochi]], around half a million assembled in peaceful rally to protest against the CAA-NRC, held between [[Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium, Kochi|Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium]] and [[Marine Drive, Kochi|Marine Drive]].{{cite news |last1=Rakesh |first1=K.M |title=Born in India, will die in India |url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/india/born-in-india-will-die-in-india/cid/1732209 |access-date=3 January 2020 |work=The Telegraph |date=2 January 2020}}{{cite news |title=Kochi sees massive rally by Islamic organisations against CAA-NRC |url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/kochi-sees-massive-rally-islamic-organisations-against-caa-nrc-115190 |access-date=3 January 2020 |work=The News Minute |date=2 January 2020}} On 14 January, the [[Kerala government]] approached Supreme Court to challenge the CAA under section 131 of the constitution and becomes the first state to do so. The article 131 of the Indian constitution provides Supreme Court the power to decide the disputes between the states and the Government of India. The Kerala government in its petition called the act "a violation of India's secular constitution" and accused the Indian government of dividing the country among religious lines. Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan said, "Kerala will always remain in the forefront of this fight to protect the Indian constitution and the fundamental rights of its citizens,". Vijayan added that Kerala will save the rights of the citizens from the unconstitutional CAA by fighting against it using constitutional methods.{{cite news |title=Kerala state challenges India's citizenship law in Supreme Court |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2020/01/india-kerala-challenges-citizenship-law-supreme-court-200114075604981.html |access-date=14 January 2020 |publisher=Al Jazeera |date=14 January 2020}} On 26 January, to protest against the CAA and the proposed NRC the [[Left Democratic Front (Kerala)|Left Democratic Front]] led by the Communist Party of India (Marxist) had organised the human chain. The chain was formed by approximately 6 to 7 million people and extended for a distance of 620 kilometres from [[Kasaragod district|Kasaragod]] to [[Kaliyakkavilai|Kaliyikkavila]].{{Cite web|url=https://scroll.in/latest/951132/republic-day-kerala-government-to-organise-620-km-long-human-chain-to-demand-repeal-of-caa|title=Republic Day: Kerala government to organise 620-km long human chain to demand repeal of CAA|website=Scroll.in|date=26 January 2020 |access-date=26 January 2020}} === Telangana === [[File:Millineum March at Dharna Chowk near Indira Park.jpg|220px|thumb|right|Million March, Hyderabad]] On 16 December, thousands of students from major institutes in [[Hyderabad]] such as [[University of Hyderabad]], [[Osmania University]] and [[Maulana Azad National Urdu University|MANU University]] participated in protests, demanding a rollback of the CAA.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/hyderabad/anti-caa-stir-major-protests-across-universities-in-hyderabad-too/articleshow/72801418.cms|title=CAA Protest: Major protests across universities in Hyderabad too |date=17 December 2019|first1=Nirupa |last1=Vatyam |website=The Times of India|access-date=22 December 2019}} On 21 December, numerous protests took place across Hyderabad organised by students, NGOs and various political parties. The [[Hyderabad Police]] stated that the protests remained peaceful. Protesters demanded Chief Minister [[K. Chandrashekar Rao]], whose party [[Telangana Rashtra Samithi]] had voted against the bill in parliament, to end his silence with respect to the CAA.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/hyderabad/protests-continue-against-caa-in-hyderabad/articleshow/72917663.cms|title=Protests continue against CAA in Hyderabad|date=21 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=22 December 2019}} By 23 December, it was reported that protests had spread to smaller towns, including Nizamabad.{{Cite web|url=https://mumbaimirror.indiatimes.com/news/india/hyderabad-caa-nrc-protests-spreads-to-small-towns-in-telangana-andhra-pradesh/articleshow/72942622.cms|title=Hyderabad: CAA, NRC protests spreads to small towns in Telangana, Andhra Pradesh|last1=Pavan|first1=P|date=23 December 2019|website=Mumbai Mirror|access-date=25 December 2019}} On 4 January, more than 100,000 protesters attended the protest march named "Million March" in Hyderabad. The protesters displayed placards with slogans saying "Withdraw CAA immediately," and "India's only religion in secularism". === Bihar === On 17 December, posters describing Bihar Chief Minister [[Nitish Kumar]] as "missing" were erected as a form of protest in Patna. Nitish Kumar had been criticised for his silence over the CAA. while his party, [[Janata Dal (United)]], had supported the bill in both the Houses of Parliament as part of the alliance with the [[Bharatiya Janata Party]]. The JD(U) party strategist [[Prashant Kishor]], party spokesperson [[Pavan Varma|Pavan Verma]] and MLC [[Gulam Rasool Balyawi]] had voiced dissatisfaction over the party's stance on CAA.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/bihar-caa-protests-nitish-kumar-patna-posters-hunt-missing-1628899-2019-12-17|title=Anti-CAA protests: Posters hunt for missing Nitish Kumar in Patna|date=17 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=19 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/disappointed-to-see-jdu-supporting-cab-says-prashant-kishor-1626881-2019-12-10|title=Disappointed to see JDU supporting CAB, says leader Prashant Kishor|first1=Rohit|last1=Kumar Singh|date=10 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=21 December 2019}} On 19 December, Nitish Kumar declared that the NRC will not be implemented in the state, becoming the first major [[Bharatiya Janata Party]] ally to reject the controversial measure.{{Cite web|url=https://www.deccanherald.com/national/national-politics/nrc-will-not-be-implemented-in-bihar-nda-ally-nitish-787385.html|title=NRC will not be implemented in Bihar: NDA ally Nitish|date=20 December 2019|website=Deccan Herald|access-date=21 December 2019}} On 19 December, a [[bandh]] was called by [[Communism|communist]] parties in [[Bihar]], supported by a number of small parties, where protesters blocked rail and road traffic in protest against CAA and the proposed countrywide implementation of NRC.{{Cite web|url=https://www.firstpost.com/india/train-services-disrupted-roads-blocked-ambulance-vandalised-as-anti-caa-protests-turn-violent-in-bihars-patna-darbhanga-muzaffarpur-7802851.html|title=Train services disrupted, roads blocked, ambulance vandalised as anti-CAA protests turn violent in Bihar's Patna, Darbhanga, Muzaffarpur|website=Firstpost|date=19 December 2019|access-date=19 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/news/national/protests-against-citizenship-amendment-act-train-services-affected-roads-blocked-in-bihar/article30346424.ece|title=Protests against Citizenship (Amendment) Act: Rail, road services affected in Bihar; Bengal, Assam, Meghalaya peaceful|last=PTI|website=@businessline|date=19 December 2019 |access-date=19 December 2019}} On Saturday, 21 December, a "Bihar bandh" was called by the [[Rashtriya Janata Dal]] (RJD), where bandh supporters reportedly blocked railway tracks in [[Araria district|Araria]] and [[East Champaran district|East Champaran]] districts. In [[Nawada]], bandh supporters demonstrated on [[National Highway 31 (India)|National Highway 31]] where wheels were burned on the road and the movement of vehicles was disrupted, while in [[Vaishali district|Vaishali]], the highway was blocked with the help of buffaloes. In Patna, hundreds of party supporters with lathis entered the railway stations and bus stations with party flags, but were repulsed by policemen. At [[Darbhanga]], RJD workers and supporters protested bare chest, sloganeering against Bihar chief minister Nitish Kumar and central government.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/rjd-supporters-protest-against-citizenship-law-use-buffaloes-to-block-highway-2152192|title=RJD Supporters Block Highways In Bihar Amid Citizenship Law Protest|website=NDTVIndia|access-date=21 December 2019|date=21 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/patna/bihar-bandh-rjd-supporters-use-buffaloes-to-block-highway-in-vaishali-protest-at-railway-line-in-darbhanga/articleshow/72911487.cms|title=Bihar bandh: RJD supporters use buffaloes to block highway in Vaishali, protest at railway line in Darbhanga|date=21 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=21 December 2019}} During a protest at [[Phulwari Sharif]] near Patna, some of the miscreants started pelting stones and a clash broke out between protesters and the supporters of the Act. A nearby temple and a cemetery were damaged in the clash. Eyewitnesses said police had used tear gas and water cannon to disperse the people. Police said that people of Hindu community fired bullets that injured nine Muslim men.{{cite web|url=https://www.altnews.in/alt-news-investigates-the-attack-on-a-temple-in-phulwari-sharif-bihar-during-anti-caa-protests/|title=Alt News investigates the attack on a temple in Phulwari Sharif, Bihar during anti-CAA protests|date=26 December 2019|website=[[AltNews.in]]|access-date=3 February 2020}} During the stampede, an eighteen years old teenage protester Amir Hanzla ran towards the lanes of Sangat Mohalla, a neighbourhood that was a hub of [[RSS-affiliated]] right wing Hindutva members.{{cite news |title=Boy with Tricolour found dead in Bihar |url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/india/boy-with-tricolour-found-dead-in-bihar/cid/1732433 |access-date=4 February 2020 |work=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota }} His dead body was found a week after he was allegedly murdered by the [[Hindutva]] extremists.{{cite news |title=Plot that killed boy with flag |url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/india/plot-that-killed-boy-with-flag/cid/1732835 |access-date=4 February 2020 |work=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota }} Police arrested six people accused in the case who belonged to Hindu Putra Sangathan, a Hindutva group. All of the suspects confessed their crimes and police is also investigating 18 other organisations related to this extremist group.{{cite news |last1=Roy |first1=Umesh Kumar |title=Bihar Police Arrest Hindutva Activists for Killing Teenager Who Joined Anti-CAA Protest |url=https://thewire.in/communalism/bihar-hindu-fringe-groups-teenager-killed-caa-protest |access-date=4 February 2020 |work=The Wire}} === Maharashtra === On 19 December, different NGOs and college students, backed by [[Indian National Congress|Congress]], [[Nationalist Congress Party]] and [[Left Democratic Front (Maharashtra)|Left Democratic Front]] parties, jointly formed a front with the name "''Hum Bharat Ke Log''" and held a protest at the August Kranti Maidan.{{Cite news|url= https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/all-india-protests-against-citizenship-act/articleshow/72894519.cms |title=All-India protests against Citizenship Act|date=20 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=21 December 2019}} In Mumbai, several actors including [[Farhan Akhtar]], [[Swara Bhaskar]], [[Huma Qureshi]], [[Raj Babbar]], [[Sushant Singh]], [[Javed Jaffrey]], [[Aditi Rao Hydari]], [[Parvathy Thiruvothu]], [[Konkona Sen Sharma]], [[Nandita Das]], [[Arjun Mathur]] and filmmakers [[Anurag Kashyap]], [[Rakeysh Omprakash Mehra]], and [[Saeed Mirza]] joined in the protest venue.{{cite news|url=https://indianexpress.com/photos/entertainment-gallery/celebrities-join-caa-protest-mumbai-august-kranti-maidan-6175450/|title=Celebrities join CAA protest at Mumbai's August Kranti Maidan|date=19 December 2019|work=The Indian Express|access-date=20 December 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/magazines/panache/caa-protests-b-town-roars-at-demonstrations-farhan-akhtar-bats-for-democracy-stars-thank-mumbai-police-for-cooperation/articleshow/72898868.cms|title=CAA protests: B-town roars at demonstrations, Farhan Akhtar bats for democracy; stars thank Mumbai Police for cooperation|date=20 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=20 December 2019}} Mumbai police had deployed more than 2,500 policemen to monitor and control around 20,000 protesters. The peaceful protest concluded without any violent incidents. Mumbai police was praised by prominent artists including [[Farhan Akhtar]], [[Swara Bhaskar]], [[Kunal Kamra]], politician [[Milind Deora]] and citizens for its professional conduct in maintaining law and order.{{cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/cities/mumbai/mumbai-police-set-an-example-win-hearts/article30352959.ece|title=Anti-CAA protests: Mumbai Police set an example, win hearts|last1=Gajara|first1=Dhairya|date=20 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=20 December 2019}} Protests were held in other cities in the state such as [[Pune]], [[Nashik]], [[Malegaon]], [[Kolhapur]] and [[Nagpur]].{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/mumbai/anti-caa-protests-in-mumbais-august-kranti-maidan-nagpur/articleshow/72888618.cms|title=Anti-CAA protests in Mumbai's August Kranti Maidan, Nagpur|date=19 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=23 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/pune/anti-caa-protests-swell-as-citizens-join-hands-with-students-in-cities/articleshow/72892918.cms|title=Maharashtra: Anti-CAA protests swell as citizens join hands with students in cities|date=20 December 2019|first1=Ardhra|last1=Nair|website=The Times of India|access-date=25 December 2019}} On 28 December, on the occasion of the foundation day of the party, the state unit of the Congress party in Mumbai undertook a flag march and used the slogan "Save Bharat-Save Constitution". The march started from the August Kranti Maidan and ended at the statue of [[Lokmanya Tilak]] near Girgaum Chowpatty. It was attended by office bearers and workers of the party.{{cite news |title=CAA Protests LIVE Updates: Spirit of Assam Accord should not be broken, says Rahul Gandhi in Guwahati |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/india/citizenship-amendment-act-caa-protest-live-updates-rahul-gandhi-uttar-pradesh-assam-6188619/ |access-date=28 December 2019 |work=The Indian Express |date=28 December 2019}} Similar marches were held in many parts of the country.{{cite news |title=Congress Marks 135th Foundation Day with 'Save Constitution-Save India' Flag March Across Country |url=https://www.news18.com/news/india/congress-marks-135th-foundation-day-with-save-constitution-save-india-flag-march-across-country-2438145.html |access-date=28 December 2019 |work=News18 |date=28 December 2019}} On the midnight of 6 January, students of [[IIT, Bombay|IIT Bombay]] began a protest called "Occupy [[Gateway of India|Gateway]]" in Mumbai in response to the [[2020 JNU Attack|attack on Jawaharlal Nehru University campus]]. They were joined in by hundreds of protesters, including activists [[Feroze Mithiborwala]], [[Umar Khalid]] and comedian Kunal Kamra.{{Cite web|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/cities/mumbai/jnu-attack-iit-bombay-solidarity-with-jnu-gateway-of-india-protest-violence-6203275/|title=How 'Occupy Gateway' took shape through the night: Cajon, songs, poetry|date=7 January 2020|website=The Indian Express|access-date=14 January 2020}}{{Cite web|url=https://scroll.in/latest/948883/jnu-violence-students-in-kolkata-maharashtra-hyderabad-protest-in-solidarity|title=JNU violence: Students gather at Gateway of India overnight; protests also held in Kolkata, Kerala|website=Scroll.in|date=6 January 2020 |access-date=14 January 2020}} On 12 January, thousands of people joined the anti-CAA protests at Jogeshwari in Mumbai. Slogans such as "I Am From Gujarat, My Documents Burned in 2002", "No CAA, Boycott NRC, Stop Dividing India, Don't Divide us", "Save Constitution", were displayed on the banners. Large number of policemen were also deputed for security. Inspired by the Shaheen Bagh protest, a massive anti-CAA-NRC-NPR protest started in Mumbai on 17 January. Around 10,000 women gathered at the YMCA Ground in Mumbai to protest in the evening. The protest was organised by a NGO named Mumbai Citizen Quorum. A statewide shutdown, "Maharashtra Bandh" was organised by Vanchit Bahujan Aghadi (VBA) along with 35 other organisations across [[Maharashtra]] state, to protest against CAA and the economic policies of the BJP-led central government, which according to the organisers "were ruining the country". The activists of VBA forced shopkeepers to shut down their stores. A bus was damaged due to stone pelting. On 6 January, 50,000 women held an "all women's rally" in [[Malegaon]] to protest against the CAA, NRC and NPR. The rally was organised by Dastoor Bachao Committee and Shan-e-Hind, an affiliate of [[Janata Dal (Secular)]].
