On 1 February, thousands gathered for the [[pride parade]], [[Queer Azaadi Mumbai]] at [[August Kranti Maidan]] which joined in slogans against the CAA and NRC.{{Cite web|url=https://indianexpress.com/photos/india-news/thousands-throng-mumbais-azad-maidan-in-solidarity-with-queer-azaadi-march-6246412/|title=Thousands throng Mumbai's Azad Maidan in solidarity with Queer Azadi March|date=1 February 2020|website=The Indian Express}} ====Pune==== {{multiple image | width = 150 | image1 = Shaheen Bagh - Pune Camp 01.jpg | image2 = Shaheen Bagh - Pune Camp 02.jpg | image3 = Shaheen Bagh - Pune Camp 03.jpg | footer = Shaheen Bagh protests against CAA, NRC and NPR in Pune on 22 Jan 2020 }} On 11 January, an all women sit in protest was started outside Konark Mall in Kondhwa, in [[Pune]], organised by Kul Jamaat-e-Tanzeem, an umbrella body of several organisations. the protests started with fewer people participating but the crowd grew steadily and reached around 500–600 protesters as reported on 19 January. According to a protester the movement was to save the constitution and the country. Candle light vigil, human chains and speeches were made as part of the protest. The venue had banners with slogans such as 'Tumhari Lathi Se Tej Hamari Awaaz Hai' and 'Liar Liar Desh on Fire', as well as 'India Needs Education, Jobs, Not CAA, NRC, NPR'.{{cite news |title=Pune 'Shaheen Bagh in Kondhwa' anti-CAA protest enters ninth day |url=https://www.newindianexpress.com/nation/2020/jan/19/pune-shaheen-bagh-in-kondhwa-anti-caa-protest-enters-ninth-day-2091592.html |work=The New Indian Express |date=19 January 2020}} [[Shaheen Bagh protests|Shaheen Bagh-style protests]] against CAA, NPR and NRC were held over several days in [[Pune Camp]].{{cite news |title=Pune ka Shaheen Bagh: Powered by women, protest against CAA reaches Camp |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/cities/pune/citizenship-amendment-act-caa-protests-pune-camp-chhatrapati-shivaji-market-6230936/ |access-date=22 October 2023 |work=The Indian Express |date=23 January 2020 |language=en}} ====Nagpur==== In Nagpur, on 24 January, lawyers, doctors and professors conducted a rally. In the rally a high court judge, Ravi Shakar Bhure said that the judiciary had become helpless as well and termed the actions of the government to be part of a massive conspiracy.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/nagpur/lawyers-professors-join-anti-caa-protest/articleshow/73563507.cms|title=Nagpur: Lawyers, professors join anti-CAA protest {{!}} Nagpur News – Times of India|date=23 January 2020|website=The Times of India}} ===Northeast India=== The Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) implementation has faced significant opposition in Northeast India, especially in Assam. Critics argue the CAA is discriminatory and undermines indigenous rights . The central government's decision to exclude most tribal areas from the CAA has been met with mixed reactions, as regions like Assam still largely fall outside the Sixth Schedule and are thus not exempted .{{Cite web |last=PTI |date=2024-03-13 |title=Anti-CAA stir: Protests continue in NE region |url=https://theprint.in/india/anti-caa-stir-protests-continue-in-ne-region/1999676/ |access-date=2024-06-01 |website=ThePrint |language=en-US}} Assam, due to its porous borders with Bangladesh, has been particularly affected by illegal immigration, which heightens the controversy surrounding the CAA . Existing student bodies, such as the Northeast Student Union (NESU), have been criticized for their inaction on the issue . In response, the Wesean Student Federation(WSF) was formed in March 2024, advocating for international intervention and urging the United Nations to address the CAA's discriminatory aspects .{{Cite web |title=WSF seeks UN's intervention over 'unjust' CAA legislation |url=https://morungexpress.com/wsf-seeks-uns-intervention-over-unjust-caa-legislation |access-date=2024-06-01 |website=MorungExpress}}{{Cite web |title=NSF protests against CAA implementation |url=https://morungexpress.com/nsf-protests-against-caa-implementation |access-date=2024-06-01 |website=MorungExpress}} Calls for excluding the entire Northeast from the CAA's purview have been persistent, reflecting widespread regional discontent . In Assam, only areas under the Sixth Schedule, like the Bodoland Territorial Region, are exempt, leaving much of the state vulnerable to the CAA . This discrepancy has sparked questions about the state's policy consistency, as highlighted by All Assam Students Union Chief Advisor Samujjal Bhattacharya .{{Cite web |last=indiatomorrow |first=admin |date=2024-03-26 |title=Why have the northeastern states and tribal areas of Assam and Tripura excluded from Citizenship Amendment Act 2019? |url=https://indiatomorrow.net/2024/03/26/why-have-the-northeastern-states-and-tribal-areas-of-assam-and-tripura-excluded-from-citizenship-amendment-act-2019/ |access-date=2024-06-01 |website=IndiaTomorrow}} Efforts to grant Scheduled Tribe status to additional Assamese communities could potentially lead to a tribal-majority state fully exempt from the CAA, though this has not yet materialized . Meanwhile, organizations like the Asom Jatiyatabadi Yuba Chatra Parishad (AJYCP) are pushing for the implementation of an [[Inner Line Permit]] system to further protect against illegal immigration .{{Cite web |date=2024-03-15 |title=Decode Politics: Why are anti-CAA protests confined to Assam, Tripura in Northeast |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/political-pulse/anti-caa-protest-assam-tripura-northeast-9215288/ |access-date=2024-06-01 |website=The Indian Express |language=en}} ===Other states and union territories=== * '''Meghalaya:''' On 9 December, large scale protests were held in the state. Vehicles and buildings were vandalised in [[Shillong]]. Curfew and internet restrictions were imposed in several parts of the state.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/cab-protests-curfew-imposed-in-parts-of-shillong-relaxed-for-12-hours-1628017-2019-12-13|title=CAB protests: Curfew imposed in parts of Shillong relaxed for 12 hours|date=13 December 2019|website=India Today}} On 20 December, the internet restrictions were lifted after an order from [[Guwahati High Court]] while night curfews remained in place.{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/internet-restored-in-meghalaya-days-after-protest-against-citizenship-amendment-act-2152250|title=Internet Restored In Meghalaya Days After Citizenship Law Protest|last1=Choudhury|first1=Ratnadip|last2=Achorn|first2=Debanish|date=21 December 2019|website=NDTV.com}} The agitations continued in the state with the capital of Shillong being primarily affected. The agitations were led by student organisations with regular demonstrations taking place in universities like North East Hill University.{{Cite web|url=https://www.theweek.in/news/india/2020/01/04/what-meghalaya-wants-how-caa-protests-assume-a-different-meaning-in-the-state.html|title=What Meghalaya wants: How CAA protests assume a different meaning in the state|last1=Prejomon|first1=Sunny|date=4 January 2020|website=The Week}} * '''Goa''': On 13 December, [[Goa Forward Party]] chief, [[Vijai Sardesai]], who dubbed the bill as "communal ammunition bill", took part in the protests in [[Margao]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/goa-gfp-joins-in-protests-against-amended-citizenship-act-1628158-2019-12-13|title=Goa: GFP joins in protests against amended Citizenship Act|date=13 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=26 December 2019}} On 22 February, over 15,000 people gathered at a protest in the capital city [[Panaji]], organised by the "Goa Alliance Against CAA-NRC-NPR" with support from the Goa Church.{{Cite news|url=https://www.business-standard.com/article/pti-stories/goa-anti-caa-protesters-resolve-to-oppose-npr-census-2021-120022101221_1.html|title=Goa anti-CAA protesters resolve to oppose NPR, Census 2021|date=21 February 2020|work=Business Standard India|access-date=27 February 2020}} * '''Chhattisgarh:''' On 15 December, a protest march was held under the banner of the "Anti-CAB Agitation Forum" from Marine Drive area to Ambedkar Chowk in Raipur in the evening.{{Cite news|url=https://www.business-standard.com/article/pti-stories/protest-march-held-in-raipur-against-caa-nrc-119121500896_1.html|title=Protest march held in Raipur against CAA, NRC|agency=Press Trust of India|date=15 December 2019|work=Business Standard India|access-date=27 December 2019}} * '''Odisha:''' On 16 December, thousands of protesters, including farmer leaders, student activists, [[Dalit]] leaders and people with disability, hit the road in Odisha against the CAA, NRC and the attack on Jamia students. They marched from Satyanagar Mosque towards the residence of Chief Minister [[Naveen Patnaik]] in [[Bhubaneswar]]. They also submitted a memorandum to the Chief Minister and Governor [[Ganeshi Lal]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/other-states/dont-implement-caa-in-odisha/article30333963.ece|title='Don't implement CAA in Odisha'|date=18 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=18 December 2019|issn=0971-751X}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/odisha-thousands-hit-road-against-caa-nrc-1629073-2019-12-17|title=Odisha: Thousands hit road against CAA, NRC|date=17 December 2019|first1=Mohammad|last1=Suffian|website=India Today|access-date=25 December 2019}} On 30 January, 25,000 protesters marched through central Bhubaneswar to protest against the CAA.{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/rights/bhubaneswar-anti-caa-protest|title=On Gandhi's Death Anniversary, Bhubaneswar Witnesses its Biggest Anti-CAA Protest|website=The Wire|access-date=19 February 2020}} * '''Uttarakhand:''' On 16 December, protest marches were organised in [[Dehradun district|Dehradun]], [[Kashipur, Uttarakhand|Kashipur]], [[Haridwar district|Haridwar]], [[Nainital district|Nainital]] and [[Udham Singh Nagar district|Udham Singh Nagar]], [[Haldwani]], districts opposing the law terming it discriminatory and unconstitutional.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/dehradun/protest-against-caa-nrc-reaches-hills/articleshow/72944969.cms|title=Protest against CAA, NRC reaches Uttarakhand|date=24 December 2019|first1=Prashant|last1=Jha|website=The Times of India|access-date=26 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/dehradun/hundreds-hold-peaceful-march-against-caa-nrc-in-nainital-protesters-sing-national-anthem-and-read-preamble-to-show-solidarity/articleshow/72960127.cms|title=Hundreds hold peaceful march against CAA, NRC in Nainital; protesters sing national anthem and read preamble to show solidarity|date=25 December 2019|first1=Prashant|last1=Jha|website=The Times of India|access-date=26 December 2019}} * '''Chandigarh:''' On 19 December, over 700 students from Punjab University accompanied by students, activists and residents of surrounding districts like [[Ambala district|Ambala]] and [[Patiala district|Patiala]] conducted a protest march in city.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/chandigarh/march-of-solidarity-against-caa-from-pu-to-sec-17/articleshow/72892680.cms|title=Chandigarh: March of solidarity against CAA, from Punjab University to Sector 17|date=20 December 2019|first1=Arvind|last1=Bishnoi|website=The Times of India|access-date=14 January 2020}} * '''Manipur:''' On 19 December, women vendors of [[Ima Market]] held a silent protest by closing down all their shops. Students also held protest rallies where a few minor scuffles broke out between them and security forces.{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/north-east/protests-rock-manipur-hubs/cid/1720220|title=Protests rock Manipur hubs|last1=Sapam|first1=Robert|date=19 December 2019|website=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota}} * '''Madhya Pradesh:''' On 20 December, protests reportedly turned violent in [[Jabalpur]], leading to a curfew being imposed in four police station limits.{{cite news |title=Protest turns violent in Jabalpur, curfew in four police stations |url=https://www.freepressjournal.in/bhopal/protest-turns-violent-in-jabalpur-curfew-in-four-police-stations |access-date=21 December 2019 |work=Latest Indian news, Top Breaking headlines, Today Headlines, Top Stories at Free Press Journal |date=21 December 2019 }} * '''Rajasthan:''' On 22 December, Chief Minister [[Ashok Gehlot]] led a protest march of nearly 300,000 people christened "''[[Constitution of India|Samvidhan]] Bachao rally''" against CAA. The protestors stated that CAA was against the constitution and is an attempt to divide the people based on their religion. They demanded that the new law be repealed. The protest march was supported by several political parties including Congress, [[Aam Aadmi Party]], [[Rashtriya Lok Dal]] and [[Janata Dal (Secular)]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.news18.com/news/politics/as-politics-heats-up-on-citizenship-act-ashok-gehlot-claims-pm-modi-is-misleading-country-in-his-name-2432729.html|title=As Politics Heats Up on Citizenship Act, Ashok Gehlot Claims PM Modi is 'Misleading Country' in His Name|website=News18|date=22 December 2019|access-date=22 December 2019}} On 24 December 200,000 people attended a protest in [[Kota, Rajasthan|Kota]].{{Cite web|url=http://epaper.patrika.com/m5/2479884/Rajasthan-Patrika-Kota/Kota-City-Patrika-Epaper#page/6/1|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200301232706/http://epaper.patrika.com/m5/2479884/Rajasthan-Patrika-Kota/Kota-City-Patrika-Epaper#page/6/1|url-status=dead|archive-date=1 March 2020|title=Kota, Kota City Patrika Epaper : readwhere|website=epaper.patrika.com|access-date=25 December 2019}} On 25 January, Rajasthan Assembly, with Congress in the majority, passed a resolution asking the central government to repeal CAA and also objected against the NPR and NRC. ===Overseas=== [[File:CAA-NRC_protest_at_Mahatma_Gandhi_Statue_Washington_DC.jpg|thumb|Protest at the [[Mahatma Gandhi Memorial (Washington, D.C.)|Mahatma Gandhi Memorial]] in [[Washington, D.C.|Washington D.C.]], c. 19 December 2019.]]Various cities around the world, including New York City, [[Washington, D.C.|Washington D. C]].,{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/indian-americans-protest-against-caa-nrc-in-front-of-gandhi-statue-in-washington/article30377334.ece|title=Indian-Americans protest against CAA, NRC in front of Gandhi statue in Washington|date=23 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=24 December 2019|issn=0971-751X}} Paris, Berlin,{{cite web|url=https://www.thequint.com/my-report/citizenship-amendment-act-protest-berlin-germany-photos|title=Anti-CAA Protests Go Global: Chants of 'Azaadi' Echo In Berlin|date=24 December 2019|website=The Quint|access-date=25 December 2019}} [[Geneva]], Barcelona, San Francisco, Tokyo, [[Helsinki]],{{Cite news|url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/indians-finland-raise-slogans-read-preamble-anti-caa-protest-helsinki-114596|title=Indians in Finland raise slogans, read Preamble at anti-CAA protest in Helsinki|date=22 December 2019|work=[[The News Minute]]|access-date=5 January 2020}} and [[Amsterdam]], witnessed protests against the Act and the police brutality faced by Indian protesters.{{cite news|url=https://scroll.in/latest/947405/citizenship-act-indian-diaspora-organises-protests-at-several-universities-in-us-uk-france|title=Citizenship Act: Indian diaspora organises protests at several universities in US, UK, France|date=20 December 2019|work=The Scroll|access-date=28 December 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://thewire.in/rights/caa-protests-worldwide|title=CAA Protests: Indian Students Worldwide Raise Voices in Solidarity|date=23 December 2019|work=The Wire|access-date=28 December 2019}} '''North America''' Protests were held in solidarity with Indian protesters outside the [[Mahatma Gandhi Memorial (Washington, D.C.)|Mahatma Gandhi Memorial]] in [[Washington, D.C.|Washington D. C]]. Protests were also held at [[Harvard University]]{{cite news |title="Violent Suppression": Harvard Students Back Jamia, Aligarh Muslim University Protests |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/citizenship-amendment-act-caa-protests-harvard-university-students-back-jamia-amu-students-2149962 |access-date=17 December 2019 |work=NDTV.com}} and [[Massachusetts Institute of Technology]] in the United States, where the students held demonstrations against the violent police crackdown in Jamia Milia Islamia. Around 100 students and faculty members of [[Columbia University]] tore up copies of the CAA and 150 others marched to the Indian consulate in Chicago to condemn the repressive behaviour of police against the students. On 19 December, around 400 present students along with former students of [[Harvard University]], [[Columbia University]], [[Yale University]], [[Stanford University]], [[Brown University]], [[Johns Hopkins University]], [[Cornell University]], [[Carnegie Mellon University]], [[Purdue University]], [[University of California]] and the [[Massachusetts Institute of Technology]] condemned the arbitrary use of power of policing and asked the [[Minister of Home Affairs (India)|Home Minister of India]], [[Amit Shah]] to curb the brutality.{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/india/us-to-france-to-oxford-cry-against-police-brutality/cid/1727989|title=US to France to Oxford: Cry against police brutality|date=18 December 2019|work=The Telegraph (UK)|access-date=28 December 2019}} On 26 January 2020, the 71st Republic Day of India, protest held in 30 cities of the United States including cities which have Indian consulates like, New York, Chicago, Houston, Atlanta and San Francisco and in front of the Indian Embassy in Washington DC. Various organisations including Indian American Muslim Council (IAMC), Equality Labs, Black Lives Matter (BLM), Jewish Voice for Peace (JVP) and Hindus for Human Rights (HfHR) participated in the protest.{{cite news |title=Indian Americans hold anti-CAA protests in 30 US cities on Republic Day |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/nri-news/indian-amercians-in-30-us-cities-hold-anti-caa-protests-on-republic-day/story-E4lLKA9bvReXqHUEoSKr6K.html |access-date=31 January 2020 |work=Hindustan Times |date=27 January 2020}} '''Europe''' The first anti-CAA protests in Europe were held outside the [[India House, London|High Commission of India]] in London,{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/people-assamese-community-protest-london-citizenship-law-1628530-2019-12-15|title=People of Assamese community protest in London against citizenship law|date=15 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=16 December 2019|agency=Press Trust of India}} on 14 December 2019. On 16 December, students at [[University of Edinburgh]] in the United Kingdom demonstrated their protest against the Act. The university and College Union, Edinburgh had released a statement in solidarity condemning the brutal inflictions exercised by state police on the students of various universities in the country who were protesting against the Act.{{cite web |title=Statement of Solidarity with students in universities across India protesting the Citizenship Amendment Act 2019 (CAA) and the introduction of the National Register of Citizens (NRC). |url=https://ucuedinburgh.wordpress.com/2019/12/18/statement-of-solidarity-with-students-in-universities-across-india-protesting-the-citizenship-amendment-act-2019-caa-and-the-introduction-of-the-national-register-of-citizens-nrc/ |publisher=University and College Union |access-date=28 December 2019|date=18 December 2019 }} On 20 December 2019, students from various universities in the [[Netherlands]] protested against the Act and the [[National Register of Citizens for Assam|National Register of Citizens]] in front of the Indian Embassy at [[The Hague]]. Around 300 students from [[University of Leiden]], [[University of Groningen]], and [[Erasmus University]] assembled in a peaceful protest by reading the [[Preamble to the Constitution of India]] and anti-CAA slogans.{{cite news |title=Students organising anti-CAA protests overseas |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/karnataka/students-organising-anti-caa-protests-overseas/article30363151.ece |access-date=28 December 2019 |work=The Hindu |date=21 December 2019}} Till date, four protests were held in front of the embassy by the Indian diaspora living in the Netherlands.{{cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/international/indians-hold-fourth-anti-caa-protest-in-the-netherlands/article30442376.ece|title=Indians hold fourth anti-CAA protest in the Netherlands|last1=Pragati|first1=K.B|date=31 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=4 January 2020}} Protests were also held in [[Berlin]], Germany and [[Zürich|Zurich]], Switzerland with Berlin even seeing a protest march from the Brandenburg Gate till the Indian embassy.{{Cite web|url= https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XPZXweMDdnc |title=Indian Diaspora Stage Anti CAA-NRC Protest March In Germany |date=22 December 2019 |publisher=SADAA Times |via=YouTube}}{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/from-oxford-to-harvard-universities-abroad-see-protests-against-police-crackdown-in-jamia-amu/articleshow/72839850.cms|title=From Oxford to Harvard, universities abroad see protests against police crackdown in Jamia, AMU|date=17 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=17 December 2019}} On 21 December 2019, around a hundred students and professionals of Indian origin living in [[Munich]], Germany gathered at the memorial to the [[White Rose]] Movement outside the [[Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich]] (LMU) and protested against the CAA, NRC, and the police action against the students of [[Jamia Millia Islamia]] and [[Aligarh Muslim University]].{{cite web |title=Statement about the Munich protest from the local organizers|website=[[Facebook]] |url=https://www.facebook.com/IndiansAgainstFascismMunich/posts/105961954246184|access-date=9 January 2020}}{{Primary source inline|date=May 2020}}{{Better source needed|date=January 2020}} Diverse voices from France too, joined in for anti-CAA, anti-NRC protests. A letter of solidarity with protests in India and condemning the citizenship legislation, signed by hundreds of students and other professionals including eminent writers like [[Shumona Sinha]], [[Amit Chaudhuri]], musicians- [[Jean-Philippe Rykiel]], Prabhu Edouard et al. preceded a planned mass protest gathering.{{cite news|url=https://www.humanite.fr/inde-lettre-de-solidarite-682423|title=Letter of Solidarity 1 from France|date=30 December 2019|work=L'Humanité |access-date=6 January 2020}} On 4 January 2020, peaceful and song-filled protests by students, educationists and others from the Indian and foreign diaspora were held at Parvis du [[Trocadéro, Paris|Trocadéro]] in Paris and also in front of the Indian Embassy, Paris despite facing the irk of some local [[BJP]] supporters.{{cite news|url=https://www.anandabazar.com/international/indian-students-residing-in-france-raised-voice-against-caa-1.1091457|title=Indian Students Residing in France Raised Voice Against CAA|date=6 January 2020|work=Ananadabazar Patrika |access-date=6 January 2020}}{{cite news|url=https://www.humanite.fr/inde-lettre-de-solidarite-682423|title=Letter of Solidarity 2 from France|date=30 December 2019|work=L'Humanité |access-date=6 January 2020}} On 14 January, more than 500 people gathered outside the Indian embassy in London to protest against the CAA.{{cite news |last1=Acharjee|first1=Sonali |title=England, Germany, Switzerland: Anti-CAA protests break barriers, shake the world |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-protest-world-students-international-foreign-modi-india-1637241-2020-01-16 |access-date=16 January 2020 |work=India Today |date=16 January 2020 }} In [[Berlin]], Germany more than 200 people join the protest march against CAA, NRC, [[2020 JNU attack|JNU attack]] on 19 January. The march was led by the Indian diaspora and started at the Brandenburg Gate and ended at the Indian Embassy. On 20 January, several Labour MPs of the UK parliament discussed the concerns on the CAA, in a meeting organised by South Asia Solidarity Group (SASG) and Ambedkar International Mission (UK) in London. The MPs expressed concern on its implications on human rights and disenfranchisement of Muslims. On 25 January, in London, around 2000 of people from the Indian diaspora and the Human Rights organisation, conducted a protest march against CAA from the Downing street to the Indian High Commission.{{cite news |last1=T |first1=Loveena |title=2,000 people protest against CAA, NRC in London on Republic Day eve |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/protest-against-caa-nrc-in-london-on-republic-day-eve-1640343-2020-01-26 |access-date=26 January 2020 |work=India Today |date=26 January 2020 }} The protesters demanded the repeal of the CAA stating that it impacts the secular constitution of India. The protesters carried banners with slogans such as "Protect the Constitution", "Stop Dividing India", "United Against Racism in India" and "No Citizenship on the Basis of Religion". The protester also asked for the abolishing the NPR and NRC which could be used along with the CAA for a mass disenfranchisement of Muslims in India. During the march, MP [[Sam Tarry]], from the UK Opposition Labour Party while addressing the crowd, stated "We are not here as any anti-India demonstration, we are here as a pro-India demonstration. It is incredibly important that our voices are heard against laws that are not good for the future of the country," he added that the human rights in any country is an international issue. Labour MPs [[Stephen Timms]], [[Clive Lewis (politician)|Clive Lewis]] and [[Nadia Whittome]] supported the protest with written messages and asked the UK government to discuss it with the Indian government. Some of the groups backing the march were Indian Workers Association (GB), School of Oriental and African Studies (SOAS) India Society, CasteWatch UK, Tamil People in the UK, Indian Muslim Federation(UK), Federation of Redbridge Muslim Organisations (FORMO), Kashmir Solidarity Movement, South Asian Students Against Fascism. A five-page resolution against the CAA was drafted by more than 150 members of the European Union, who stated that it "marks a dangerous shift in the way citizenship will be determined in India and is set to create the largest statelessness crisis in the world and cause immense human suffering".{{cite news |title="Largest Statelessness Crisis," Predicts Draft Anti-CAA Resolution In European Union |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/largest-statelessness-crisis-predicts-draft-anti-caa-resolution-in-european-union-2169985 |access-date=26 January 2020 |work=NDTV.com |date=26 January 2020}} The members pointed out that the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights has called the CAA 'fundamentally discriminatory', and also accused the Indian government of "discriminating against, harassing and prosecuting national and religious minorities and silencing any opposition, human rights groups... and journalists critical of the government". The members have asked EU to include a "strong human rights clause with an effective implementation and suspension mechanism" while negotiating any trade agreement with India. '''Asia and Oceania''' Several silent protests were held in December by students in Israel.{{Cite web|url=https://twitter.com/PTI_News/status/1210527871069876224?s=19|title=Dozens of Indian students in Israel staged silent protest against CAA, NRC in front of Indian embassy in Tel Aviv: says statement by students|agency=Press Trust of India|date=27 December 2019}}{{Primary source inline|date=May 2020}} On the same day,{{clarify|date=February 2020}} students of [[University of Dhaka]] assembled in solidarity for the student protesters who were beaten by police and also condemned the CAA.{{cite news|url=https://www.huffingtonpost.in/entry/students-bangladesh-protest-with-jamia-millia-islamia-university_in_5dfaf0cfe4b0eb2264d3fdb7|title=Students In Bangladesh Protest In Solidarity With Jamia Millia Islamia And AMU Students|last1=Hasnet|first1=Saif|date=19 December 2019|work=HuffPost|access-date=31 December 2019}} On 22 December, members of the [[Indian Australians|Indian community in Australia]] gathered at the [[Parliament of Victoria]] in [[Melbourne]] in huge numbers and protested against the new Act and police brutality through sloganeering.{{cite news |title=Members of the Indian community staged a protest against the police brutality on protesting students in India and against the Citizenship Amendment Act and the National Register of Citizens on Sunday |url=https://www.sbs.com.au/language/english/audio/new-citizenship-law-indian-community-holds-protest-in-melbourne |access-date=28 December 2019 |work=SBS Hindi |date=25 December 2019}}{{cite news |title=CAA-NRC protest: 'Aazaadi' slogan resonates in Australia too |url=https://www.siasat.com/caa-nrc-protest-aazaadi-slogan-resonates-australia-too-1771899/ |access-date=28 December 2019 |work=The Siasat Daily |date=22 December 2019}} On 24 December, a 32-year-old Indian national protested against the CAA in the district of [[Marina Bay, Singapore|Marina Bay]] in Singapore. The local authorities called it an unauthorised protest on foreign politics.{{cite web|first=Aradhana|last=Aravindan|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-india-citizenship-protests-singapore/singapore-police-probe-indian-for-alleged-modi-citizenship-law-protest-idUSKBN1YU02Q|title=Singapore police probe Indian for alleged Modi citizenship law protest|work=Reuters|date=26 December 2019|access-date=27 December 2019}} '''Africa''' Protests were held outside the Indian Consulate in [[Cape Town]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.aa.com.tr/en/africa/india-s-citizenship-law-sparks-protests-in-south-africa/1698328|title=India's citizenship law sparks protests in South Africa|website=aa.com.tr|access-date=22 January 2020}}{{Citation|title=Protests against CAA laws outside Indian Consulate in Cape Town|url=https://www.newsflare.com/video/332043/protests-against-caa-laws-outside-indian-consulate-in-cape-town|access-date=22 January 2020}} == Methods == The protesters used various methods including [[Demonstration (political)|demonstrations]], [[civil disobedience]], ''[[Dharna]]'', ''[[Gherao]]'', [[hunger strikes]], ''[[Satyagraha]]'', ''[[Hartal]]'', [[vandalism]], ''[[arsons]]'', [[stone pelting]], [[hashtag activism]], [[general strike]] and ''[[Bandh]]'' against the bill. Protesters used several slogans and poems during the protests. Lyricist and Urdu poet [[Rahat Indori]]'s famous ''[[ghazal]]'' ''"Sabhi ka khoon hai shaamil yahan ki mitti mein; kisi ke baap ka Hindustan thodi hai"'' (This land has seen sacrifices from everyone; Hindustan is not anyone's property) resonates throughout the protest.{{cite news|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/india/kisi-ke-baap-ka-hindustan-thodi-hai-a-line-from-three-decades-ago-is-rallying-call-6183588/|title='Kisi ke baap ka Hindustan thodi hai': Rahat Indori's line from three decades ago is rallying call|last1=Onial|first1=Divyani|date=25 December 2019|work=The Indian Express|access-date=29 December 2019}} Revolutionary poems by [[Pash]] like ''"Main Ghas Hoon; Main aapke Kiye Dhare Par Ugh Ayunga"'' (I am grass, I will rise everywhere),{{cite news|url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9AKNWRhR9xc|title=Bollywood actors on NRC protest|date=25 December 2019|via=YouTube|access-date=29 December 2019|publisher=Bahujan TV}} lyricist and writer [[Varun Grover (writer)|Varun Grover]]'s poem like ''"Tanashah aakar jayenge, hum kagaz nahi dikhayenge"'' (Dictators will come and go but we will not show our documents),{{cite news|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/kagaz-nahi-dikhayenge-echoes-through-anti-caa-nrc-protest-in-mumbai-s-azad-maidan-1632085-2019-12-28|title=Kagaz nahi dikhayenge echoes through anti-CAA, NRC protest in Mumbai's Azad Maidan|date=28 December 2019|work=India Today|access-date=29 December 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://www.outlookindia.com/website/story/india-news-kagaaz-nahi-dikhayenge-varun-grovers-anti-nrc-poem-is-viral/344545|title=Kagaz Nahi Dikhayenge': Varun Grover's Anti-NRC Poem Is Viral|date=22 December 2019|work=The Outlook India|access-date=29 December 2019}} ''"Main Inkaar Karta Hoon"'' (I refuse) penned by singer Aamir Aziz{{cite news|url=https://www.theweek.in/news/india/2019/12/22/How-these-poems-have-defined-anti-CAA-protests.html|title=How these poems have defined anti-CAA protests|date=22 December 2019|work=The Week|access-date=29 December 2019}} and the popular poem ''"Main Hindustani Musalmaan Hoon"'' (I am an Indian Muslim) by Indian spoken word poet [[Hussain Haidry]] has been used during the protests.{{cite news|url=https://scroll.in/video/948119/caa-protest-watch-when-hussain-haidrys-hindustani-musalmaan-rang-out-at-mumbais-azaad-maidan|title=CAA protest watch: When Hussain Haidry's 'Hindustani Musalmaan' rang out at Mumbai's Azaad Maidan|date=27 December 2019|work=The Scroll|access-date=29 December 2019}} Along with poems, the famous revolutionary ''"Aazadi!"'' slogan by JNU students has been used extensively throughout the protest in all over the country and abroad.{{cite news|url=https://qz.com/india/1772469/azadi-makes-its-way-from-jnu-to-bollywood-and-caa-nrc-protests/|title=Once "azadi" got students arrested—now all Indians are raising the slogan|last1=Kapoor|first1=Manavi|date=20 December 2019|work=Quartz India|access-date=29 December 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/kanhaiya-kumar-brings-back-azaadi-slogan-to-protest-against-caa-1628773-2019-12-16|title=Gandhi wali azaadi: Kanhaiya Kumar brings back azaadi slogan to protest against Jamia violence|date=16 December 2019|work=India Today|access-date=29 December 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://scroll.in/video/947918/kerala-caa-protests-football-stadium-chants-azaadi-church-choir-sings-in-skullcaps-and-hijabs|title=Kerala CAA protests: Football stadium chants 'Azaadi,' church choir sings in skullcaps and hijabs|date=29 December 2019|work=The Scroll|access-date=29 December 2019}} Slogans, poems and songs used during [[Indian independence movement]] like ''"Inquilab Zindabad"'' (Long live the revolution) coined by Indian freedom fighter [[Hasrat Mohani]], ''"[[Sarfaroshi Ki Tamanna]] aab haamare dill mein hai; Dekhna hai zor kitna baazu-e-qatil mein hai"'' (The desire for revolution is in our hearts; Let's see how much strength the enemy has) written by [[Bismil Azimabadi]] which were later popularised by martyr [[Bhagat Singh]], [[Ashfaqullah Khan]], [[Chandrashekhar Azad]] and [[Ram Prasad Bismil]] were used as main vocal resistance during the protest.{{cite news|url=https://www.news18.com/news/buzz/azadi-sarfaroshi-ki-tamanna-what-the-nationwide-student-protests-in-solidarity-with-jamia-milia-looked-like-2425537.html|title='Azadi', 'Sarfaroshi ki Tamanna': What the Nationwide Student Protests in Solidarity with Jamia Milia Looked Like|last1=Mukherjee|first1=Jashodhara|date=16 December 2019|work=News 18|access-date=29 December 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://www.outlookindia.com/newsscroll/religion-as-criterion-for-citizenship-wrong-says-a-us-citizen-at-delhi-gate-protest-against-caa/1690873|title=Religion as criterion for citizenship wrong, says a US citizen at Delhi Gate protest against CAA|date=20 December 2019|work=India Today|access-date=29 December 2019}} Protesters used the poetry written by revolutionary poets such as [[Faiz Ahmad Faiz]] and [[Habib Jalib]], both considered symbols of resistance against military dictatorships and state oppression in Pakistan. Poems such as ''"[[Hum Dekhenge]]"'' (We will witness) penned by Faiz and ''"Main nahin janta, main nahin manta"'' (I refuse to acknowledge, I refuse to accept) penned by Jalib inspired large scale protests in form of banners and recitations. ''"[[Saare Jahan Se Achcha]] Hindustan Haamara"'' (Better than the entire world, is our Hindustan) by Urdu poet [[Muhammad Iqbal]] was recited in the protests.{{Cite web|url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2019/12/28/poets-celebrated-in-pakistan-inspire-indias-protesters|title=Poets celebrated in Pakistan inspire India's protesters|publisher=Al Jazeera}} Singer and lyricist Aamir Aziz wrote ''Main Inkaar Karta Hoon'' as a Hindustani protest poem. Aziz's work was [[political poetry]] which was used during the protests and also to protest against the police brutality on the student protesters who were demonstrating against the Act.{{cite news |last1=Katariya |first1=Meenu |title=This Powerful Poem From Anti-CAA Protests Hints At The Revolution The Indian Youth Is Capable Of |url=https://www.scoopwhoop.com/news/powerful-poem-from-anti-caa-protests/?ref=social&type=fb&b=0 |access-date=29 December 2019 |work=Scoop Whoop |date=23 December 2019}}{{cite news |last1=Bhuyan |first1=Avantika |title=The fine art of staging a protest |url=https://www.livemint.com/mint-lounge/features/the-fine-art-of-staging-a-protest-11577441051083.html |access-date=29 December 2019 |work=The Live Mint |date=27 December 2019}}{{cite news |title=The smiles amidst the violence |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/cities/mumbai/the-smiles-amidst-the-violence/article30375580.ece |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191222211622/https://www.thehindu.com/news/cities/mumbai/the-smiles-amidst-the-violence/article30375580.ece |url-status=dead |archive-date=22 December 2019 |access-date=29 December 2019 |work=The Hindu |date=23 December 2019}}{{cite news |title=Political Landscape |url=https://www.pressreader.com/india/the-times-of-india-mumbai-edition/20191229/282136408326429 |access-date=29 December 2019 |work=The Times of India |publisher=Press Reader |date=29 December 2019}} Along with Hindi and Urdu literature, English poems and slogans were also used for the protests. Among those one such [[dramatic monologue]] by a teacher named Ajmal Khan, "Write me down!, I am an Indian; This is my land, If I have born here, I will die here; There for, Write it down! Clearly In bold and capital letters, On the top of your NRC, that I am an Indian!",{{cite news |title=Write me down, I am an Indian, my name is Ajmal: A poem of protest for these troubled times |url=https://www.firstpost.com/india/write-me-down-i-am-an-indian-my-name-is-ajmal-a-poem-of-protest-for-troubled-times-7792801.html |work=The First Post|access-date=30 December 2019 |date=18 December 2019}} improvised from [[Mahmoud Darwish]]'s song [[Mahmoud Darwish#2016 poem controversy|Write Down!]], questions the Act's secularism and the Government. The poem strongly urged not to question the identity and patriotism of the Indian Muslims, the tribals, the poor, the landless, the Dalits and questioned the various actions of [[Right-wing politics|right wing organisations]] in the country.{{cite news |title=CAA protests: The poems, songs and slogans that marked citizens' dissent across the country |url=https://www.firstpost.com/living/caa-protests-the-poems-songs-and-slogans-that-marked-citizens-dissent-across-the-country-7813201.html |access-date=30 December 2019 |work=The First Post |date=22 December 2019}} [[Pink Floyd]] co-founder and musician [[Roger Waters]] recites "Everything will be remembered, Everything recorded", an English Transliteration of Aamir Aziz's poem ''"Sab Yaad Rakha Jayega"'' at a protest in London.{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/world/roger-waters-recites-aamir-azizs-poem-says-caa-a-fascist-racist-law/cid/1749231|title=Roger Waters recites Aamir Aziz's poem, says CAA a fascist, racist law|website=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota|access-date=3 March 2020}} [[Kannada literature|Kannada]] poem were also recited to protest. Siraj Bisaralli, a Kannada poet, recited his own poem ''"Ninna Dakhale Yaavaga Needuttee?"'' (When will you show your documents?) at a cultural festival and got arrested.{{cite news |title=Karnataka: Poet booked for reciting Anti-CAA poem |url=https://gaurilankeshnews.com/karnataka-poet-booked-for-reciting-anti-caa-poem/ |access-date=4 March 2020 |work=Gauri Lankesh News |date=29 February 2020}}{{cite news |title=Watch: Former Karnataka CM Kumaraswamy reads out poem, which led to arrest of poet Bisarali yesterday |url=https://www.msn.com/en-in/news/newsindia/watch-former-karnataka-cm-kumaraswamy-reads-out-poem-which-led-to-arrest-of-poet-bisarali-yesterday/ar-BB109Pya |access-date=4 March 2020 |publisher=[[MSN]] |date=19 February 2020}} English placards like "[[Narendra Modi|Modi]]-[[Amit Shah|Shah]], You Gave Me Depression", "Rise Against Fascism", "Don't Be Dead Inside For Democracy", "Fascism, Down Down", "I'll show you my documents, if you show your degree!" were used against the CAA-NRC and the Government, and placards like "Delhi Police: Shanti (Peace), Sewa (Service), Nyaya (Justice)?" and "Make Tea Not War" questions the Delhi Police's brutal actions against student protesters.{{cite news |title=10 Posters At The CAA Protests In Delhi That Scream Resistance|last1=Roy |first1=Pragya |url=https://feminisminindia.com/2019/12/20/posters-caa-protests-delhi-screams-resistance/ |access-date=30 December 2019 |work=Feminism in India (FII) |date=20 December 2019}} === Art === [[File:No NPR CAA NRC graffiti public toilet Shaheen Bagh protests New Delhi 8 Jan 2020.jpg|thumb|right|200px|alt=No NPR CAA NRC graffiti in spray paint|No NPR CAA NRC graffiti on a public toilet at Shaheen Bagh protests, New Delhi, 8 January 2020]]Various artists created comics, illustrations, and posters against the CAA and NRC.{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/society/how-art-on-social-media-became-the-face-of-anti-caa-protests/article30379272.ece|title=How art on social media became the face of anti-CAA protests|last1=Majumdar|first1=Meghna|date=23 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=30 December 2019|issn=0971-751X}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.firstpost.com/india/amid-anti-caa-nrc-protests-artists-in-india-are-creating-and-sharing-a-portrait-of-dissent-7832351.html|title=Amid anti-CAA, NRC protests, artists in India are creating — and sharing — a portrait of dissent|last1=Khemani|first1=Tanvi|date=28 December 2019|website=Firstpost|access-date=30 December 2019}} Several [[Editorial cartoonist|political cartoonists]] created pieces covering the protests.{{Cite web|url=https://scroll.in/article/947381/one-nation-versus-one-forty-four-indian-cartoonists-on-citizenship-act-protests|title='One nation' versus 'One forty four': Indian cartoonists on Citizenship Act protests|website=Scroll.in|date=20 December 2019 |access-date=30 December 2019}} A group of activists in Chennai drew ''[[kolam]]s'' (geometrical patterns drawn on the ground with rice flour or chalk) criticising the CAA and NRC. This novel protest was then officially supported by the opposition DMK party. Following the ''kolam'' protests in Tamil Nadu, anti-CAA protestors in West Bengal also started drawing ''kolams'', known as ''alpana'' or ''[[rangoli]]'' in Bengal. ===Government=== The government used various methods to stop the protests, including [[mass shooting]] by [[riot police]],{{cite news |last1=Husain |title=Lucknow: Nadwa students protest in solidarity with Jamia and AMU, college shut {{!}} Lucknow News – Times of India |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/lucknow/lucknow-nadwa-students-protest-in-solidarity-with-jamia-and-amu-college-shut/articleshow/72757850.cms |access-date=11 January 2020 |work=The Times of India |agency=TNN |date=16 December 2019 }} [[Baton charge|''lathi charge'']], [[mass arrest]], [[Internet shutdown]], [[curfew]], transport restrictions, [[water cannon]], and imposing ban on assembly ([[Section 144]]).{{cite news |title=CAA protests: Video shows police firing at protesters, contrary to UP DGP claims |url=https://gulfnews.com/world/asia/india/caa-protests-video-shows-police-firing-at-protesters-contrary-to-up-dgp-claims-1.1577078324096 |access-date=11 January 2020 |work=Gulf News |date=23 December 2019 }}{{cite news |title=CAA: UP Police Shot And Killed Muslims Who Weren't Even Protesting, Says Activist Kavita Krishnan |url=https://www.huffingtonpost.in/entry/uttar-pradesh-police-muslims-killed-caa-protests-kavita-krishnan_in_5e044bdce4b05b08babc8cc7 |access-date=11 January 2020 |work=HuffPost India |date=26 December 2019 }} == Participants == The law was considered controversial since the time it was proposed, leading to protests from students, political organisations and citizen groups. On 4 December, the draft legislation was shared and the student organisation,  [[All Assam Students' Union]] (AASU) objected to the proposal. AASU had participated in the [[Assam Movement]] in the 1970s and 1980s against the illegal immigration of Bangladeshis.{{Cite web|url=https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/india/in-depth-dislodging-govts-shaking-foundations-and-offering-resistance-a-short-history-of-indias-student-protests-4746571.html|title=In-Depth {{!}} Dislodging govts, shaking foundations and offering resistance — a short history of India's student protests|website=Moneycontrol|date=20 December 2019 |access-date=20 December 2019}} The protests started in Assam in early December. By 12 December, students in at least 50 colleges and universities nationwide had joined the protests.{{Cite magazine|url=https://time.com/5752186/india-protests-citizenship-act/|title=How a New Law in India Sparked Mass Protests and a Brutal Police Clampdown|magazine=Time|access-date=20 December 2019}} The student protests subsequently grew and spread all over the country and several political and citizen groups joined it. The protests came into national and international prominence after the police crackdown following the violent crackdown of protests in Aligarh Muslim University{{Cite news |title=Police stormed a university in India. Muslim students say the violence was an act of revenge. |language=en-US |newspaper=Washington Post |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/asia_pacific/police-stormed-a-university-in-india-muslim-students-say-the-violence-was-an-act-of-revenge/2020/02/16/6727aa24-37e8-11ea-a1ff-c48c1d59a4a1_story.html |access-date=2023-02-26 |issn=0190-8286}}{{Cite news |author= |date=2019-12-15 |title=At least 60 injured in police crackdown at Aligarh Muslim University |language=en-IN |work=The Hindu |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/students-injured-in-police-crackdown-at-aligarh-muslim-university/article30313968.ece |access-date=2023-02-26 |issn=0971-751X}} and Jamia Millia Islamia.{{Cite web |title=80 students undergo treatment at Delhi hospital after violence at Jamia |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/jamia-protests-anti-citizenship-amendment-act-caa-injured-hospital-1629100-2019-12-17 |access-date=2023-02-26 |website=India Today |date=17 December 2019 |language=en}}{{Cite news |date=2019-12-16 |title=Protests erupt across India over CAA, police action against Jamia students |work=The Economic Times |issn=0013-0389}} Student leaders from Jamia Millia Islamia Aysha Renna{{Cite web |title=Our Leaders - Fraternity Movement |url=https://fraternityindia.org/our-leaders |access-date=2023-02-26 |website=fraternityindia.org |language=en}} and Ladeeda Farzana who were brutally attacked became the prominent faces of the movement.{{Cite web |last=Gupta |first=Helen Regan,Swati |date=2019-12-18 |title=The women at the center of viral video say India will not be divided |url=https://www.cnn.com/2019/12/18/asia/india-protests-muslim-woman-viral-video-intl-hnk/index.html |access-date=2023-02-26 |website=CNN |language=en}}{{Cite web |title='Face' of Jamia protest uses RSS leader's book to take jibe at SC |url=https://www.theweek.in/news/india/2020/01/22/face-of-jamia-protest-uses-rss-leaders-book-to-take-jibe-at-sc.html |access-date=2023-02-26 |website=The Week |language=en}}{{Cite web | date=2021-01-31 |title=Aysha Renna: Protest against Casteism and Islamophobia must continue |url=https://maktoobmedia.com/2021/01/31/aysha-renna-protest-against-casteism-islamophobia-must-continue/
