web|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/caa-not-acceptable-in-northeast-say-student-bodies-to-hold-protest-against-jnu-violence/story-xQlTDJbqphaX8u6WTAEuiI.html|title='CAA not acceptable in Northeast', say student bodies; to hold protest against JNU violence|date=7 January 2020|website=Hindustan Times|access-date=13 January 2020}} * [[Naga Students' Federation]] * All Bodo Students Association *Wesean Student Federation * [[Twipra Students Federation]] * [[All India Sikh Students Federation]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/chandigarh/caa-nrc-row-ex-jnu-leader-umar-khalid-to-address-rally-in-malerkotla-today/story-es2XcDVOx37r4txQUFD2WI.html|title=CAA, NRC ROW: Ex-JNU leader Umar Khalid to address rally in Malerkotla today|date=2 January 2020|website=Hindustan Times|access-date=13 January 2020}} * [[All Idu Mishmi Students Union]]{{Cite web|url=https://arunachaltimes.in/index.php/2019/12/15/anti-ca-protest-march-taken-out/ |title=Anti-CA protest march taken out |publisher=The Arunachal Times |author=Karyir Riba |date=15 December 2019 |access-date=14 January 2020}} * [[All Tai Ahom Students Union|All Tai Ahom Students’ Union]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/north-east/hunger-strike-on-new-years-day-in-assam/cid/1732145|title=Hunger strike on New Year's Day in Assam|website=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota|access-date=14 January 2020}} ==== Pro-government ==== * [[Bharatiya Janata Yuva Morcha]] (student wing of the [[Bharatiya Janata Party]]){{Cite web |date=2020-01-13 |title=Odisha: BJYM members in Bhubaneswar form human chain in support of CAA |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/odisha-bjym-members-in-bhubaneswar-form-human-chain-in-support-of-caa-1636552-2020-01-13 |access-date=2024-10-12 |website=India Today |language=en}} == Casualties == As of 4 January, 21 people were killed by police firing guns during the CAA protests in Uttar Pradesh,{{cite news |title=UP Police Say 57 Cops Had Bullet Injuries In Clashes. We Found Only One |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/citizenship-amendment-act-up-police-say-57-cops-had-bullet-injuries-in-clashes-but-only-one-found-2158556 |access-date=4 January 2020 |work=NDTV.com |date=4 January 2020}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/caa-protests-uttar-pradesh-numbers-1631855-2019-12-27|title=Uttar Pradesh CAA protests in numbers: 19 dead, over 1,100 arrested for violence|date=27 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=27 December 2019}}{{cite news |title=Death toll touches 16 in U.P. as protests continue |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/anti-caa-protests-toll-rises-to-15-in-uttar-pradesh-vandalism-during-bihar-bandh/article30368185.ece |access-date=21 December 2019 |work=The Hindu |date=21 December 2019 }} 3 killed in Karnataka and 5 in Assam. Overall, 27 people were killed in the whole of India.{{cite news |title=Internet banned in India's Uttar Pradesh amid anger over killings |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2019/12/internet-banned-india-uttar-pradesh-anger-killings-191227060434667.html |access-date=30 December 2019 |publisher=Al Jazeera}}{{cite news |title=These Are the 25 People Killed During Anti-Citizenship Amendment Act Protests |url=https://thewire.in/rights/anti-caa-protest-deaths |access-date=29 December 2019 |work=The Wire}} With 19 killed and 1,246 people arrested based on 372 FIRs lodged in the state, UP was the most affected state with the biggest police crackdown in India. * '''12 December''' [[File:Name of those killed in anti-caa protests on an India Gate artwork at Shaheen Bagh 11 Jan 2020.jpg|thumb|Names of those killed in anti-caa protests on a miniature India Gate artwork at Shaheen Bagh on 11 January 2020.]] :* Dipanjal Das (23), Sam Stafford (17), Abdul Alim (23), Ishwar Nayak (25), Azizul Haque (45) and Dwijendra Panging (35) in [[Assam]].{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/rights/assam-anti-citizenship-amendment-act-protest-deaths|title=How Five People in Assam Were Killed During Anti-Citizenship Amendment Protests|website=The Wire|access-date=19 December 2019}} * '''19 December''' :* Nausheen Bengre (23) and Jaleel Kudroli (49) in Mangalore, [[Karnataka]]{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/anti-caa-protests-deaths-mangaluru-police-firing-1629836-2019-12-19|title=Anti-CAA protests: 2 protesters killed in police firing in Mangaluru|first=Nolan|last=Pinto|date=19 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=19 December 2019}} * '''20 December''' :* Mohammad Sageer (8), in Varanasi Uttar Pradesh, was killed in a stampede of people fleeing police [[lathi charge]] following CAA protests after Friday prayers.{{Cite web|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/opinion/columns/caa-protest-death-youngest-sageer-ahmad-two-bicycles-a-broken-dream-6236865/|title=Why 8-year-old Sageer Ahmad's death should make us rethink our fractured world order|date=27 January 2020|website=The Indian Express|access-date=1 February 2020}} :* Mohammad Wakeel (25) in Uttar Pradesh. :*Aftab Alam (22) and Mohammad Saif (25) in Kanpur, Uttar Pradesh :*Asif (20), Arif (25), Zaheer (40), and Moshin (25) from Meerut, Uttar Pradesh{{cite news |title=6 killed as U.P. protests against citizenship law turn violent |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/6-killed-as-up-protests-against-citizenship-law-turn-violent/article30361932.ece |access-date=20 December 2019 |work=The Hindu |date=20 December 2019 }} :* Nabi Jahan (24) in Firozabad, Uttar Pradesh :* Faiz Khan (24) in Rampur, Uttar Pradesh :* Anas (22) and Sulaiman (26) from Nehtaur area, [[Bijnor]], Uttar Pradesh :* Noor-e-Alam in Muzaffarnagar, Uttar Pradesh * '''21 December''' :* Mohammad Bilal (27) and Shehroz (22) in Uttar Pradesh.{{cite news |author=Scroll Staff |title=Citizenship Act protests: One person killed in Rampur in fresh violence, toll in UP rises to 16 |url=https://scroll.in/latest/947486/caa-protests-seven-killed-in-uttar-pradesh-chandrashekhar-azad-surrenders-in-delhi |access-date=21 December 2019 |work=Scroll.in |date=21 December 2019}} * == Impact == As the ongoing protest against the Citizenship Act turned violent, authorities of [[Gauhati University]], [[Dibrugarh University]] and [[Cotton University]] postponed all semester exams scheduled up-to 16 December 2019.{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/north-east/all-university-exams-postponed-till-december-16/cid/1726020|title=All University exams postponed till December 16|website=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota|access-date=22 December 2019}} No play was possible on the fourth day of the [[cricket]] match between [[Assam cricket team|Assam]] and [[Services cricket team|Services]] in the [[2019–20 Ranji Trophy]] because of the protests.{{cite web|url=https://sportstar.thehindu.com/cricket/ranji-trophy-2019-20-day-four-games-in-assam-and-tripura-suspended-due-to-curfew-citizenship-amendment-bill/article30284054.ece|title=Ranji Trophy 2019–20: Day four game in Assam suspended due to curfew over CAB|work=Sport Star|date=12 December 2019 |access-date=12 December 2019}} [[Board of Cricket Control in India|BCCI]] shifted two fixtures featuring three northeastern teams to other venues.{{Cite news|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/india-domestic/ranji-trophy/bcci-shifts-two-ranji-trophy-ties-out-of-northeast/articleshow/72815663.cms|title=BCCI shifts two Ranji Trophy ties out of Northeast – Times of India|website=The Times of India|date=17 December 2019 |access-date=17 December 2019}} The protests also affected the football matches of [[NorthEast United FC|NorthEast United]], with their fixture against [[Chennaiyin FC|Chennaiyin]] getting postponed.{{Cite web|url=https://www.news18.com/news/football/isl-2019-20-northeast-united-fc-vs-chennaiyin-fc-in-guwahati-postponed-due-to-cab-protests-2421245.html|title=ISL 2019–20: NorthEast United FC vs Chennaiyin FC in Guwahati Postponed Due to CAB Protests|website=News18|date=12 December 2019|access-date=17 December 2019}} The India-Japan summit in Guwahati, which was supposed to be attended by Japanese Prime Minister [[Shinzō Abe|Shinzo Abe]] was also cancelled.{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/international/india-japan-guwahati-summit-cancelled/article30294202.ece|title=India-Japan Guwahati summit cancelled in view of protests|last1=Bhattacherjee|first1=Kallol|date=13 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=13 December 2019|issn=0971-751X}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.saudigazette.com.sa/article/584894/World/Asia/India-protests-spread-over-anti-Muslim-law|title=India protests spread over 'anti-Muslim' law|date=13 December 2019|website=Saudigazetteglish|access-date=13 December 2019}} ===Economy=== ''[[The Indian Express]]'' reported that, during the second half of December, there had been a decline in the sales of cars, watches and other consumer goods, due to the ongoing protests.{{cite news |title=From cars to watches, protests hit India Inc: 'Decline in customers visiting showrooms' |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/india/citizenship-amendment-act-caa-protests-india-inc-decline-in-customers-6213438/ |access-date=13 January 2020 |work=The Indian Express |date=13 January 2020}} ===Transport=== Several trains and at least 700 flights were delayed and more than 20 cancelled as a result of the protests.{{Cite web|url=https://www.deccanchronicle.com/nation/current-affairs/121219/citizenship-bill-protests-affect-assam-flights-suspended-train-servi.html|title=Citizenship Bill protests affect Assam; flights suspended, train services hit|date=12 December 2019|website=[[Deccan Chronicle]]|access-date=12 December 2019}}{{cite news |author= |title=Anti-CAA protests: 700 flights delayed, 20 cancelled at Delhi's IGI Airport |url= https://www.thehindu.com/news/cities/Delhi/anti-caa-protests-700-flights-delayed-20-cancelled-at-delhis-igi-airport/article30347949.ece|access-date=20 December 2019 |work=The Hindu |date=19 December 2019 }} Train services were completely suspended in parts of Assam after two railway stations in the state were set on fire. It was reported that the [[Indian Railways]] suffered losses worth {{INRConvert|90|c|lk=on}} in property damage due to the protests, including losses worth over {{INRConvert|72|c}} in West Bengal alone.{{Cite web|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/railways-property-worth-rs-90-crore-damaged-in-anti-caa-protests/story-HkrDOg4o5ZUQYAkLvj7KfN.html|title=Railways property worth Rs 90 crore damaged in anti-CAA protests|website=Hindustan Times|access-date=27 December 2019|date=20 December 2019}} On 20 December, the [[Kerala State Road Transport Corporation]] suspended all bus services to Mangalore, while many [[Karnataka State Road Transport Corporation]] buses were blocked in Kerala.{{Cite web | url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/mangaluru-firing-north-kerala-put-on-high-alert-1630039-2019-12-20 | title=Mangaluru firing: North Kerala put on high alert| date=20 December 2019}} On 19 and 20 December, several stations of the [[Delhi Metro]] were closed as a precautionary measure.{{Cite news|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/anti-caa-protest-gates-of-seven-delhi-metro-stations-closed/articleshow/72880035.cms|title=Delhi anti-CAA protests: 18 Delhi metro stations reopened, 2 still shut|date=19 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=21 December 2019|archive-date=26 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191226075031/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/anti-caa-protest-gates-of-seven-delhi-metro-stations-closed/articleshow/72880035.cms|url-status=dead}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.ndtv.com/delhi-news/17-delhi-metro-stations-closed-amid-protests-against-citizenship-act-2152085|title=17 Delhi Metro Stations Closed Amid Protests Against Citizenship Act|publisher=NDTV|access-date=21 December 2019}} On 19 December, the [[Delhi Gurgaon Expressway|Delhi-Gurgaon highway]] was closed in view of the protests scheduled in Delhi.{{Cite web|url=https://www.telegraphindia.com/india/protest-marches-across-delhi/cid/1728522|title=Protests across Delhi, Gurgaon highway sealed|website=The Telegraph|location=Kolkota|access-date=27 December 2019}} ===Communication=== The government imposed [[Internet outage|internet shutdowns]] in the states of [[Assam]] and [[Tripura]], five districts in [[West Bengal]], [[Bhopal]], [[Dakshina Kannada]] and parts of Delhi.{{Cite news|url=https://www.business-standard.com/article/pti-stories/internet-services-suspended-in-5-districts-of-bengal-amid-119121500303_1.html|title=Citizenship Act: Govt suspends internet in 5 Bengal districts amid protests|agency=Press Trust of India|date=15 December 2019|work=Business Standard India|access-date=15 December 2019}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/other-states/anti-citizenship-bill-protests-army-deployed-in-assam-tripura-internet-suspended/article30277108.ece|title=Anti-Citizenship Bill protests: Army deployed in Assam, Tripura; Internet suspended|date=11 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=12 December 2019|issn=0971-751X}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/mobile-internet-services-suspended-mangaluru-city-and-dakshina-kannada-48-hours-114410|title=Mobile internet services suspended in Mangaluru city and Dakshina Kannada for 48 hours |website=The News Minute|access-date=20 December 2019|date=19 December 2019 }} Mobile internet and SMS services were suspended in several places in [[Uttar Pradesh]] such as [[Lucknow]], [[Ghaziabad]], [[Bareilly]], [[Meerut]] and [[Prayagraj]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/other-states/anti-caa-protests-mobile-internet-sms-remain-shut-in-parts-of-up-including-lucknow/article30356036.ece|title=Anti-CAA protests: Mobile internet, SMS remain shut in parts of UP, including Lucknow|date=20 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=21 December 2019|agency=Press Trust of India|issn=0971-751X}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.news18.com/news/india/as-violence-grips-up-internet-suspended-in-ghaziabad-prayagraj-bareilly-lucknow-could-be-hit-too-2429997.html|title=As Violence Grips UP, Internet Shut in Ghaziabad, Prayagraj, Bareilly & Meerut; Lko Could be Hit Too|website=News18|date=19 December 2019|access-date=21 December 2019}} ===Tourism=== Canada, France, Israel, Russia, Singapore, [[Taiwan]], the U.S. and the UK have issued travel advisories for nationals travelling to northeast India.{{Cite web|url=https://in.usembassy.gov/travel-alert-for-u-s-citizens-protests-in-northeastern-states-121319/|title=Travel Alert for U.S. Citizens: Protests in Northeastern States|date=13 December 2019|website=U.S. Embassy & Consulates in India}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/anti-citizenship-act-protests-us-uk-france-israel-issue-travel-advisories/article30304634.ece|title=Anti-Citizenship Act protests: U.S., U.K., France, Israel issue travel advisories|date=14 December 2019 |newspaper=The Hindu}} The protests reportedly resulted in a 60% decline in tourists visiting the [[Taj Mahal]] in [[Agra]] for the month of December. The number of visitors went down by at least 90% in the state of Assam, according to the head of [[Assam Tourism Development Corporation]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.livemint.com/news/india/caa-protests-tourism-worst-hit-by-protests-7-countries-issue-travel-warnings-11577602257782.html|title=CAA protests: Tourism worst hit, 7 countries issue travel warnings|date=29 December 2019 |newspaper=Livemint}} == Reactions == === Domestic === ====Return of Awards and Honours==== * [[Mujtaba Hussain]] returned his [[Padma Shri]] and claimed Indian democracy has reduced to "a joke".{{Cite news|title=Urdu writer Mujtaba Hussain to return Padma Shri award in protest against CAA |url=https://www.livemint.com/news/india/urdu-writer-mujtaba-hussain-to-return-padma-shri-award-in-protest-against-caa-11576656902329.html |newspaper=Livemint|date=18 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019}}{{Cite news|title=Urdu satirist Mujtaba Hussain on returning his Padma Shri: Our democracy has been reduced to a joke |url=https://www.firstpost.com/living/urdu-satirist-mujtaba-hussain-on-returning-his-padma-shri-our-democracy-has-been-reduced-to-a-joke-7801861.html |newspaper=Firstpost|date=19 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019}} * The crews of the [[Malayalam film]] ''[[Sudani from Nigeria]],'' which had won a [[National Film Award for Best Feature Film in Malayalam|national award for the best Malayalam film]] in the 66th [[National Film Awards]] decided to boycott the awards ceremony over the protests.{{Cite news|title=Malayalam film 'Sudani from Nigeria' crew to boycott national awards function over NRC, CAA |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/kerala/sudani-from-nigeria-film-crew-to-boycott-award-function/article30315705.ece |newspaper=The Hindu|date=16 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019}}{{Cite news|title='Sudani from Nigeria' director Zakariya to boycott National Awards event over CAB |url=https://www.thenewsminute.com/article/sudani-nigeria-director-zakariya-boycott-national-awards-event-over-cab-114129 |website=The News Minute|date=16 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019}} * [[Shirin Dalvi]] decided to return his [[Sahitya Akademi Award|Maharashtra Rajya Sahitya Akademi Award]].{{Cite news|url=https://thewire.in/rights/urdu-journalist-shirin-dalvi-cab|title=CAB: Two Urdu Writers to Return Awards in Protest|date=12 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019|website=The Wire}}{{Cite news|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/india/cab-protests-two-urdu-writers-to-return-awards-in-protest-of-bill-passage-6164307/|title=CAB protests: Two Urdu writers to return awards in protest of Bill passage|date=13 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019|website=The Hindu}} * [[Jahnu Barua]] withdrew his film ''[[Bhoga Khirikee]]'' from the Assam State Film Awards.{{Cite news|title=Jahnu Barua withdraws his film from Assam film awards over Citizenship Bill |url=https://frontline.thehindu.com/dispatches/article30265692.ece |website=Frontline|date=10 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019}}{{Cite news|title=National award-winner Jahnu Barua withdraws 'Bhoga Khirikee' from Assam film festival in protest against CAB |url=https://frontline.thehindu.com/dispatches/article30265692.ece |website=The Economic Times|date=10 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019}} === International === *'''{{flagu|Afghanistan}}''': Former Afghanistan President [[Hamid Karzai]] urged Government of India to treat all minorities equally. In an interview to ''[[The Hindu]]'', he said, "We don't have persecuted minorities in Afghanistan." Mentioning about the [[Afghanistan conflict (1978–present)|Afghanistan conflict]], he said, "The whole country is persecuted. We have been in war and conflict for a long time. All religions in Afghanistan, Muslims and Hindus and Sikhs, which are our three main religions, have suffered."{{cite news |last1=Haider |first1=Suhasini |title=Former Afghanistan President Hamid Karzai urges India to treat all minorities including Muslims equally |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/all-afghans-have-been-persecuted-karzai/article30600574.ece |access-date=20 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=19 January 2020}} *'''{{flagu|Bangladesh}}''': In an interview to ''[[Gulf News]]'' Bangladesh Prime Minister [[Sheikh Hasina]] said, "Within India, people are facing many problems" and expressed her concerns saying, "We don't understand why (the Indian government) did it. It was not necessary". Though she maintained her stance that the CAA and NRC are internal matters of India. But Hasina's government had previously said that minority communities did not leave Bangladesh because of persecution. She also said that Prime Minister Modi assured her of no reverse migration from India.{{cite news |last1=Bhattacharjee |first1=Kallol |title=India's new citizenship law unnecessary, says Sheikh Hasina |url= https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/indias-new-citizenship-law-unnecessary-says-sheikh-hasina/article30599102.ece |access-date=19 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=19 January 2020}} *'''{{flagu|China}}''': ''[[People's Daily]]'', an official newspaper of [[Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party]], justified internet restrictions citing India's internet shutdowns. The newspaper said that India did not hesitate to shut down the internet in its two states when there was a significant threat to its national security; according to an article in ''[[The Times of India]]'', activists described the justification as setting a dangerous precedent for internet freedom.{{Cite news|first1=Anam|last1=Ajmal|title=China cites India to justify Internet curbs|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/china-cites-india-to-justify-internet-curbs/articleshow/72876443.cms|newspaper=The Times of India|date=19 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019}}{{Cite news|last1=Qing|first1=Qiu|title=India's internet shutdown shows normal practice for sovereign countries|url=http://en.people.cn/n3/2019/1217/c90000-9641267.html|newspaper=People's Daily|date=17 December 2019|access-date=20 December 2019}} *'''{{flagu|Hungary}}''': The [[Fidesz]] Hungarian government in Hungary has expressed support for India on [[Revocation of the special status of Jammu and Kashmir|Kashmir]] and the Citizenship Amendment Act protests.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/mail-today/story/we-back-india-on-caa-nrc-and-kashmir-hungarian-foreign-minister-1637601-2020-01-17|title = We back india on CAA, NRC and Kashmir: Hungarian foreign minister| date=17 January 2020 }} *'''{{flagu|Kuwait}}''': In a statement issued during [[National Assembly (Kuwait)|National Assembly]] session in Kuwait group of lawmakers expressed their concern regarding "abusive legislative and repressive security measures" taken by the Government of India.{{cite news |title=Kuwaiti MPs express 'deep concern' over situation of Muslims in India, China |url=https://gulfbusiness.com/kuwaiti-mps-express-deep-concern-situation-muslims-india-china/ |access-date=31 December 2019 |work=Gilf Business |date=26 December 2019}} *'''{{flagu|Malaysia}}''': Malaysian Prime Minister [[Mahathir Mohamad]] said "Already people are dying because of this law, so why is there a necessity to do this thing?".{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/world/malaysia-caa-india|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191221152539/https://thewire.in/world/malaysia-caa-india|title=Malaysia PM Questions CAA, India Asks Him Not to Comment Without Grasping Facts|date=21 December 2019|website=The Wire|access-date=22 December 2019|url-status=dead|archive-date=21 December 2019}} In response, the Government of India summoned the Chargé d'Affaires of the Malaysian Embassy over the matter.{{Cite news|url= https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/india-lodges-strong-protest-with-malaysia-over-mahathirs-criticism-of-citizenship-law/articleshow/72916477.cms |title=Top Malaysian diplomat summoned to MEA over Mahathir's comments on India's 'internal affairs'|date=21 December 2019|work=The Economic Times|access-date=29 December 2019}} *'''{{flagu|Pakistan}}''': The Prime Minister of Pakistan, [[Imran Khan]] criticised the CAA by claiming that "the present Government. of India has been exposed of its fascist agenda as minorities have come out to protest against the CAA". He also expressed his concern that, such policies by the government. may create a major refugee crisis in the sub-continent.{{cite news |last1=Ameer |first1=Hamza |title=Witnessing fascist, racist govt in India: Pak PM Imran Khan on CAA protests |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/world/story/witnessing-fascist-racist-govt-in-india-pak-pm-imran-khan-on-caa-protests-1632209-2019-12-28 |access-date=5 January 2020 |work=India Today |date=28 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://scroll.in/latest/947117/imran-khan-on-citizenship-act-modi-governments-policies-can-cause-a-big-refugee-crisis|title=Imran Khan says Citizenship Act can lead to 'big refugee crisis', India-Pakistan conflict|date=18 December 2019|work=The Scroll|access-date=22 December 2019}} *'''{{flagu|United States}}''': [[United States Congress|US Congress]] think tank [[Congressional Research Service]] expressed concerns that the CAA along with the NRC may affect the status of the Indian Muslim community.{{cite news |title=CAA, NRC 'may affect status' of Muslims in India: US Congressional report |url=https://www.business-standard.com/article/pti-stories/in-tandem-with-nrc-caa-may-affect-status-of-india-s-muslim-minority-congressional-research-service-119122700116_1.html |access-date=31 December 2019 |work=Business Standard |date=27 December 2019}} ====Organisation==== *'''{{flagu|European Union}}''': EU resolution states that CAA marks 'a dangerous shift ' in the way citizenship will be determined in India and is set to create the 'largest statelessness crisis in the world'.{{Cite web|url=https://theprint.in/world/european-parliament-to-debate-and-vote-on-anti-caa-resolution/354754/|title=European Parliament to debate and vote on anti-CAA resolution|last=PTI|date=26 January 2020|website=ThePrint|access-date=27 February 2020}} *'''{{flagu|Organisation of Islamic Cooperation}}''': [[Organisation of Islamic Cooperation|OIC]] expressed their concern about present situation of CAA-NRC and urged the Government of India to ensure the safety of the Muslim minority and to follow obligation of [[Charter of the United Nations]].{{cite news |title=OIC expresses concern over CAA; says 'closely' following developments |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/oic-expresses-concern-over-caa-says-closely-following-developments/articleshow/72931689.cms |access-date=31 December 2019 |work=The Economic Times |date=23 December 2019}} *'''{{flagu|United Nations}}''': [[Secretary-General of the United Nations]], [[António Guterres]] condemned the "violence and alleged use of excessive force by security forces" and urged the Government of India to respect the freedom of expression and opinion and [[freedom of assembly|freedom of peaceful assembly]].{{cite news |title=UN voices concern over violence in India against CAA, urges respect for freedom of expression |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/un-voices-concern-over-violence-in-india-against-caa-urges-respect-for-freedom-of-expression-1629399-2019-12-18 |access-date=31 December 2019 |work=India Today |date=18 December 2019}} === Human rights organisations === * [[United Nations Commission on Human Rights]] (UNCHR): [[Stéphane Dujarric]], the spokesperson for the [[Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights|U.N. High Commissioner for Human Rights]] (OHCHR) had said that it is ‘concerned’ that the CAA is ‘fundamentally discriminatory in nature’.{{cite news |title=UN voices concern over violence in India against CAA, urges respect for freedom of expression |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/international/un-voices-concern-over-violence-in-india-against-caa-urges-respect-for-freedom-of-expression/article30338241.ece |access-date=2 February 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=18 December 2019}} In a press briefing OHCHR said that CAA appears to "undermine the commitment to equality before the law enshrined in India's constitution and India's obligations under the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights and the Convention for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination", which prohibit discrimination based on racial, ethnic or religious grounds. It also said that "all migrants, regardless of their migration status, are entitled to respect, protection and fulfilment of their human rights."{{cite news |title=Press briefing on India: Citizenship (Amendment) Act 2019 |url=https://www.ohchr.org/EN/NewsEvents/Pages/DisplayNews.aspx?NewsID=25425&LangID=E |access-date=2 February 2020 |work=Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights |date=13 December 2019}} * [[Human Rights Watch]] (HRW): South Asia Director for HRW, Meenakshi Ganguly said that the "government failed to grasp the extent of public opposition over the erosion of basic rights evident in these protests". She urged that the government must establish an independent investigation into allegations of "excessive force, brutality, and vandalism" by police. Its "strongest response to the protests would be to repeal" the Act and "withdraw its plan for citizenship verification that threatens marginalized communities", the organisation said.{{cite news |title=Repeal CAA, order probe into police brutality: Human Rights Watch to Centre |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/repeal-caa-order-probe-police-brutality-human-rights-watch-centre-1629088-2019-12-17 |access-date=2 February 2020 |work=The India Today |date=17 December 2020}} According to an 82-page report published on 9 April 2020, by [[Human Rights Watch]], "‘Shoot the Traitors’: Discrimination Against Muslims Under India's New Citizenship Policy," the police forces and other law enforcement officials perpetually failed in intervening during clashes between the supporters of the [[BJP]] government and those protesting against the new citizenship policies, in which the latter became a victim of repeated attacks.{{cite web|url=https://www.hrw.org/report/2020/04/09/shoot-traitors/discrimination-against-muslims-under-indias-new-citizenship-policy|title=Shoot the Traitors|date=9 April 2020|access-date=9 April 2020|publisher=Human Rights Watch}} * [[Amnesty International]]: Avinash Kumar, Executive Director of Amnesty India said "The CAA is a bigoted law that legitimises discrimination on the basis of religion." He also said that people should have the right to protest peacefully and should have right to freedom of peaceful assembly which facilitate freedom of expression and allows public debate. However, the Central Government and other state governments "rather than respecting, protecting and promoting this right, are clamping down on protestors by using repressive laws".{{cite news |title=India: Crackdown on peaceful Citizenship Act protests must stop |url=https://www.amnesty.org.uk/press-releases/india-crackdown-peaceful-citizenship-act-protests-must-stop |access-date=2 February 2020 |publisher=Amnesty International |date=19 December 2019}} Amnesty has also informed the US lawmakers that the CAA stands in "clear violation" of the constitution of India and international human rights law and "legitimises discrimination" on the basis of religion.{{cite news |last1=Jha |first1=Lalit K |title=CAA in 'Clear Violation' of Indian Constitution: Amnesty International to US Congress |url=https://thewire.in/rights/caa-in-clear-violation-of-indian-constitution-amnesty-international-to-us-congress |access-date=2 February 2020 |work=The Wire |date=1 February 2020}} == Pro-CAA demonstrations == Rallies and demonstrations in support of Citizenship Amendment Bill were held in New Delhi,{{Cite news|last1=Mehta|first1=Pratap Bhanu|url=https://www.foreignaffairs.com/articles/india/2019-12-20/modi-pushes-india-revolt|title=Modi Pushes India Into Revolt|journal=Foreign Affairs: America and the World|date=23 December 2019|access-date=9 February 2020|issn=0015-7120}}{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/delhi/people-gather-at-delhis-central-park-raise-slogans-in-support-of-caa/articleshow/72904498.cms|title=Protest in Delhi Today: People gather at Delhi's Central Park, raise slogans in support of CAA|last=PTI|date=20 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=24 December 2019}} Mumbai,{{Cite web|url=https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/news/national/at-pro-caa-rally-in-mumbai-people-hail-pm-modi/article30367926.ece|title=PM Modi hailed at a pro-CAA rally in Mumbai|last=PTI|website=@businessline|date=21 December 2019 |access-date=24 December 2019}} Nagpur,{{Cite news|url=http://www.ddinews.gov.in/national/massive-rally-support-citizenship-amendment-act-nagpur|title=Massive rally in support of Citizenship Amendment Act in Nagpur|date=22 December 2019|work=DD News|access-date=24 December 2019}} Bangalore,{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/pro-caa-rallies-in-bengaluru-nagpur-man-returning-from-event-stabbed/articleshow/72930768.cms|title=Pro-CAA rallies in Bengaluru, Nagpur; man returning from event stabbed|date=23 December 2019|website=[[The Times of India]]|access-date=24 December 2019}} Dehradun{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/dehradun/anti-pro-caa-protests-held-in-city/articleshow/72944979.cms|title=Anti & pro-CAA protests held in city|last1=Anab|first1=Mohammad|date=24 December 2019|website=[[The Times of India]]|access-date=24 December 2019}} and many other places.{{cite news|url=http://www.ddnews.gov.in/national/bjp-takes-out-massive-rally-support-caa|title=BJP takes out massive rally in support of CAA {{!}} DD News|date=24 December 2019|work=ddnews.gov.in|access-date=24 December 2019}} A rally in Kolkata was headed by [[Jagat Prakash Nadda]], working national president of BJP, and was attended by persecuted Hindu refugees from Pakistan and Bangladesh.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/hindu-refugees-from-pakistan-honour-bjps-jp-nadda-at-pro-caa-function/articleshow/72924706.cms|title=Hindu refugees from Pakistan honour BJP's JP Nadda at pro-CAA function|last=22 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=25 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/jp-nadda-takes-out-bjp-rally-in-kolkata-in-support-of-caa/articleshow/72937000.cms|title=JP Nadda takes out BJP rally in Kolkata in support of CAA|last=PTI|date=23 December 2019|website=[[The Times of India]]|access-date=24 December 2019}} Protests against CAA were condemned in these rallies and hailed Prime Minister [[Narendra Modi]] for taking a decision on CAA.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/at-pro-caa-rally-in-mumbai-people-hail-pm-modi/articleshow/72917692.cms|title=At pro-CAA rally in Mumbai, people hail PM Modi|date=21 December 2019|website=[[The Times of India]]|agency=Press Trust of India|access-date=24 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.freepressjournal.in/mumbai/now-some-pro-caa-rallies-too-in-mumbai|title=Now, some pro-CAA rallies too in Mumbai|website=Latest Indian news, Top Breaking headlines, Today Headlines, Top Stories at Free Press Journal|access-date=24 December 2019}} Nine [[Jainism|Jain]] organisations came out in support of CAA on 20 December 2019 and thanked a BJP politician and BJP for the Amendment. Over 1,000 academicians released statements in support of CAA.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/over-academicians-release-statement-supporting-citizenship-amendment-act-caa-1630336-2019-12-21|title=Over 1,000 academicians release statement supporting Citizenship Amendment Act|last=Press Trust of India|date=21 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=24 December 2019}} Signatories included [[Swapan Dasgupta]], [[Shishir Bajoria]], journalist Kanchan Gupta and [[Jawaharlal Nehru University|JNU]] faculty and administration including professor [[Syed Ainul Hasan|Ainul Hasan]], dean of students Umesh Ashok Kadam and registrar Pramod Kumar.{{Cite web|url=https://thewire.in/politics/pro-caa-statement-scholars|title='Avoid Propaganda Trap': 1,100 Academics, Researchers Issue Statement Supporting CAA|website=The Wire|access-date=24 December 2019}} The President of [[Delhi University Students' Union]] released a statement in support of the CAA which was condemned by partisan student unions of colleges under Delhi University.{{Cite web |date=2019-12-18 |title=Many DU college unions condemn DUSU stand in favour of CAA |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/many-du-college-unions-condemn-dusu-stand-in-favour-of-caa-1629486-2019-12-18 |access-date=2024-10-12 |website=India Today |language=en}} A pro-CAA rally named ''Jana Jagarana Rally'', was organised at [[Tirupati]] and attended by BJP national vice-president and former Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister [[Shivraj Singh Chouhan]] and mostly BJP workers. The participants raised slogans in support of CAA and carried placards with slogans such as ''‘We support CAA’'', ''‘India supports CAA’'', ''‘CAA a punishment to intruders and traitors’'' and ''‘CAA protects refugees’''. The participants also carried a 500-metre-long [[Indian national flag]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.newindianexpress.com/states/andhra-pradesh/2019/dec/28/bjp-leaders-take-out-rally-in-support-of-citizenship-act-in-tirupati-target-congress-2081851.html|title=BJP leaders take out rally in support of Citizenship Act in Tirupati, target Congress|website=The New Indian Express|date=28 December 2019 |access-date=2 January 2020}} === Assam === A rally was organised by BJP to support the Citizenship Amendment Act in Assam's [[Morigaon]]{{Cite news|title=Assam BJP holds mega rally in Morigaon supporting Citizenship Amendment Act|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/assam-bjp-holds-mega-rally-in-morigaon-supporting-citizenship-amendment-act/videoshow/73008140.cms|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191230223056/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/assam-bjp-holds-mega-rally-in-morigaon-supporting-citizenship-amendment-act/videoshow/73008140.cms|url-status=dead|archive-date=30 December 2019|access-date=1 January 2020}} on 27 December in which over 50,000 civilians, including BJP workers, [[Asom Gana Parishad]] and [[Bodoland People's Front]] leaders took part.{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/assam-bjp-takes-out-mega-peace-rally-in-guwahati-1632070-2019-12-27|title=Assam BJP takes out mega peace rally in Guwahati|first1=Hemanta|last1=Kumar Nath|date=27 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=1 January 2020}} The 4-km long rally was led by Assam [[Chief Minister]] of Assam [[Sarbananda Sonowal]] and state Finance Minister [[Himanta Biswa Sarma]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.financialexpress.com/india-news/citizenship-act-protests-assam-holds-pro-caa-rally-for-peace-and-harmony-amid-violent-protests-nationwide/1799947/|title=Assam holds pro-CAA rally for peace and harmony amid violent protests nationwide|date=20 December 2019|website=The Financial Express|access-date=1 January 2020}} === West Bengal === Thousands of BJP workers took out a massive rally named ''Abhinandan Yatra'' or ''Thanks-giving rally'' in North Kolkata in support of CAA on 30 December.{{Cite web|url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/at-pro-caa-rally-in-bengal-nadda-says-huge-turnout-signals-change/story-k5JUbIlec1rkzKpgdy3dJJ.html|title=At pro-CAA rally in Bengal, Nadda says 'huge' turnout signals change|date=24 December 2019|website=Hindustan Times|access-date=1 January 2020}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/bjp-takes-out-pro-caa-rally-in-kolkata-mamata-writes-to-opposition-cms/article30382844.ece|title=Nadda leads BJP rally for CAA in Kolkata|date=23 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=1 January 2020|issn=0971-751X}} It was led by the party's Working President [[J P Nadda]] to thank Prime Minister [[Narendra Modi]] for enacting the Citizenship Amendment Act.{{Cite web|url=https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/politics/j-p-nadda-takes-out-bjp-rally-in-kolkata-in-support-of-caa-4755691.html|title=J P Nadda takes out BJP rally in Kolkata in support of CAA|website=Moneycontrol|date=23 December 2019 |access-date=1 January 2020}} === Gujarat === On 24 December, a large gathering was organised at [[Sabarmati Ashram]], [[Ahmedabad]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/muslims-have-150-countries-to-go-hindus-have-only-india-gujarat-cm-1631267-2019-12-24|title=Muslims have 150 countries to go to, Hindus have only India: Gujarat CM Vijay Rupani|date=24 December 2019|website=India Today|access-date=25 December 2019}} [[Vijay Rupani]], Chief Minister of Gujarat was present at the event, said that Gujarat will definitely implement CAA.{{Cite web|url=https://ahmedabadmirror.indiatimes.com/ahmedabad/others/cm-to-join-rss-backed-pro-caa-event/articleshow/72945328.cms|title=CM to join RSS-backed pro-CAA event|last1=Patadiya|first1=Vishal|date=24 December 2019|website=Ahmedabad Mirror|access-date=25 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/ahmedabad/gujarat-implements-caa-cm/articleshow/72961393.cms|title=Gujarat implements CAA: CM Vijay Rupani|date=25 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=25 December 2019}} He also stated that Muslims have 150 countries to go while Hindus have only one.{{Cite web|url=https://www.newindianexpress.com/nation/2019/dec/24/muslims-have-150-countries-to-go-but-hindus-only-have-india-says-gujarat-cm-vijay-rupani-2080475.html|title=Muslims have 150 countries to go but Hindus only have India, says Gujarat CM Vijay Rupani|website=The New Indian Express|date=24 December 2019 |access-date=25 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://indianexpress.com/article/cities/ahmedabad/in-support-of-new-citizenship-law-cm-bjp-leaders-power-rallies-across-state-6183378/|title=In support of New citizenship law: CM, BJP leaders power rallies across Gujarat|date=25 December 2019|website=The Indian Express|access-date=25 December 2019}} BJP leaders and ministers took parts in the different rallies organised across all 33 districts of Gujarat.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/vadodara/caa-rallies-held-in-central-gujarat/articleshow/72961181.cms|title=CAA rallies held in central Gujarat |date=25 December 2019|website=The Times of India|access-date=25 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=http://newsonair.com/Main-News-Details.aspx?id=376608|title=Gujarat BJP leaders participate in rallies in support of CAA|website=All India Radio (AIR)|access-date=25 December 2019|archive-date=25 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191225163849/http://newsonair.com/Main-News-Details.aspx?id=376608|url-status=dead}}{{Cite web|url=https://in.news.yahoo.com/guj-pro-caa-rally-surat-083134715.html|title=Guj: Pro-CAA rally in Surat, CM to attend Ahmedabad event|publisher=Yahoo! News|access-date=25 December 2019}} === Maharashtra === Maharashtra witnessed pro-CAA demonstrations in cities including Nagpur, Mumbai, [[Yavatmal]], [[Wardha]] and Pune.{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/nagpur/pro-caa-rallies-carried-out-in-yavatmal-and-wardha/articleshow/72971996.cms|title=Pro-CAA rallies carried out in Yavatmal and Wardha|date=26 December 2019|first1=Balwant|last1=Dhage|website=The Times of India|access-date=28 December 2019}} On 23 December 2019, RSS linked organisations carried out rally in Nagpur which was attended by [[Nitin Gadkari]] and [[Devendra Fadnavis]] along with more than 25,000 people.{{Cite web|url=https://www.asianage.com/metros/mumbai/231219/rss-linked-organisation-holds-pro-caa-rally-in-nagpur.html|title=RSS-linked organisation holds pro-CAA rally in Nagpur|date=23 December 2019|website=The Asian Age|access-date=28 December 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/nagpur/nearly-25000-join-pro-caa-rally-amid-nationwide-protests/articleshow/72928488.cms|title=Nearly 25,000 join pro-CAA rally amid nationwide protests|date=23 December 2019|first1=Sarfaraz|last1=Ahmed|website=The Times of India|access-date=28 December 2019}} [[Devendra Fadnavis]], former chief minister of Maharashtra, targeted [[Shiv Sena]] at the event organised by BJP's Samvidhan Sanman Manch and also organised pro-CAA rally in [[Mumbai]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/other-states/pro-caa-rally-at-mumbais-kranti-maidan-draws-crowds/article30412958.ece|title=Pro-CAA rally at Mumbai's Kranti Maidan draws crowds|date=27 December 2019|work=The Hindu|access-date=28 December 2019|agency=Press Trust of India|issn=0971-751X}} === Phone call campaign === Several BJP leaders including Home Minister Amit Shah had publicised a phone number, asking people to call the number as a way to show their support for the CAA. Many accounts on social media were seen sharing the same number and asking people to call, intending to inflate the number of supporters of CAA.{{cite news |title=BJP Resorts To Fake 'Lonely Woman' On Twitter To Drum Up Support For CAA |url=https://www.huffingtonpost.in/entry/bjp-lonely-woman-twitter-caa-support_in_5e10660cc5b6b5a713ba970b |access-date=4 January 2020 |work=HuffPost India |date=4 January 2020 }} The number was also shared on bogus posts offering free six-month subscriptions to the streaming site [[Netflix]] for free. Netflix called the offer as fake.{{cite news |title="Absolutely Fake": Netflix On Tweet Luring People To Back Citizenship Law |url=https://www.ndtv.com/india-news/netflix-says-fake-after-call-for-subscription-links-number-to-back-caa-citizenship-amendment-act-2158813 |access-date=4 January 2020 |work=NDTV.com |date=4 January 2020}} === Fake videos === A video of ''[[Naga Sadhu]]s'' celebrating at [[Kumbh Mela]] at [[Allahabad]] in March 2019 was shared and made [[Viral video|viral]] on social media,{{cite news |last1=Dahiya |first1=Himanshi |title=Video Falsely Shows Kumbh Mela as Pro-CAA Rally in Haridwar |url=https://www.thequint.com/news/webqoof/video-from-prayagraj-kumbh-mela-shared-as-pro-caa-rally-in-haridwar |access-date=9 January 2020 |work=The Quint |date=8 January 2020}} falsely claiming that this was the video of Hindus in the rally supporting CAA.{{cite news |last1=Bajpai |first1=Akshay |title=सीएए के समर्थन के नाम से नागा साधुओं का वीडियो वायरल, पड़ताल में पता चला क्लिप कुंभ की है |url=https://www.bhaskar.com/no-fake-news/news/no-fake-news-on-sadhus-shows-a-pro-caa-demonstration-in-haridwar-126467494.html |access-date=9 January 2020 |work=Dainik Bhaskar |date=8 January 2020 |language=hi}}{{cite news |title=FAKE ALERT: Old video from Kumbh Mela shared as naga sadhus |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/times-fact-check/news/fake-alert-old-video-from-kumbh-mela-shared-as-naga-sadhus-rallying-in-support-of-caa/articleshow/73138324.cms |access-date=9 January 2020 |work=The Times of India |date=7 January 2020}} ===Overseas=== Members of the Indian-American community held a pro-CAA rally in front of the Indian Consulate in Houston on 20 December 2019.{{Cite web|url=https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/news/world/indian-americans-hold-rallies-in-support-of-caa-nrc-in-us/article30394138.ece|title=Indian-Americans hold rallies in support of CAA, NRC|last=[[Press Trust of India]]|website=[[Business Line]]|date=25 December 2019 |access-date=26 December 2019}} They also held other rallies at [[Victor Steinbrueck Park]], [[Seattle]] and [[Texas State Capitol]] building in [[Austin, Texas|Austin]] on 22 December 2019. Rallies were also held at Ted Kaltenbach Park, [[Dublin, California|Dublin]], [[Ohio]] and at Nash Square Park, [[Raleigh, North Carolina]].{{Cite web|url=http://news.rediff.com/commentary/2019/dec/25/indianamericans-hold-rallies-backing-caa-nrc/c2697a59a9fdfcbda73408a07aa75821|title=Indian-Americans hold rallies backing CAA, NRC|work=Rediff.com|access-date=26 December 2019}} ==Petitions in Supreme Court== On 14 January, the [[Kerala government]] approached Supreme Court to challenge the CAA under section 131 of the constitution that provides Supreme Court the power to decide the disputes between the states and the Government of India. The Kerala government in its petition called the act "a violation of India's secular constitution" and accused the Indian government of dividing the country among religious lines. The Supreme Court had scheduled the hearing of CAA related petitions on 22 January. The students' association have called a complete shutdown of the colleges and university in the north-east, on 22 January, asking the court to declare CAA unconstitutional. On 22 January 144 CAA related petitions scheduled for hearing in the Supreme Court of India were brought up. The [[Chief Justice of India]] (CJI) [[Sharad A. Bobde]] had led the three judge bench in hearing the petitions. The court gave notice to the government on the petitions and allowed one month time to respond in the next hearing in February.{{cite news |last1=Rajagopal |first1=Krishnadas |title=SC refuses to stay CAA without hearing govt |url=https://www.thehindu.com/news/national/caa-petitions-sc-says-no-stay-without-hearing-centre-may-refer-pleas-to-larger-constitution-bench/article30622277.ece |access-date=22 January 2020 |work=The Hindu |date=22 January 2020 }} K.V. Vishwanathan, a senior advocate addressed the court, stating "The most immediate concern now is the sweeping powers given to [[Executive (government)|executive authorities]] to brand people as 'doubtful citizens'. Once this is done, there are no guidelines to help these people. This is sinister. It will lead to [[gerrymandering]] of electoral rolls. The concern is spread across both the majority Hindus and the minorities as well. You have to address this fear... Otherwise fear and insecurity will pervade the country." Reacting to the concerns the CJI stated that the laws like the CAA are not irreversible, and the court will hear the interim prayer for a stay on CAA at a future date in February. On 3 March, the [[Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights]] (OHCHR), filed an application in the Supreme Court and asked to be made a party in the case citing "the exclusions of persons... on the basis of their religion". The OHCHR Commissioner, [[Michelle Bachelet]] said that the "differentiations" drawn by the Act are not "sufficiently objective and reasonable". However, the [[Ministry of External Affairs (India)|Ministry of External Affairs]] reacted to the move and said that the CAA is an "internal matter" of India and "no foreign party had any locus standi on issues pertaining to India's sovereignty".{{cite news |last1=Mohan |first1=Geeta |title=UN human rights body moves Supreme Court over CAA, India hits back saying citizenship law internal matter |url=https://www.indiatoday.in/india/story/now-un-human-rights-body-moves-supreme-court-over-caa-1651950-2020-03-03 |access-date=4 March 2020 |work=The India Today |date=3 March 2020}}{{cite news |title=UN High Commissioner for Human Rights files intervention plea in Supreme Court on CAA, India protests |url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/news/politics-and-nation/un-high-commissioner-for-human-rights-files-intervention-plea-in-supreme-court-on-caa-india-protests/articleshow/74454875.cms |access-date=4 March 2020 |work=The Economic Times |date=3 March 2020}} ==See also== {{Portal|India|Politics|Law|History|Freedom of speech|Society}} * [[2019 Jamia Millia Islamia attack]] * [[2020 JNU Attack]] * [[Assam Accord]] * [[Assam Movement]] * [[National Register of Citizens]] * [[North East Delhi Riots]] * [[Shaheen Bagh protests]] {{Clear}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category|Citizenship Amendment Act protests}} * "[https://indiancitizenshiponline.nic.in/UserGuide/E-gazette_2019_20122019.pdf The Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019]". ''[[The Gazette of India]]'' (2019). {{Citizenship Amendment Act protests}} [[Category:Citizenship Amendment Act protests| ]] [[Category:2019 protests]] [[Category:2019 riots]] [[Category:2020 protests]] [[Category:2020 riots]] [[Category:December 2019 in India]] [[Category:Immigration to India]] [[Category:Immigration-related protests]] [[Category:Islamophobia in India]] [[Category:Anti-Islam sentiment in India]] [[Category:January 2020 in India]] [[Category:Politics of India]] [[Category:Protests in India]] [[Category:Secularism in India]] [[Category:Xenophobia in Asia]]
