revision.text:
{{Short description|none}} {{Use American English|date=October 2025}} {{Use mdy dates|date=October 2025}} {{multiple image | total_width = 270 | image_style = border:1; | perrow = 2/2/2/2 | image1 = Michael Jackson Live in Bucharest 1992 Toni Salabasev (cropped).jpg | image2 = Michael Jackson Impersonator Billie Jean.jpg | image3 = Michael Jackson Ronald and Nancy Reagan.png | image4 = Michael Jackson Thriller Jacket.jpg | image5 = Postal Cover of Romania - Michael Jackson (1992) 04.jpg | image6 = Michael Jackson sculpture.jpg | image7 = Thrill the World.jpg | align = right | caption_align = left | footer = '''Clockwise from top:''' Michael Jackson live in [[Bucharest]] (1992), German [[Michael Jackson impersonator]] Andre Santisi performing "[[Billie Jean (song)|Billie Jean]]", [[Thriller (song)|Thriller jacket]], [[List of monuments and memorials to Michael Jackson|Michael Jackson monument]] in the [[Netherlands]], Asian fans in Thailand dancing to "[[Beat It (song)|Beat It]]", [[Thrill the World]] event in Texas, Jackson's [[Romania]]n postal stamps, Jackson with former President [[Ronald Reagan]] and wife [[Nancy Reagan|Nancy]] (1984). | image8 = Thai-fans-of-Michael-Jack-001.jpg }} American singer [[Michael Jackson]] is widely regarded as one of the most culturally significant figures of the 20th century.<ref>Sources comparing Michael Jackson to Jesus Christ{{Cite magazine |last=Stansfield |first=Ted |date=June 28, 2018 |title=How Michael Jackson Impacted the World of Art |url=https://www.anothermanmag.com/life-culture/10396/how-michael-jackson-impacted-the-world-of-art |access-date=November 8, 2024 |magazine=[[Another Man]] |quote=Few have permeated the realm of art like Michael Jackson. Aside from Jesus Christ, there aren't many people who have inspired creative practitioners to the degree that the MJ has.}}{{Cite book |last1=Kopecka-Piech |first1=Katarzyna |url=https://www.routledge.com/The-Covid-19-Pandemic-as-a-Challenge-for-Media-and-Communication-Studies/Kopecka-Piech-Lodzki/p/book/9781032139418?srsltid=AfmBOoqldtsNbek0j8_TZLqg6zRR_QYZUfAZ0oOtbNqhlsYFCb3M-d3k |title=The Covid-19 Pandemic as a Challenge for Media and Communication Studies |last2=Łódzki |first2=Bartłomiej |date=2022-01-31 |publisher=[[Routledge]] |isbn=978-1-000-53742-0 |language=en |quote=I don't even want to say its name anymore because it has become more famous than Jesus or Michael Jackson}}{{Cite book |last=Eyl |first=Jennifer |url=https://academic.oup.com/book/36495 |title=Signs, Wonders, and Gifts: Divination in the Letters of Paul (Christian Studies) |date=2019-06-03 |publisher=[[Oxford University Press]] |isbn=978-0-19-092466-9 |language=en |quote="…observers could not decide if the miraculous face discovered in a birch tree stump was Michael Jackson or Jesus; both were equally famous and Michael Jackson, according to one interviewee, was capable of the same salvific work as Jesus."}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Powell |first=Rose |date=2014-06-10 |title=Wikipedia's most influential people: Carl Linnaeus, Jesus, Hitler, Michael Jackson |url=https://www.smh.com.au/technology/wikipedias-most-influential-people-carl-linnaeus-jesus-hitler-michael-jackson-20140611-zs39r.html |access-date=2024-04-28 |website=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":13">{{Cite web |last=Stevens |first=Hampton |date=2010-06-24 |title=Michael Jackson was the most influential artist of the 20th century. |url=https://www.theatlantic.com/entertainment/archive/2010/06/michael-jacksons-unparalleled-influence/58616/ |access-date=2024-04-28 |website=The Atlantic |language=en}}</ref> Often considered the greatest entertainer of all time,<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3">{{Cite news |last=Satariano |first=Adam |date=2009-07-08 |title=Family, Fans Mourn Jackson at Site of Final Rehearsal |url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2009-07-08/family-fans-mourn-jackson-at-site-of-final-rehearsal |access-date=2025-01-16 |work=Bloomberg.com |language=en |quote="the greatest entertainer that ever lived."}}</ref> Jackson broke [[Timeline of African-American firsts|racial barriers in the U.S.]] and profoundly influenced the evolution of pop music, earning him the title of "[[Honorific nicknames in popular music|King of Pop]]".<ref>{{Cite news |last=Segal |first=David |date=June 27, 2009 |title=After Michael Jackson, Fame May Never Be the Same |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2009/06/28/weekinreview/28segal.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191002032527/https://www.nytimes.com/2009/06/28/weekinreview/28segal.html |archive-date=October 2, 2019 |access-date=October 2, 2019 |work=The New York Times |issn=0362-4331}}</ref> He is one of the [[List of best-selling music artists|best-selling music artists in history]], having sold over 500 million records worldwide. His unparalleled success spans multiple decades, with numerous albums that rank amongst the [[List of best selling albums|best-selling of all time]], such as ''[[Off the Wall]]'' (1979), ''[[Thriller (album)|Thriller]]'' (1982), ''[[Bad (album)|Bad]]'' (1987), ''[[Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)|Dangerous]]'' (1991), and ''[[HIStory: Past, Present and Future, Book I]]'' (1995); ''Thriller'' remains the best-selling album of all time. ''[[Guinness World Records]]'' named him the most successful entertainer of all time. His achievements in the 1980s helped [[Desegregation in the United States|desegregation]] of popular music in the United States and introduced an era of [[multiculturalism]] globally. Through his dance, fashion and redefinition of [[Michael Jackson music videos|music videos]], Jackson proliferated visual performance for musical artists. Credited for [[#Influence on other performers|influencing hundreds of musicians]], his songs are [[List of cover versions of Michael Jackson songs|among the most covered and sampled in music history]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Most Sampled Artists |url=https://www.whosampled.com/most-sampled-artists/ |access-date=November 25, 2024 |website=[[WhoSampled]]}}</ref><ref name="auto2">Roberts, "Kingdom", p. 36.</ref> His influence extended to inspiring a vast array of trends and raising awareness for social causes around the world; as a result, Jackson is widely regarded as one of the most famous figures in history.<ref>Sources calling Michael Jackson the most famous person of all time or one of the most famous people in history: {{Cite news |last=Vieira |first=Meredith |date=September 26, 2009 |title=Children 'saved me,' Michael Jackson said |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/id/wbna32987303 |access-date=September 12, 2025 |quote=He may well have been the most famous celebrity of all time |agency=NBC News}} {{Cite web |last=Greene |first=Al |date=January 21, 2014 |title=Michael Jackson - Biography (Rhino) |url=https://www.rhino.com/article/michael-jackson |access-date=September 12, 2025 |publisher=[[Rhino Entertainment]] |quote=He remains an object of adoration, fascination and speculation even after his death in 2009. Indeed, Jackson surpassed all previous notions of fame to attain a level of iconic attention that was not only awe-inspiring but also daunting and, on some levels, damaging. He has been proclaimed "the biggest-selling artist of all time," "the single most awarded entertainer the world has ever known," "the most popular artist in the history of show business," and "the world's most famous man."}} {{Cite news |last=Beaumont-Thomas |first=Ben |date=June 25, 2014 |title=Michael Jackson: Five years after his death, how his influence lives on |url=https://www.theguardian.com/music/2014/jun/25/five-ways-michael-jackson-influence-lives-on |access-date=September 12, 2025 |newspaper=The Guardian |issn=0261-3077 |quote=technically it should be easy to get as famous as Jackson – you can be adored from the Mongolian steppes to the Madagascan rainforest, all via YouTube. But in reality, fame is diluted by the internet as everyone makes their own collaged personal vision of global culture, and so that Jackson-level fame, an omnipresence mediated via the restricted channels of radio, TV and recorded sound, is unlikely ever to be repeated}} {{Cite magazine |date=February 23, 2017 |title=18 Documentaries About Your Favorite Celebrities That Will Make You Look At Them In All New Ways |url=https://www.bustle.com/p/18-documentaries-about-your-favorite-celebrities-that-will-make-you-look-at-them-in-all-new-ways-40136 |access-date=September 12, 2025 |magazine=[[Bustle (magazine)|Bustle]] |quote=The world simply could not function without a solid documentary of Michael Jackson, arguably the most famous person of all time.}} {{Cite news |last=Grundey |first=Adam |date=June 29, 2019 |title=Wanted dead or alive: Despite 'Leaving Neverland,' Michael Jackson's star seems undiminished in the Middle East |url=https://www.arabnews.com/node/1517811/lifestyle |access-date=September 12, 2025 |newspaper=[[Arab News]] |quote=Jackson was idolized around the world. He became an enormous part of people's lives — and arguably the most recognizable person in history}} {{Cite book |last=Kisuule |first=Vanessa |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=NEPpEAAAQBAJ&dq=michael+jackson+amazon+tribe+currency&pg=PT49 |title=Neverland: The Pleasures and Perils of Fandom |date=September 12, 2024 |publisher=Canongate Books |isbn=978-1-83885-708-0 |quote=Söderström observed: "They didn't know about the moon landing and were concerned about the negative impact it could have for conditions on Earth. They couldn't understand modern warfare and appeared to be shocked by the footage of the 9/11 attacks in New York. Neither did they know France's most famous soccer star of the day. However, all the young members of the (Amazonian) tribe were familiar with Michael Jackson."}} {{Cite web |last=Vogel |first=Joseph |date=September 24, 2017 |title=Michael Jackson's Forgotten Humanitarian Legacy |url=https://www.huffpost.com/entry/michael-jacksons-forgotten-humanitarian-legacy_b_59c7c8d3e4b08d661550436a |access-date=October 20, 2025 |website=[[HuffPost]] |quote=He was the most famous person on the planet. Everywhere he travelled, he created mass hysteria. The day after his sold-out concert at Prater Stadium in Vienna, an AP article ran, "130 Fans Faint at Jackson Concert." If the Beatles were more popular than Jesus, as John Lennon once claimed, Jackson had the entire Holy Trinity beat.}}</ref> Before [[Death of Michael Jackson|he died]], Jackson was received by over 30 different world leaders.<ref name="auto12" /><ref>President Ali Hassan Mwinyi, Nelson Mandela, Dali Lama, George H.W. Bush, Bill Clinton, Ronald Reagan, Kim Dae Jung, Carlos Salinas, Omar Bongo, Jacques Chirac, Queen Elizabeth II, Princess Diana, Jerry Rawlings, Pierre Mauroy, Ion Illiescu, Ariel Shanon, Aleksander Kwaśniewski, Jim Bolger, Hassan Diria, tribal chief Amon N'Djafolk, King Nana Amon Ndufu IV, Saudi Arabia's Prince Alwaleed Bin Talal, Theo-Ben Gurirab, Nangolo Mbumba, President Laurent Kabila, prince Al-Waleed bin Talal, Carlos Menem, Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah, Sultan Haitham Bin Tariq, Prince Abdullah, Yuri Luzhkov, Robert et Grace Mugabe, HRH Prince Albert of Monaco [https://www.tampabay.com/archive/1993/11/10/what-s-michael-doing-in-mexico/][https://www.ghanaweb.com/GhanaHomePage/NewsArchive/Watch-video-of-when-Michael-Jackson-presented-a-gold-sword-to-Rawlings-in-1995-1737875][https://www.billboard.com/music/music-news/king-of-pop-meets-israeli-pm-80275/]</ref> Jackson's [[Michael Jackson fandom|global brand]] resulted in celebrity products and commemorations such as [[Michael Jackson in video games|video games]], [[Michael Jackson videography#Film|documentaries]], and [[List of monuments and memorials to Michael Jackson|monuments]].
Popularity of Michael Jackson began as a child star in the 1960s, his introduction as the lead singer of [[the Jackson 5]], a band formed with his older brothers.<ref>Warwick, p. 249.</ref> The group was recognized by [[United States Congress|U.S. Congress]] for their contribution to American youth culture, and Jackson was embraced by the American public to a degree not afforded a child star since the height of [[Shirley Temple]] in the 1930s.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=484}} In the early 1980s, Jackson became a dominant figure in popular culture and the first African-American entertainer to have a strong [[crossover music|crossover]] fanbase on [[music television]].<ref name="Nothing More Than ... Feelings">{{cite news|first=Jonathan|last=Yardley|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-962615.html|title=Nothing More Than ... Feelings|newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]|date=August 30, 1993|access-date=March 10, 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121102054128/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-962615.html|archive-date=November 2, 2012}}</ref><ref name="Off the wall but still invincible">{{cite news|first=Elizabeth|last=Day|url=https://www.theguardian.com/music/2009/mar/08/michael-jackson-profile-pop-culture|title=Off the wall but still invincible|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]|date=March 8, 2009|access-date=March 10, 2009|location=London|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140326134523/http://www.theguardian.com/music/2009/mar/08/michael-jackson-profile-pop-culture|archive-date=March 26, 2014|url-status=live}}</ref> As he became a rising solo star, [[Michael Jackson music videos|his music videos]], including those for "[[Beat It]]", "[[Billie Jean]]", and "[[Thriller (song)|Thriller]]" from his album ''Thriller'' (1982), are credited with breaking several racial barriers both [[Timeline of African-American history|in the United States]] and worldwide, transforming the medium into an art form and promotional tool.<ref>{{Cite web | url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/entertainment/michael-jackson-broke-the-colour-barrier-1.833673 |title = Michael Jackson broke the colour barrier}}</ref> The popularity of these videos helped bring the television channel [[MTV]] to fame.<ref>Brackett, David. [https://www.oxfordmusiconline.com/grovemusic/view/10.1093/gmo/9781561592630.001.0001/omo-9781561592630-e-0000047207 "Jackson, Michael (Joseph)"]. ''[[Grove Music Online]].'' Accessed October 1, 2019.</ref> Prior to ''Thriller'', timely layoffs were occurring for radio and music record companies, who both suffered during a four year unemployment high between 1978 and 1982 due to the [[early 1980s recession]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walther |first=Matthew |date=2019-02-07 |title=The immorality of layoffs |url=https://theweek.com/articles/822188/immorality-layoffs |access-date=2024-12-17 |website=theweek |language=en}}</ref> [[List of Michael Jackson records and achievements|Jackson's world record sales and achievements]] are credited with revolutionizing the music industry by initiating marketing plans on blockbuster albums with an emphasis on video presentation focus going forward.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Johnson |first=Martin |date=2009-06-26 |title=The Album That Saved Pop Music |url=https://www.theroot.com/the-album-that-saved-pop-music-1790869682 |access-date=2024-12-17 |website=The Root |language=en}}</ref>
[[File:Moonwalk paso.gif|thumb|[[Moonwalk (dance)|Moonwalk]], a signature move of Jackson that he named]] Further development through his videos and live performances, Jackson popularized [[street dance]] moves, particularly his signature move the [[Moonwalk (dance)|moonwalk]], [[patent]]ed the [[Smooth Criminal#Music video|anti-gravity lean]] and attracted [[Michael Jackson impersonator|a cult of impersonators]] throughout the world. He is credited with helping to spread dance to a global audience and having an authority comparable to dance icons such as to [[Fred Astaire]] and [[Sammy Davis Jr.]] With an aesthetic borrowed from the [[musical film]] tradition, the ''Thriller'' videos created a sub-industry of [[Choreography (dance)|choreographers]] as other pop artists deliberately sought to produce sophisticated dance-oriented promotional films and concerts for music on an unprecedented scale. In the latter half of the 1980s, Jackson's personal idiosyncrasies and changing appearance became the source of fascination for the [[Tabloid journalism|tabloid media]], a phenomenon furthered by the [[1993 Michael Jackson sexual abuse allegations|child abuse accusations leveled against him]] in 1993. These eccentricities and controversies created major debate, both from [[Comedy|comedic]] and [[critic]]al perspectives alike. As his last two albums focused more on [[social commentary]], he matchingly pioneered charitable causes as a philanthropist, putting his wealth into several hospitals and nonprofits in various countries. [[Philanthropy of Michael Jackson|His philanthrophy]] has been described as having set a standard for celebrity charity.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Wilhelm |first=Ian |date=2009-06-26 |title=Michael Jackson and Philanthropy |url=https://www.philanthropy.com/article/michael-jackson-and-philanthropy/ |access-date=2025-10-20 |website=The Chronicle of Philanthropy |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Daunt |first=Tina |date=2009-07-08 |title=Giving in spirit and deed |url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-2009-jul-08-et-cause8-story.html |access-date=2025-10-20 |website=[[Los Angeles Times]] |language=en-US}}</ref>
Jackson influenced a wide range of subjects, from celebrity studies, music and dance production to visual culture to gender and sexuality studies, and many more including ones not directly related to his profession.<ref>Roberts, "Popular Culture", p. 1.</ref><ref>{{cite news |first= Jill |last= Rosen |title= 7 Ways Michael Jackson Changed The World |date= June 28, 2009 |newspaper= [[The Baltimore Sun]] |access-date= April 24, 2016 |url= https://www.baltimoresun.com/2009/06/28/7-ways-michael-jackson-changed-the-world/ |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20170130202126/http://articles.baltimoresun.com/2009-06-28/news/0906260178_1_michael-jackson-jackson-changed-jackson-five |archive-date= January 30, 2017 |url-status= live }}</ref> Various life events inspired further discussion while many cultural films, televisions, books reference or depict Jackson on a global scale to present day. According to a study published in ''[[The Journal of Pan African Studies]]'' in 2010, his influence extended to [[academia]], with references to the singer in literature concerning [[mass communications]], [[psychology]], [[medicine]], [[engineering]] and [[chemistry]].<ref name="Chandler">{{cite web |first= Cory |last= Chandler |date= May 20, 2010 |title= Librarians Prove Michael Jackson Was a Rock Star in Academic Literature |url= http://today.ttu.edu/2010/05/librarians-prove-michael-jackson-was-a-rock-star-in-academic-literature |publisher= [[Texas Tech University]] |access-date= May 31, 2015 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20141006123627/http://today.ttu.edu/2010/05/librarians-prove-michael-jackson-was-a-rock-star-in-academic-literature/ |archive-date= October 6, 2014 |url-status= live }}</ref><ref>Hidalgo & Weiner 2010, pp. 14–28.</ref><ref>Hidalgo & Weiner 2010, p. 25.</ref> He inspired a wealth of products exploring his public image, some of which have been displayed, examined or auctioned; an example being reinterpretation by leading artists in the 2018 exhibition ''[[Michael Jackson: On the Wall]]'' at London's [[National Portrait Gallery, London|National Portrait Gallery]]. The [[British Council]] named Jackson on their list of "80 Moments that Shaped the World" with regard to international cultural relations.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.britishcouncil.org/sites/default/files/80-moments-report.pdf|title=80 Moments that Shaped the World|date=2014|website=British Council|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190412111049/https://www.britishcouncil.org/sites/default/files/80-moments-report.pdf|archive-date=April 12, 2019}}</ref> Since Jackson's death, there have been many [[Michael Jackson impersonator|tribute shows]] performed by fans in concert, [[Cirque du Soleil]] or [[Broadway theatre]] which garnered millions of tickets worldwide.
==Performing arts== ===Music=== {{See also|Michael Jackson albums discography|Album era}} [[File:Jackson 5 1969.jpg|thumb|left|upright=0.8|Jackson (center, wearing a hat) with the Jackson 5 in 1969|alt=]] As the lead singer and youngest member of [[the Jackson 5]] from the late 1960s, Michael Jackson was the group's focal point for the media. They became one of the most popular family acts in pop music, with many hit records, [[Jackson 5ive (TV series)|a self-titled cartoon series]] and, from 1976, [[The Jacksons (TV series)|a self-titled variety show]]. He and his brothers were widely viewed as role models for contemporary society; the press celebrated them as a family founded on core religious beliefs and a strong work ethic, and in 1972 the Jackson 5 received a commendation from [[United States Congress|U.S. Congress]] in recognition of the brothers' contribution to American youth culture.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|pp=487–88}} Amid this recognition, according to the editors of ''[[Rolling Stone]]''{{'}}s ''Encyclopedia of Rock & Roll'', Jackson captivated the nation on a level not seen in a child star since [[Shirley Temple]] in the 1930s.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=484}}
{{refn|group=nb|Music academic Jacqueline Edmondson states that "The [Jackson] family haveleft an indelible mark on American popular music and culture through their music, acting, and influence on fashion and pop culture."{{sfn|Edmondson|2013|p=591}}}}
In the description of author and pop culture critic [[Joseph Vogel (author)|Joseph Vogel]], Jackson's album ''[[Thriller (album)|Thriller]]'' (1982) changed the direction of popular music.<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 1438.</ref> [[Jay Cocks]], writing for ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]'' magazine in 1984, said the album was "a thorough restoration of confidence, a rejuvenation [and] its effect on listeners, especially younger ones, was nearer to a revelation". It reintroduced black music to mainstream American radio; until then the so-called "restrictive special-format programming", a genre-driven radio content philosophy which segregated music by race introduced in the mid-1970s, limited airplay of black music. Jackson, whose success was compared to that of Elvis Presley and [[the Beatles]], appeared on the cover of ''Time''.<ref name="Time/Thriller">{{Cite magazine|url=https://time.com/5033766/thriller-michael-jackson-anniversary/|first=Lily|last=Rothman|title=Why Michael Jackson's Biggest Success Was a Surprise|magazine=Time|date=November 29, 2017|access-date=September 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190605134018/http://time.com/5033766/thriller-michael-jackson-anniversary/|archive-date=June 5, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> The album established Jackson as the world's top entertainment star and, in Stanley's recollection, "you'd expect to see a copy in the corner of any room, in any town, in any country in the world."{{sfn|Stanley|2014|p=412}} Its unprecedented commercial success also provided the model for record companies recovering from the economic downturn of the late 1970s, whereby they focused on promoting a blockbuster album at the expense of releases by their lesser-known acts.{{sfn|Cateforis|2011|p=64}} Amid his description of the cultural phenomenon that Jackson represented in 1984, Cocks deemed him "A one-man rescue team for the music business ... A singer who cuts across all boundaries of taste and style, and color too."<ref name="Time">{{cite magazine|first=Jay|last=Cocks|title=Why He's a Thriller|date=March 19, 1984|magazine=[[Time (magazine)|Time]]|url=http://content.time.com/time/magazine/article/0,9171,950053,00.html|access-date=April 25, 2010|archive-date=June 17, 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200617211459/http://content.time.com/time/magazine/article/0,9171,950053,00.html}}</ref> In 2008, the [[Library of Congress]] added ''Thriller'' to the [[National Recording Registry]] for its "stratospheric national and international success".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loc.gov/item/prn-08-079/2007-national-recording-registry-announced/2008-05-14/|title="Thriller" in the Library of Congress: 2007 National Recording Registry Announced|date=May 14, 2008|website=Library of Congress|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191001035945/https://www.loc.gov/item/prn-08-079/2007-national-recording-registry-announced/2008-05-14/|archive-date=October 1, 2019}}</ref>
Jackson's music has been extensively covered by other artists of various styles, including [[Mariah Carey]], [[Miles Davis]], [[Willie Nelson]] and [[Alien Ant Farm]].<ref>{{Cite magazine|url=https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-lists/the-10-best-covers-of-michael-jacksons-songs-20592/|title=The 10 Best Covers of Michael Jackson's Songs|date=June 27, 2014|magazine=Rolling Stone|access-date=October 1, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191001030643/https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-lists/the-10-best-covers-of-michael-jacksons-songs-20592/|archive-date=October 1, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> Artists who often mention Jackson in their music include [[Kanye West]], [[Lil Wayne]], [[LL Cool J]], [[Rick Ross]] and [[Drake (musician)|Drake]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mtv.com/news/2501409/michael-jackson-still-popular-in-rap-songs-4-years-after-his-death/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161129023626/http://www.mtv.com/news/2501409/michael-jackson-still-popular-in-rap-songs-4-years-after-his-death/|archive-date=November 29, 2016|title=Michael Jackson Still A Fixture In Rap Songs 4 Years After Death|author=MTV News Staff|website=MTV News}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2013/06/16/arts/music/kanye-west-talks-about-his-career-and-album-yeezus.html|title=Behind Kanye's Mask|first=Jon|last=Caramanica|date=June 11, 2013|work=The New York Times}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=9m4a71xIWnMC&q=mj+taught+me+that+Lil+wayne&pg=PA323|title=Bruno Mars|publisher=epubli|via=Google Books}}{{Dead link|date=July 2024 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> According to Edmondson, writing in 2013, Jackson "redefined the term pop star" and his cultural legacy is reflected "in the very landscape of the modern, genre-crossing, multimedia pop music scene".{{sfn|Edmondson|2013|p=592}}
===Dance and choreography=== From the start of his performing career, Jackson incorporated dance moves into a stage presence that invited comparison with [[James Brown]], [[Sammy Davis Jr.]], [[Mick Jagger]] and [[Tina Turner]].{{sfn|Doggett|2015|p=425}} He went on to popularize street dances such as [[popping]], [[Locking (dance)|locking]], the [[Robot (dance)|robot]], and his signature move, the [[Moonwalk (dance)|moonwalk]].<ref name="dancer gifts">{{Cite web|url=https://www.nydailynews.com/entertainment/michael-jackson-dancer-moved-measure-gifts-jackson-dance-genius-article-1.373191|title=Michael Jackson the dancer moved us beyond measure; among other gifts, Jackson was dance genius, too|last=Smith|first=Olivia|website=nydailynews.com|date=June 29, 2009 |access-date=October 1, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191001073422/https://www.nydailynews.com/entertainment/michael-jackson-dancer-moved-measure-gifts-jackson-dance-genius-article-1.373191|archive-date=October 1, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> Professor of performance studies at [[New York University|NYU]] Tavia Nyongo said that "No dancer has done as much to popularize the art form since [[Fred Astaire]]."<ref name="dancer gifts"/><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://danceheritage.org/|title=Dance Heritage Coalition - National Alliance Of Institutions|date=January 28, 2020}}</ref>
Jackson first performed the moonwalk when miming to "Billie Jean" at the close of the ''[[Motown 25]]'' television special, which aired in May 1983. The performance was viewed by an international audience of around 50 million and, according to ''Rolling Stone'', "energize[d] the music scene once again and set in motion all the forces that would go on to shape the popular culture of the 1980s".{{sfn|Kooijman|2006|p=119}} Media studies academic Jaap Kooijman writes that, although the moonwalk was an additional element in his routine at ''Motown 25'', Jackson's replication of his dancing from the already popular "Billie Jean" music video presented a new phenomenon whereby a concert performance involved re-creating a video sequence and the music, including live vocals, ceded to visual imagery such as dance.{{sfn|Kooijman|2006|pp=119–20, 126–27}}
Jackson is credited with helping to spread dance to a global audience. [[Nigel Lythgoe]], executive producer and judge on the TV dance competition ''[[So You Think You Can Dance]]'', said that "countless" applicants had begun dancing because of Jackson.<ref name="dancer gifts"/> Ronni Favors, a director at the [[Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater]], said Jackson was "a trailblazer for his generation", setting the expectation that future pop stars, such as [[Britney Spears]] and [[Beyoncé]], integrate dance in their performances.<ref name="dancer gifts" /> In Japan, as a result of him opening his [[Bad World Tour]] there, Jackson is credited with reshaping [[J-pop]]'s choreography.<ref name="auto3">{{Cite web | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=T0sB47TxoCwC&q=michael+Jackson%27s+Bad+tour+is+credited+for+reshaping+J-pop%27s+choreography.&pg=PT18 | title=Billboard| date=July 11, 2009}}</ref> He also influenced India's [[Bollywood]] film scene, where dance sequences, films and soundtrack music all borrowed heavily from Jackson's work.<ref name="auto3"/>
Following the singer's death in 2009, Andy Gill of ''[[The Independent]]'' said that through Jackson's example, "the Eighties became the decade of dance stars like [[Prince (musician)|Prince]] and [[Madonna]], neither of whom would have been able to establish themselves as quickly as they did had Jackson not moonwalked across the room and kicked down the door for them."<ref name="Gill/Independent">{{cite news|first=Andy|last=Gill|title=Michael Jackson: ''Thriller'' was the Masterpiece that Set Tone for Pop's Next Generation|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/music/news/thriller-was-the-masterpiece-that-set-tone-for-pops-next-generation-1721535.html|newspaper=[[The Independent]]|date=June 26, 2009|access-date=March 25, 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150407235307/http://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/music/news/thriller-was-the-masterpiece-that-set-tone-for-pops-next-generation-1721535.html|archive-date=April 7, 2015|url-status=live}}</ref> Gill added that with thousands of dancers imitating his moves in the moonwalk and the zombie, Jackson became "the most significant mainstream dance icon since the mid-century heyday of Fred Astaire, [[Gene Kelly]] and Sammy Davis Jr."<ref name="Gill/Independent" />{{refn|group=nb|In her 2016 novel ''[[Swing Time (novel)|Swing Time]]'', [[Zadie Smith]] refers to Jackson as part of a seamless dance tradition, writing: "A great dancer has no time, no generation, he moves eternally through the world, so that any dancer in any age may recognise him. [[Picasso]] would be incomprehensible to [[Rembrandt]], but [[Nijinsky]] would understand Michael Jackson."<ref name="Searle/Guardian" />}} Jackson was posthumously inducted into the [[National Museum of Dance and Hall of Fame]] in 2010.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rttnews.com/1395755/michael-jackson-inducted-into-dance-hall-of-fame.aspx|title=Michael Jackson Inducted Into Dance Hall Of Fame|website=RTTNews}}</ref>
==Music videos== {{See also|Michael Jackson videography|Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award}} Jackson had a lasting influence on the [[music video]] medium, starting with the clips for his ''Thriller'' singles "Billie Jean" and "Beat It".{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=687}} At the time most music videos had small budgets, low production values and little narrative.<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=9fR8DwAAQBAJ&q=Before+Michael+Jackson%E2%80%99s+thriller,+the+music+video+was+primarily+a+promotional+tool,+featuring+poor+production,+small+budgets+and+little+storytelling.&pg=PA16|title=Man in the Music|first=Joseph|last=Vogel|date=August 27, 2019|publisher=Knopf Doubleday Publishing Group|isbn=978-0-525-56658-8|via=Google Books}}</ref> Jackson's videos began a transformation, replacing low-budget montage promos with elaborate [[short film]]s consisting of in-depth narratives and sophisticated visuals,<ref name="auto">Vogel, (Kindle location) 391.</ref> and taking the form of a mini [[Musical film|musical]].{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=687}} Jackson collaborated with several Hollywood directors on these works, including [[John Landis]], [[Martin Scorsese]], [[Spike Lee]], [[David Fincher]] and [[John Singleton]].<ref name="reuters new standard">{{Cite news|first=Ayala|last=Ben-Yehuda|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-jackson-mtv-idUSTRE56254W20090703|title=Michael Jackson's videos set a new standard|date=July 3, 2009|work=[[Reuters]]|access-date=September 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190930191621/https://www.reuters.com/article/us-jackson-mtv-idUSTRE56254W20090703|archive-date=September 30, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> "Beat It" featured unusually sophisticated choreography and, according to ''The Rolling Stone Encyclopedia of Rock & Roll'', created a "music video subindustry of dancers and choreographers" such as [[Paula Abdul]] and [[Toni Basil]].{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=687}}
The "Thriller" video—which gained a commercial release as ''[[Michael Jackson's Thriller (music video)|Michael Jackson's Thriller]]''—was unusually long (at 14 minutes) and took the form of a short film presentation.{{sfn|Kooijman|2006|p=126}} It features Jackson dancing with [[zombie]]s and cost more than $1 million to produce. The film sealed [[MTV]]'s position as a cultural force, helped disassemble racial barriers for black artists, revolutionized music video production, popularized the making-of documentaries, and drove rentals and sales of VHS tapes.<ref name="Hebblethwaite/Guardian">{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/music/2013/nov/21/michael-jackson-thriller-changed-music-videos|title=How Michael Jackson's Thriller changed music videos for ever|first=Phil|last=Hebblethwaite|date=November 21, 2013|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]|access-date=October 10, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191218125357/https://www.theguardian.com/music/2013/nov/21/michael-jackson-thriller-changed-music-videos|archive-date=December 18, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> It has been described as the most influential music video in history;<ref name="auto"/> according to Edmondson, the "Thriller" video "is credited with single-handedly revolutionizing the landscape of pop music".{{sfn|Edmondson|2013|p=593}} Former MTV executive [[Nina Blackwood]] said, "[After 'Thriller'] we saw videos get more sophisticated—more story lines, way more intricate choreography. You look at those early videos and they were shockingly bad."<ref name="reuters new standard"/> Music video director [[Brian Grant (director)|Brian Grant]] credited "Thriller" as the turning point when music videos became a "proper industry".<ref name="Hebblethwaite/Guardian" /><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-jackson-thriller-idUSTRE56300320090706|title=How Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' changed music business|work=Reuters|date=July 6, 2009|via=www.reuters.com|access-date=October 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191030050326/https://www.reuters.com/article/us-jackson-thriller-idUSTRE56300320090706|archive-date=October 30, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> Gill recognized the Landis-directed film as a work that "altered forever the balance of sound and vision in the entertainment industry", adding: "Prior to Jackson, music alone had been the premier conduit of cultural dissemination among young people; after Jackson, it was merely the accompaniment to a dance routine, one small element in a larger spectacle."<ref name="Gill/Independent" /> In December 2009, the video was inducted into the [[National Film Registry]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/thriller-lands-national-film-registry-92683|title='Thriller' Lands in National Film Registry|website=The Hollywood Reporter|date=December 30, 2009|access-date=September 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190717234808/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/thriller-lands-national-film-registry-92683|archive-date=July 17, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> "Thriller" has become closely associated with [[Halloween]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://dailycollegian.com/2017/10/michael-jacksons-thriller-remains-a-halloween-hit/|title=Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' remains a Halloween hit|last=Clifford|first=Edward|website=Massachusetts Daily Collegian|access-date=September 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190930033054/https://dailycollegian.com/2017/10/michael-jacksons-thriller-remains-a-halloween-hit/|archive-date=September 30, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="vox thriller">{{Cite web|url=https://www.vox.com/2018/10/31/18046940/michael-jackson-thriller-facts-trivia-making-of-john-landis|title=Michael Jackson's "Thriller" is the eternal Halloween bop — and so much more|last=Romano|first=Aja|date=October 31, 2018|website=Vox|access-date=October 4, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191004001542/https://www.vox.com/2018/10/31/18046940/michael-jackson-thriller-facts-trivia-making-of-john-landis|archive-date=October 4, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> The dance is performed in major cities around the world; the largest zombie dance included 12,937 dancers, in Mexico City.<ref name="Griffin">{{cite web|url=https://www.vanityfair.com/culture/2010/07/michael-jackson-thriller-201007|title=The "Thriller" Diaries|last1=Griffin|first1=Nancy|date=January 24, 2010|website=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]]|access-date=October 9, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170621024311/http://www.vanityfair.com/culture/2010/07/michael-jackson-thriller-201007|archive-date=June 21, 2017|url-status=live}}</ref> A [[Thriller (viral video)|YouTube video of more than 1,500 prisoners performing the dance in the Philippines]] had attracted 14 million views as of 2010.<ref name="Griffin" />
Director [[Guillermo del Toro]] cites "Thriller" as an early inspiration. He said, ""I realized that the people actually making these monsters were not the men I saw in the old books in lab coats. It's young guys with long hair and rock 'n' roll shirts, who I could relate to."<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-07-20 |title=Guillermo del Toro Was Inspired By 'Making of Michael Jackson's Thriller' |url=https://www.michaeljackson.com/news/guillermo-del-toro-was-inspired-by-making-of-michael-jacksons-thriller/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240420061225/https://www.michaeljackson.com/news/guillermo-del-toro-was-inspired-by-making-of-michael-jacksons-thriller/ |archive-date=2024-04-20 |access-date=2024-04-20 |website=Michael Jackson Official Site |language=en-US}}</ref>
With MTV's initial broadcast of the film, in December 1983, the debut of a new Jackson video became a major media event.{{sfn|Kooijman|2006|p=126}} MTV's belated support signaled the end of its rock-only policy and, due to the popularity the film gained for the network, of concerns for its commercial survival.{{sfn|Tannenbaum|Marks|2011|pp=144, 227}} [[Robert Pittman (media executive)|Bob Pittman]], MTV's co-founder and CEO, said that "'Thriller' brought people to MTV for the first time, and it made them stay and watch it again and again. Now everybody was into MTV."{{sfn|Tannenbaum|Marks|2011|p=156}} This development ensured an upswing in the economics of the music industry after its sharp decrease in revenue since the late 1970s and, with the expansion of MTV's reach in 1984, new music stars being created through the video medium as well as established acts such as [[Bruce Springsteen]] embracing high-production music videos.{{sfn|Tannenbaum|Marks|2011|p=166}} The ''Rolling Stone'' editors state that Jackson's breakthrough was the "turning point" for MTV, initiating a transformation in which the network "not only revolutionized virtually every aspect of the music business, from promotion to concert tours, but changed the way listeners/viewers related to music and to artists".{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=687}} According to Landis, the "Thriller" video project was the subject of a course at [[Harvard Business School]], although he said this study incorrectly highlighted the role of business and legal professionals rather than Jackson's creative vision.{{sfn|Tannenbaum|Marks|2011|pp=153–154}}
According to ''The Rolling Stone Encyclopedia of Rock & Roll'', the $160,000 budget for Jackson's "Beat It" video was considered an "exorbitant" amount; the video for his 1995 single "[[Scream (Michael Jackson and Janet Jackson song)|Scream]]" cost an estimated $4 million, making it the most expensive clip in pop music history at that time.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|pp=484, 486}} MTV's premiere of Jackson's "[[Black or White]]" single was broadcast simultaneously in 27 countries on November 14, 1991, and was watched by an estimated 500 million people. As of 2006, this remained the largest audience to view a music video.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.today.com/popculture/return-king-pop-wbna15529981|title=The return of the King of Pop|publisher=[[Today (American TV program)|Today.com]]|date=November 3, 2006|access-date=March 31, 2020}}</ref>
== Television appearances == {| class="wikitable sortable" !Date !Broadcast !Region !Total viewers !Type !{{Reference column heading}} |- |September 11, 1971 |[[Jackson 5ive (TV series)|The Jackson 5ive - Episode I]] |[[United States]] |25,000,000 |[[Television show|TV Show]] |<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=iBVKAAAAIBAJ&dq=The+Jackson+5+tour+million+people&pg=PA1&article_id=5919,2225218 |title=Jackson 5 - Neither is serious about one girl yet |date=30 Oct 1971 |publisher=The Afro American |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=c05AAAAAIBAJ&dq=jackson+5+rating&pg=PA5&article_id=4381,1474782 |title=Jackson 5 Animation tops Nielsen Ratings |publisher=Washington Afro-American |language=en |quote=The Show which premiered Saturday Sept. 11 topped the Nielsen ratings for Saturday Morning. The New York overnight rating, revealed it lead with a Audience Share of 48.3%. In Los Angeles it beat it's Competitors with a audience share of 49.3%.}}</ref> |- |May 16, 1983 | ''[[Motown 25: Yesterday, Today, Forever]]'' |[[United States]] |47,000,000 |[[Concert]] |<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-05-24 |title='Motown 25' Was Seen By 47 Million Viewers - NYTimes.com |website=[[The New York Times]] |url=https://www.nytimes.com/1983/05/19/arts/motown-25-was-seen-by-47-million-viewers.html |access-date=2024-02-08 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150524141617/https://www.nytimes.com/1983/05/19/arts/motown-25-was-seen-by-47-million-viewers.html |archive-date=May 24, 2015 }}</ref> |- |February 26, 1984 |[[26th Annual Grammy Awards|26th Annual Grammy Awards (Thriller)]] |United States |51,600,000 |[[Awards ceremony|Award Show]] |<ref>{{Cite magazine |last=Gallo |first=Phil |date=2012-02-13 |title=Grammys Ratings Soar, Nab Second-Largest Audience Ever |url=https://www.billboard.com/music/music-news/grammys-ratings-soar-nab-second-largest-audience-ever-506164/ |access-date=2024-08-16 |magazine=Billboard |language=en-US}}</ref> |- |March 1984 |[[Pepsi Generation|Pepsi: A New Generation]] |Worldwide |500,000,000 |[[Commercial broadcasting|Commercial]] |<ref>{{Cite web |last=Muse |first=Tyler |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Michael Jackson Pepsi |url=https://www.historyoasis.com/post/michael-jackson-pepsi |access-date=2024-03-11 |website=www.historyoasis.com |quote=The universal appeal of Jackson's electrifying performance translated across continents, airing in over 180 countries worldwide. ==Fashion== [[File:Michael Jackson glove (8229092427).jpg|thumb|left|Jackson's rhinestone glove on display at the [[Museum of Pop Culture]] in Seattle, 2011]] {{Multiple image | total_width = 230 | perrow = 2/1 | image1 = Rally IMG 3412.CR2 (30862587485) (cropped).jpg | image2 = George H. W. Bush with Michael Jackson (cropped).png | image3 = Beyonce Super Bowl 50 (b).jpg | footer = [[Lady Gaga]] (top left) wearing Jackson's (top right) jacket from his 1990 visit to the [[White House]], at [[Hillary Clinton]]'s final campaign rally during the [[2016 United States presidential election|2016 U.S. presidential election]]. [[Beyoncé]] (bottom) wearing a Jackson-inspired outfit at [[Super Bowl 50 halftime show]] in 2016. }}
The ''Rolling Stone'' editors describe Jackson as "one of the most intriguing personas (sic) in popular music, at once childlike and obsessed with control" and comment on his ubiquitous presence, "spotlit in his trademark red zippered jacket and white sequined glove".{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|pp=483–84}} Jackson often asked tailors to make him clothes that defied convention.<ref name="vogue 60th">{{Cite web|url=https://www.vogue.co.uk/article/michael-jackson-60th-birthday|title=60 Years Of Michael Jackson, The Fashion Icon|website=British Vogue|date=August 28, 2018|access-date=September 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190930214156/https://www.vogue.co.uk/article/michael-jackson-60th-birthday|archive-date=September 30, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> His defiance led to a notable style that includes<ref name="vogue 60th"/> sequined gloves, a [[fedora]], red leather jackets, sequined jackets, [[aviator sunglasses]], black [[high-water pants|high-waisted pants]], white socks, and leather [[slip-on shoe|penny loafers]].<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 1988.</ref> Jackson was also interested in [[Monarchy of the United Kingdom|British royalty]] and military history, which resulted in his adoption of regalia and [[Full dress uniform|military jackets]].<ref name="vogue 60th"/> His jackets often had a single-colored armband on one sleeve.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.mtv.com/news/1614819/michael-jacksons-style-legacy-from-military-jackets-to-one-glove/|title=Michael Jackson's Style Legacy, From Military Jackets To One Glove|last=Vena|first=Jocelyn|website=MTV News|access-date=September 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190930205643/http://www.mtv.com/news/1614819/michael-jacksons-style-legacy-from-military-jackets-to-one-glove/|archive-date=September 30, 2019}}</ref> At the height of his fame, Jackson inspired fashion trends around the world.<ref name="auto12" /> [[British Vogue|British ''Vogue'']] called him "a fashion pioneer [...] who gave new meaning to moonwalking, immortalised solitary, sparkly gloves, initiated the trophy jacket trend in the Eighties and was brave enough to couple dress with Madonna on the red carpet".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.vogue.co.uk/gallery/michael-jacksons-style-26062009|title=Michael Jackson – A Tribute|website=British Vogue|date=June 26, 2009|access-date=September 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190930212703/https://www.vogue.co.uk/gallery/michael-jacksons-style-26062009|archive-date=September 30, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref>
Others have been influenced by Jackson's fashion sense. In 2012, [[Lady Gaga]] named Jackson as an inspiration. She owns around 400 pieces from his personal collection. In 2016, she wore Jackson's jacket from his 1990 visit to the [[White House]] at [[Hillary Clinton]]'s final campaign rally during the [[2016 United States presidential election|2016 U.S. presidential election]].<ref>{{Cite magazine|url=https://www.billboard.com/articles/news/7572357/lady-gaga-michael-jackson-jacket-hillary-clinton-rally|title=Lady Gaga Wore Michael Jackson's Jacket to Hillary Clinton's Final Rally|magazine=Billboard|date=November 8, 2016|access-date=October 1, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191001000645/https://www.billboard.com/articles/news/7572357/lady-gaga-michael-jackson-jacket-hillary-clinton-rally|archive-date=October 1, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> Also in 2016, Beyoncé honored Michael Jackson at [[Super Bowl 50]] by wearing a Jackson-inspired outfit, a black and gold military jacket similar to the one Jackson wore in his [[Super Bowl XXVII halftime show|Super Bowl halftime show]] in 1993.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/music/news/super-bowl-50-beyonc-paid-tribute-to-michael-jackson-in-halftime-show-a6860501.html |title=Beyoncé honoured Michael Jackson in Super Bowl halftime show |website=[[Independent.co.uk]] |date=February 8, 2016 |access-date=January 6, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170331231702/http://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/music/news/super-bowl-50-beyonc-paid-tribute-to-michael-jackson-in-halftime-show-a6860501.html |archive-date=March 31, 2017 |url-status=live }}</ref>
==Visual arts== [[File:100 Caras del Auditorio de Tenerife, Santa Cruz de Tenerife, España, 2012-12-15, DD 04.jpg|thumb|left|Michael Jackson (bottom-center) was part of the artwork "100 Faces of the Tenerife Auditorium".]] Jackson has been depicted by a large number of contemporary artists, including [[Jeff Koons]], [[Michael Craig-Martin]] and [[Grayson Perry]].<ref name="NPG/OnTheWall">{{Cite web|url=https://www.npg.org.uk/whatson/michael-jackson-on-the-wall/exhibition/|title=Michael Jackson: On the Wall – Exhibition|publisher=[[National Portrait Gallery, London]]|access-date=November 29, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191225015618/https://www.npg.org.uk/whatson/michael-jackson-on-the-wall/exhibition|archive-date=December 25, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> The [[silkscreen]] image of Jackson used on the cover of ''Time'' in 1984 was created by [[Andy Warhol]].<ref name="Time/Thriller" /> The final portrait Jackson commissioned before his death, by [[Kehinde Wiley]], portrayed him as [[Philip II of Spain]] in the manner of a painting by [[Pieter Paul Rubens]].<ref name="Dowd/BBC">{{cite web|first=Vincent|last=Dowd|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-44615890|title=Michael Jackson: On the Wall exhibition explores images of the star|website=[[BBC News]]|date=June 27, 2018|access-date=March 27, 2020}}</ref>
In June 2018, the [[National Portrait Gallery, London|National Portrait Gallery]] in London opened an exhibition titled ''[[Michael Jackson: On the Wall]]'', featuring art inspired by Jackson and created by many leading artists. The curators stated that Jackson was "the most depicted cultural figure in visual art".<ref name="NPG/OnTheWall" /> The exhibition included the Warhol and Wiley pieces, and culminated in a 2005 film by [[Candice Breitz]] in which 16 young people are shown dancing to "Thriller".<ref name="Dowd/BBC" /> ''Guardian'' art critic [[Adrian Searle]] wrote that the singer's eccentricities and the common characterizations of him as "Ariel of the ghetto", a modern-day [[Baudelaire]] and [[Frankenstein's monster]] had provided artists with a wealth of imagery to explore in their work, and that as a muse he was "an inspiration, a model, a tragedy". Dick Zimmerman's portrait of Jackson used for Thriller is cited as "the world's largest distributed portrait in history".<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dick Zimmerman - "His iconic portrait of Michael Jackson used for the famed "Thriller" album cover is still the world's largest distributed portrait in history." |url=https://dickzimmerman.com/ |access-date=2024-02-13 |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Searle/Guardian">{{cite news|first=Adrian|last=Searle|url=https://www.theguardian.com/artanddesign/2018/jun/27/michael-jackson-on-the-wall-review-artists-dream-model-national-portrait-gallery-london|title=Michael Jackson: On the Wall review – king of pop as the ultimate muse|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]|date=June 27, 2018|access-date=March 27, 2020}}</ref>{{refn|group=nb|''Michael Jackson: On the Wall'' was later displayed in Paris, Bonn and Espoo.<ref name="NPG/OnTheWall" />}}
===Sculptures=== {{See also|List of monuments and memorials to Michael Jackson}} [[File:Lage do Michael Jackson, Morro Santa Marta (RJ).JPG|thumb|right|Statue of Michael Jackson in Favela Santa Marta, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil]] Most of statues and sculptures depicting Jackson are monuments dedicated to the music artist located across the world. Notable among them is the statue located in [[Favela Santa Marta]], [[Rio de Janeiro]], whose creation was announced the day after [[Death of Michael Jackson|Jackson's death]] by the mayor of the city, with the intention of dedicating a memorial to the artist.<ref>{{cite web|last=Hennigan|first=Kerry|title=Michael's Lookout – Santa Marta Favela, Rio de Janeiro |website=Michael Jackson World Network |date=July 7, 2013|access-date=July 26, 2023 |url=https://www.mjworld.net/news/2013/07/07/michaels-lookout-santa-marta-favela-rio-de-janeiro/ }}</ref> Another significant example is the plaster and resin [[Statue of Michael Jackson (Fulham F.C.)|sculpture of Jackson]] that stood outside [[Craven Cottage]] in [[Fulham]], [[London]], the ground of [[Fulham Football Club]], from 2011 until 2013, which was later moved to the [[National Football Museum]] in [[Manchester]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/football/27302594 | title=Michael Jackson statue moves to National Football Museum | publisher=[[BBC News]] | date=May 6, 2014 | access-date=July 26, 2023 | archive-date=September 24, 2015 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924204118/http://www.bbc.com/sport/0/football/27302594 }}</ref> Other countries and territories where statues dedicated to Jackson have been unveiled since his death include [[Hong Kong]], [[Russia]], [[Italy]], [[Malaysia]], [[India]], [[China]] and [[Georgia (country)|Georgia]]. Currently, the country with the largest number of statues dedicated to Jackson is China. There are also some statues dating from before Jackson's death, such as the 10-foot sculpture of Jackson by [[Diana Walczak]] depicted on the cover of Jackson's album ''[[HIStory: Past, Present and Future, Book I]]'', currently located in the lobby of the [[Mandalay Bay]] in [[Paradise, Nevada|Paradise]], [[Nevada]].<ref>{{cite news |last=Jones |first=Jay |title=Original Michael Jackson 'HIStory' statue comes to Mandalay Bay in Las Vegas |newspaper=[[Los Angeles Times]] |date=August 12, 2016 |access-date=July 26, 2023 |url=https://www.latimes.com/travel/deals/la-tr-las-vegas-michael-jackson-statue-20160811-snap-story.html}}</ref>
On the other hand, there are other sculptural artworks depicting Jackson that are not monuments, such as the porcelain sculpture ''[[Michael Jackson and Bubbles]]'' by the American artist [[Jeff Koons]]. It was created in 1988 within the framework of Koons's [[Banality (sculpture series)|''Banality'' series]]. Apart from the artist's proof, which is currently on display at [[The Broad]] in [[Los Angeles]], three copies were made; one of them was sold at [[Sotheby's]] on 15 May 2001, when it was auctioned off to the record price of 5.6 million dollars,<ref>{{cite web | url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/1333396.stm | title=Jackson sculpture breaks record | publisher=[[BBC News]] | date=May 16, 2001 | access-date=July 26, 2023}}</ref> and the other two are in the [[Astrup Fearnley Museum of Modern Art]] in [[Oslo]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://afmuseet.no/en/samlingen/utvalgte-kunstnere/k/jeff-koons |title=Astrup Fearnley Museet - Jeff Koons |access-date=July 26, 2023 |archive-date=January 10, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180110054906/http://afmuseet.no/en/samlingen/utvalgte-kunstnere/k/jeff-koons }}</ref> and the [[San Francisco Museum of Modern Art]],<ref>[http://files.shareholder.com/downloads/BID/0x0x103340/875e9a6e-84a4-43a8-8a9a-f6b43c26ece6/20010403-36478.pdf "Sotheby's New York to offer Jeff Koons' ''Michael Jackson and Bubbles'' for the first time at auction"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217225811/http://files.shareholder.com/downloads/BID/0x0x103340/875e9a6e-84a4-43a8-8a9a-f6b43c26ece6/20010403-36478.pdf |date=December 17, 2013 }}, notification of [[Sotheby's]] New York (April 3, 2001). Retrieved April 1, 2013.</ref> respectively.
==Race politics== Early in his career, Jackson and his family were often portrayed in the press as having risen out of black [[Ghetto|ghetto culture]]. This stereotype reinforced their standing as role models for American youth but, as a fabrication by Motown's publicity department, it displeased the family.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=488}} To many African-Americans, his youth and energy were a source of inspiration in the aftermath to [[Martin Luther King Jr.|Martin Luther King]]'s 1968 assassination, at a time when the country's [[Black power movement|black-power]] and [[Civil rights movement|civil rights]] movements were in disarray.<ref name="RS Superhero" /> Jackson became a notable figure in the [[Desegregation in the United States|desegregation]] of US popular culture and music.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://edition.cnn.com/2009/SHOWBIZ/Music/06/28/michael.jackson.black.community/ |title=Michael Jackson broke down racial barriers - CNN.com |access-date=January 5, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160416073636/http://edition.cnn.com/2009/SHOWBIZ/Music/06/28/michael.jackson.black.community |archive-date=April 16, 2016 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="auto2"/> ''Off the Wall'' succeeded at a time when [[disco]] was perceived as inferior to [[Rock music|rock]] by critics.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://pitchfork.com/reviews/albums/21548-off-the-wall/|title=Michael Jackson: Off the Wall|website=Pitchfork|access-date=October 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191030202407/https://pitchfork.com/reviews/albums/21548-off-the-wall/|archive-date=October 30, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/magazines/panache/from-billie-jean-to-beat-it-michael-jacksons-top-5-hits-of-all-time/the-enigma/slideshow/65590190.cms|title=From 'Billie Jean' to 'Beat It': Michael Jackson's Top 5 Hits Of All Time – The Enigma|website=The Economic Times|access-date=October 30, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191121050252/https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/magazines/panache/from-billie-jean-to-beat-it-michael-jacksons-top-5-hits-of-all-time/the-enigma/slideshow/65590190.cms|archive-date=November 21, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> According to Vogel, one of the album's significant achievements was to integrate a diverse collection of talents from different races, cultures, and countries, and to coalesce them seamlessly into the record.<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 1003.</ref>
"Billie Jean" was one of the first music videos by a black artist to be shown on [[MTV]], which hitherto had been a channel directed toward a white, rock-oriented audience.<ref name="Vogel K2018" /> Although the song was already a number one hit on the ''Billboard'' charts,{{sfn|Tannenbaum|Marks|2011|p=143}} MTV initially refused to play the video because of the network's commitment to rock music.<ref name="Vogel K2018" /> When [[CBS Records International|CBS Records]] executive [[Walter Yetnikoff]] threatened to remove all of their products off MTV and expose its discriminatory policies, the network gave in.<ref name="Vogel K2018">Vogel, (Kindle location) 2018.</ref> According to Edmondson, "The video [for 'Thriller'] is often cited as the musical phenomenon that completed the racial integration of popular music begun in the rock 'n' roll era."{{sfn|Edmondson|2013|p=593}}
The success of ''Thriller'' not only broke down racial barriers in music but also in other areas of contemporary society. Critic [[Greg Tate]] said, "Black people cherished ''Thriller''{{'}}s breakthrough as if it were their own battering ram [against] [[apartheid]]."<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 568.</ref> Civil rights activist [[Al Sharpton]] commented, "Way before [[Tiger Woods]] or [[Barack Obama]], Michael made black people go pop-culture global." He also attributed Obama's [[Barack Obama 2008 presidential campaign|presidential win]] to "a process that Michael helped America graduate to" as crossover fans and imitators grew up to become voters.<ref name="RS Superhero">{{Cite magazine|url=https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-news/michael-jackson-black-superhero-71199/|title=Michael Jackson: Black Superhero|author=Touré|date=June 26, 2014|magazine=[[Rolling Stone]]|access-date=March 27, 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190929202432/https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-news/michael-jackson-black-superhero-71199/|archive-date=September 29, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> To some commentators in the 1980s, however, Jackson had betrayed his African-American roots, especially in his musical collaborations with former Beatle [[Paul McCartney]]—a point of criticism that Stanley says Jackson sought to address with his album ''[[Bad (album)|Bad]]'' (1987).{{sfn|Stanley|2014|p=412}}
The video for "Black or White" showed Jackson dancing with dancers of various ethnic groups and traditions, and the lyrics plead for racial tolerance and understanding.<ref>Childs, p. 44.</ref> In early 1993, he launched a $1.25 million program to assist children affected by the [[1992 Los Angeles riots]] and, in a TV interview conducted by [[Oprah Winfrey]], discussed issues related to his African-American heritage and the abuse he suffered under his father. The viewer ratings for the show were among the highest in the history of US television.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=485}}{{refn|group=nb|The broadcast followed Jackson's halftime concert performance at [[Super Bowl XXVII]], in January, which ''Rolling Stone'' describes as "the most widely viewed entertainment event in [US] TV history" up to that time.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=485}}}}
In 1995, "[[They Don't Care About Us]]" was released as one of the singles from ''[[HIStory: Past, Present and Future, Book I|HIStory]]''. In the mid-2010s, the track was used as an anthem for the [[Black Lives Matter]] movement.<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 5472.</ref> Originally recorded as part of the ''[[Dangerous (Michael Jackson album)|Dangerous]]'' sessions, the song was inspired by the [[Rodney King|Rodney King beating]], which had led to the 1992 Los Angeles riots. The lyrics became more personal after Jackson felt dehumanized by the Santa Barbara County police's behavior during the [[1993 Michael Jackson sexual abuse allegations|investigation into the child sexual abuse accusations brought against him in 1993]].<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 5511.</ref>
"They Don't Care About Us" attracted controversy over its supposedly [[Antisemitism|antisemitic]] lyrics. [[Bernard Weinraub]] of ''The New York Times'' cited the lines "''Jew me, sue me / Everybody do me / Kick me, [[kike]] me / Don't you black or white me''" as "pointedly critical of Jews".<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 5479.</ref> Rabbi Abraham Cooper of the [[Simon Wiesenthal Center]] described the lyrics as "deeply disturbing" and potentially harmful to young people.<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 5489.</ref> Jackson issued statements saying that his lyrics were about "the pain of prejudice and hate" and that the song was "a way to draw attention to social and political problems". He described himself as "the voice of the accused and the attacked".<ref>Vogel, (Kindle locations) 5482-5484.</ref> The lyrics were eventually edited out with muffles.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/archive/lifestyle/1995/06/23/michael-jackson-changes-his-tune-on-lyrics/bd138b88-c73c-4e4d-9bd2-5c1d3bf82952/|title=MICHAEL JACKSON CHANGES HIS TUNE ON LYRICS|first=Richard|last=Harrington|date=June 23, 1995|via=www.washingtonpost.com}}</ref>
== Global media == [[File:Websites_interlinking_to_illustrate_PageRank_percents.png|thumb|A PageRank diagram illustrating the process used to determine the most searched terms in the world]] Michael Jackson's impact has been well-documented by global media, [[Statistics|statistical]] lists and entertainment outlet [[op-ed]]s.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Martin |first=Sylvia J. |date=2012-05-01 |title=The Roots and Routes of Michael Jackson's Global Identity |url=https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12115-012-9550-z |journal=Society |language=en |volume=49 |issue=3 |pages=284–290 |doi=10.1007/s12115-012-9550-z |issn=1936-4725}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2004-09-26 |title=Yale group studies Michael Jackson's life |url=https://www.today.com/popculture/yale-group-studies-michael-jacksons-life-wbna6106134 |access-date=2024-12-28 |website=TODAY.com |language=en}}</ref> [[Google Search]] using [[PageRank|PageRank algorithm]] includes Michael Jackson among the 100 most Googled terms ever between 2003 to 2022, being one of a few persons to be included on the list along with technology and natural events.<ref name=":42">{{Cite web |title=Google turns 25: A look at the world's top performing searches |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/9/4/google-turns-25-a-look-at-the-worlds-top-performing-searches |access-date=2024-12-24 |website=Al Jazeera |language=en}}</ref> Branded titles including but not limited to "The King of Pop", "Biggest Pop Star" factor him as one of the most influential musical artists based on his sales, impact and acclaim.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Brown |first=Stacy M. |date=2024-11-29 |title=Michael Jackson's Thriller: A Legacy That Continues to Dominate Music and Culture |url=https://d-mars.com/michael-jacksons-thriller-a-legacy-that-continues-to-dominate-music-and-culture/ |access-date=2024-12-28 |website=d-mars.com by Stacy M. Brown, NNPA Newswire Senior National Correspondent |language=en-US}}</ref> Particular descriptions by economic researchers and cultural journalists have also been detailed in various countries. Such titles, rankings and achievements in both English and non-English languages include:

=== Public poll or [[listicle]] === {{See also|List of awards and nominations received by Michael Jackson#Listicle}}
== Impact by country == {{See also|Philanthropy of Michael Jackson|Death of Michael Jackson#International}}Regarded as one of the most famous figures in modern history, Jackson had a major impact, both culturally and politically in various countries.<ref name=":34">{{Cite book |last= |first= |url= |title=Billboard Magazine: The King of Pop edition (page 18 - We are the World article) |date=2009-07-11 |publisher=Nielsen Business Media, Inc. |isbn= |edition=July 11, 2009 |publication-date=2009-07-11 |pages=18–19 |language=English |oclc=732913734 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Michael Jackson: World reacts |url=https://www.houmatoday.com/picture-gallery/news/local/2009/06/26/michael-jackson-world-reacts/786725007/ |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=The Courier |language=en-US}}</ref> Ben Beaumont-Thomas, music editor for ''[[The Guardian]]'', said Jackson "ushered in a global culture" and that his impact extended into "areas previously untouched by Western pop culture".<ref name="theguardian2">{{cite news |title=Michael Jackson: Five years after his death, how his influence lives on |url=https://www.theguardian.com/music/2014/jun/25/five-ways-michael-jackson-influence-lives-on?_e_pi_=7%2CPAGE_ID10%2C3576005891 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171201031812/https://www.theguardian.com/music/2014/jun/25/five-ways-michael-jackson-influence-lives-on?_e_pi_=7%2CPAGE_ID10%2C3576005891 |archive-date=December 1, 2017 |access-date=July 11, 2017 |work=[[The Guardian]] }}</ref> In an [[Ethnography|ethnographic]] portrait collection on the [[subculture]] of his tribute fans, writer Lorena Turner states his "ability to transcend race, gender and a wide range of cultural stigmas".<ref>{{Cite book |last=Turner |first=Lorena |title=The Michael Jacksons: Ethnographic Monograph |date=2014-08-16 |publisher=Little Moth Press |isbn=978-1-62890-955-5 |edition=1st |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Garrett |first=Jenna |date=2014-01-07 |title=Fascinating Portraits of Michael Jackson Impersonators |url=https://www.featureshoot.com/2014/01/fascinating-portraits-of-michael-jackson-impersonators/ |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=Feature Shoot |language=en-US}}</ref> His moniker "King of Pop" is accepted and used in media by other countries.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=483}} Particularly during tours, Jackson additionally held a [[Foreign policy|foreign affairs]] role, holding meetings with prime ministers, royalty and presidents as well as part of his concert proceeds funneling to orphanages of the country visited.<ref name=":33">{{Cite web |title=Politics-Weary Romanians Welcome Michael Jackson To 'Relieve Tension' {{!}} The Seattle Times |url=https://archive.seattletimes.com/archive/19920930/1515867/politics-weary-romanians-welcome-michael-jackson-to-relieve-tension |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=archive.seattletimes.com}}</ref> [[The Sydney Morning Herald]]'s political editor [[Peter Hartcher]] compared his 2009 death a "remarkable moment of worldwide political and popular unity is a reminder of the immensity of American soft power".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hartcher |first=Peter |date=2009-06-29 |title=Soft power: Jackson and a new anthem for American politics |url=https://www.smh.com.au/politics/federal/soft-power-jackson-and-a-new-anthem-for-american-politics-20090629-d2ek.html |access-date=2025-01-24 |website=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |language=en}}</ref> Over the years, journalists have detailed his unifying ability as a cultural figure:{{Blockquote|text=The reason Michael mattered — continues to matter — is because he was one of the first truly international stars. Not just transatlantic, not just big in [[Japan]]: He was global. The obvious effect was economic. Michael opened markets around the world. It is Michael Jackson selling records — but it is also a stall in [[Malaysia]] selling bootlegged Michael Jackson tapes and T-shirts [...] It is a flea market in [[South Africa]] where they sell framed illustrations of Michael Jackson and Jesus. [...] Last night, impersonators massed in [[Mexico City]]. In [[London]], plans for a mob moonwalk this evening. In my neighborhood in [[New York City|New York]], Michael wafts out of every car, from every window. In [[Taiwan]], an inordinate number of kids are named "Michael" or "Jackson." Packets of information, traveling the globe.|author=[[Pulitzer Prize]]-winning Cultural writer [[Hua Hsu]]|title=''Michael Jackson: He Was The World''<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hsu |first=Hua |date=2009-06-29 |title=Foreign Policy: Michael Jackson: He Was The World |work=NPR |url=https://www.npr.org/2009/06/29/106036486/foreign-policy-michael-jackson-he-was-the-world |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250113234229/https://www.npr.org/2009/06/29/106036486/foreign-policy-michael-jackson-he-was-the-world |archive-date=2025-01-13}}</ref>|style=width: 80%; font-size:small; background-color: #E8E9EB|source=|multiline=true}}Furthermore, the [[United States House Committee on Foreign Affairs]] during the [[111th United States Congress|111th Congress]] was proposed a resolution to honor Michael Jackson's global [[Humanitarianism|humanitarian]] contributions. The resolution detailed over 40 spearhead efforts of Jackson in the country and abroad which include [[cancer research]], [[Terminal illness|terminally-ill]] funding, education, [[immunization]] grants, [[anti-racism]] campaigns and global hunger charities.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jackson-Lee |first=Sheila |date=2009-06-26 |title=Text - H.Res.600 - 111th Congress (2009-2010): Honoring an American Legend and Musical Icon. |url=https://www.congress.gov/bill/111th-congress/house-resolution/600/text |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=www.congress.gov}}</ref>
=== United Kingdom === In [[United Kingdom]] history, ''Thriller'' and ''Bad'' are the two highest-selling albums by a male solo artist.<ref name=":29">{{cite web |title=Record-Breakers and Trivia – Albums |url=http://www.everyhit.com/recordalb.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303205902/http://www.everyhit.com/recordalb.html |archive-date=March 3, 2016 |access-date=June 25, 2010 |publisher=everyHit.com}}</ref> Only Jackson and rock band [[Queen (band)|Queen]] have multiple entries in the top ten.<ref name=":29" /> His [[Bad (tour)|Bad tour]] once held London's [[Wembley Stadium]] record of 504,000 attendees over seven nights until [[Taylor Swift]] was reported to beat the record 36 years later for her eight nights at Wembley for [[The Eras Tour|The Eras tour]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Michael Jackson's Record-Breaking Wembley Stadium Performances Relived |url=https://www.juliensauctions.com/en/articles/michael-jackson-wembley-stadium-performances-relived |access-date=2025-01-12 |website=Julien's Auctions |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Ibarra |first=Dominoe |date=2024-08-21 |title=Taylor Swift Breaks Michael Jackson's Wembley Record, Sings "So Long, London" as UK Eras Tour Ends {{!}} THR News Video |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/video/taylor-swift-breaks-michael-jackson-wembley-record-sings-so-long-london-uk-eras-tour-ends/ |access-date=2025-01-12 |website=The Hollywood Reporter |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last= |date=2024-08-21 |title=Taylor Swift breaks Michael Jackson's Wembley Stadium record |url=https://www.movin925.com/taylor-swift-breaks-michael-jacksons-wembley-stadium-record/ |access-date=2025-01-12 |website=MOViN 92.5 |language=en-US}}</ref> Reports stated Wembley's ticket demand for Jackson's dates exceeded 1.5 million, enough to fill the 72,000-capacity venue 20 times, though the number of nights were strictly kept as the venue reached their quota length for security maintenance.<ref name="camp216">Campbell 1993, p. 216.</ref> To date, Jackson holds the record for most sold out events at Wembley with 15 sold out concerts over his career.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2009-08-15 |title=Wembley record 'broken by U2 gig' |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/8202230.stm |access-date=2025-01-31 |work=[[BBC]] |language=en-GB |quote=}}</ref> Comparably in [[Liverpool]] at [[Aintree Racecourse]], reported amongst [[List of most-attended concert tours|most-attended concerts in history]] with 125,000 fans, of which 1,550 fans were reported having minor [[Major trauma|injuries]] due to uncontrollable crowd, with an estimated 31 to 40 people requiring hospitalization.<ref>{{Cite web |last= |first= |date=2019-04-21 |title=Michael Jackson 'Bad' Sep 11, 88, Aintree Gig Pre-Hillsborough Cover-Up |url=https://enchantedlifepath.com/2019/04/21/michael-jackson-bad-gig-crush-aintree-88/ |access-date=2025-01-16 |website=Enchanted LifePath |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="hals80">Halstead 2003, p. 80.</ref><ref>{{cite news |date=September 12, 1988 |title=1,550 injured at Jackson concert |url=https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=eN8NAAAAIBAJ&dq=michael%20jackson%20tour&pg=5265%2C3398932 |access-date=September 30, 2010 |work=Pittsburgh Post-Gazette}}{{Dead link|date=November 2023|bot=InternetArchiveBot|fix-attempted=yes}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Archives |first=L. A. Times |date=1988-09-12 |title=1,550 Hurt in Crush at Jackson Show |url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-1988-09-12-mn-1359-story.html |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=Los Angeles Times |language=en-US}}</ref> This was despite Merseyside Police drafting in 500 extra officers and a first aid team for the event, though no deaths were reported.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Williams |first=Olivia |date=2023-09-11 |title=Night Michael Jackson performed to 125,000 people at Aintree |url=https://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/news/nostalgia/gallery/night-michael-jackson-performed-125000-27690606 |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=Liverpool Echo |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Barton |first=Phoebe |date=2022-09-12 |title=Michael Jackson hosted 'iconic' night in Aintree with 125k people |url=https://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/news/nostalgia/michael-jackson-hosted-historic-night-24906517 |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=Liverpool Echo |language=en}}</ref> His 2009 ''[[This Is It (concert residency)|This Is It]]'' concert residency was to be held exclusively at [[The O2 Arena]] in London sold out of 750,000 tickets within 4 hours, to which organizers described to be "a cultural phenomenon".<ref name="cnn">{{cite web |title=750,000 tickets for Jackson concerts sell in 4 hours - CNN.com |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2009/SHOWBIZ/Music/03/13/michael.jackson.concerts.tickets/index.html}}</ref>
=== Germany === [[File:Bundesarchiv_B_145_Bild-F079012-0030,_Berlin,_Michael_Jackson-Konzert,_Wartende.jpg|thumb|German fans lining up for the Bad tour in 1988]] During the last four years of the [[Cold War]], Jackson's popularity in [[Germany]] was notable in that Jackson's visit to Berlin for his 1988 Bad tour held immense protocols. As the city was [[New states of Germany|physically divided]], fans attempted to watch or hear the June 19 concert over the wall, either from behind the [[Brandenburg Gate]] area or the nearby [[Charité|Charite hospital]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-07-30 |title=East German Police Feared 1988 Jackson Concert |url=https://www.cbs8.com/article/entertainment/east-german-police-feared-1988-jackson-concert/509-f413b9ee-d581-4d76-850e-79a4c00b2c08 |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=cbs8.com |language=en-US}}</ref> An infamous [[Stasi]] file was kept of Jackson,<ref>{{Cite magazine |last=Boston |first=William |date=2009-08-05 |title=The Stasi File on Michael Jackson |url=https://time.com/archive/6910225/the-stasi-file-on-michael-jackson/ |access-date=2025-01-13 |magazine=TIME |language=en}}</ref> detailing the secret police deemed the visit a possible perceived threat,<ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-07-30 |title=Revealed: how Stasi tackled Jackson 'threat' |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/europe/revealed-how-stasi-tackled-jackson-threat-1764948.html |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=The Independent |language=en}}</ref> as an outlet described the police had fear of his potential to inspire and mobilize.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Connolly |first=Kate |date=2009-07-30 |title=Stasi file details plans for riot during 1988 Michael Jackson concert |url=https://www.theguardian.com/music/2009/jul/30/michael-jackson-berlin-wall-germany |access-date=2025-01-13 |work=[[The Guardian]] |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Copley |first=Caroline |date=2009-07-30 |title=Stasi worried about 1988 Jackson concert |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/business-news/stasi-worried-1988-jackson-concert-87142/ |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=The Hollywood Reporter |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name=":30">{{Cite web |date=2024-06-19 |title=When the King of Pop Defied the Stasi: Michael Jackson's 1988 Berlin Performance |url=https://pmamagazine.org/when-the-king-of-pop-defied-the-stasi-jacksons-berlin-performance/ |access-date=2025-01-12 |website=PMA Magazine |language=en}}</ref> Fearing the concert could cause rebellion if Jackson were to make any politically sensitive comments, they methodized [[censorship]].
