A [[look-alike]] during his [[Sat.1|SAT 1]] conference was hired,<ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-08-05 |title=Germany's Stasi fooled in 1988 by Jackson double |url=https://www.twincities.com/2009/08/05/germanys-stasi-fooled-in-1988-by-jackson-double/ |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=Twin Cities |language=en-US}}</ref> and a two-minute [[broadcast delay]] would be put in place in which they could transfer to a pre-recording.<ref name=":30" /> At one point, Jackson famously pointed towards the Wall and declared, "This is for all the fans over there in East Berlin!" to which a PMA journalist expressed, "In the end, Jackson's [[Berlin Wall]] concert stands as a testament to the transformative power of art and the enduring human spirit. It's a vivid reminder that even in the darkest of times, a song can light up the night, and a performance can inspire change."<ref name=":30" /> German newspaper ''[[Der Spiegel]]'' called Jackson "Deutschland's King of Pop" for his unique impact on the country's culture.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Kloth |first=Hans Michael |date=2009-06-29 |title=Deutschland's King of Pop: Michael Jackson's Secret Soft Spot for Germany |url=https://www.spiegel.de/international/germany/deutschland-s-king-of-pop-michael-jackson-s-secret-soft-spot-for-germany-a-633187.html |access-date=2025-01-13 |work=Der Spiegel |language=en |issn=2195-1349}}</ref>
=== Africa === Jackson first visited Africa in 1974 with the Jackson 5, a visit that inspired his ''Bad'' single "[[Liberian Girl]]".<ref name="Shields/OkayAfrica2">{{Cite web |last=Shields |first=Derica |date=August 29, 2013 |title=Michael Jackson In Africa |url=https://www.okayafrica.com/michael-jackson-in-africa/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191003233100/https://www.okayafrica.com/michael-jackson-in-africa/ |archive-date=October 3, 2019 |access-date=October 3, 2019 |website=[[OkayAfrica]]}}</ref> Along with Jackson compositions such as "[[Heal the World]]" and "[[We Are the World]]", fans from [[Liberia]] found the song uplifting and his message resonated there in a climate of [[First Liberian Civil War|civil war]] and [[List of war crimes#1990–2000: Liberia / Sierra Leone|human atrocities]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=July 2, 2009 |title=Memory of Michael Jackson uplifts Liberia |url=https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2009/jul/2/king-of-pop-uplifts-liberia/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191003233059/https://www.washingtontimes.com/news/2009/jul/2/king-of-pop-uplifts-liberia/ |archive-date=October 3, 2019 |access-date=March 31, 2020 |website=[[The Washington Times]]}}</ref> By 1992, Jackson carried out a multi-nation African cultural tour to [[Tunisia]], [[Gabon]], [[Ivory Coast]], [[Senegal]], [[Tanzania]], [[Egypt]] and [[South Africa]],{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=485}} intent on visiting "orphanages, children's hospitals, churches, schools and playgrounds".<ref name="Shields/OkayAfrica2" /> Jackson's trip was a huge success as his visit drew more spectators in Gabon than [[Nelson Mandela]] and more in the Ivory Coast than the Pope, "From his sunset arrival in Gabon, where more than 100,000 people greeted him with spiritual bedlam".<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Documentary, Michael Jackson: The Thrill Of Thriller |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p00zfxhf |access-date=2024-02-13 |website=BBC World Service |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite periodical |last=Johnson |first=Robert E. |date=May 1992 |title=Michael Jackson: Crowned in Africa, pop music king tells real story of controversial trip |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tMwDAAAAMBAJ&pg=PA34 |magazine=Ebony |publisher=Johnson Publishing Company |pages=34–36, 40, 42–43 |language=en |volume=47 |number=7}}</ref> While in [[Ivory Coast]], he was crowned king of the [[Anyi people|Agni people]] in the [[Kingdom of Sanwi]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Raga |first=Suzanne |date=June 25, 2018 |title=When King of Pop Michael Jackson Was Officially Crowned King of a Small African Nation |url=http://mentalfloss.com/article/528931/when-michael-jackson-was-crowned-king-a-small-african-nation |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191003233100/http://mentalfloss.com/article/528931/when-michael-jackson-was-crowned-king-a-small-african-nation |archive-date=October 3, 2019 |access-date=October 3, 2019 |website=[[Mental Floss]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Pflanz |first=Mike |date=August 3, 2009 |title=Royal Ivory Coast funeral for tribe 'prince' Michael Jackson |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/music/michael-jackson/5965126/Royal-Ivory-Coast-funeral-for-tribe-prince-Michael-Jackson.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191119173217/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/music/michael-jackson/5965126/Royal-Ivory-Coast-funeral-for-tribe-prince-Michael-Jackson.html |archive-date=November 19, 2019 |access-date=October 31, 2019 |via=www.telegraph.co.uk}}</ref> Later that year, he established the [[Heal the World Foundation]] to raise awareness of social issues related to children.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=485}}
Until 1994, with the fall of [[Apartheid|apartheid in South Africa]], Jackson was the only artist whose songs were played on white pop stations and black R&B stations there. According to [[Metro FM]] presenter Lupi Ngcayisa, his lyrics "forced black families to debate issues surrounding individualism and race".<ref name="auto12">{{Cite web |author=Billboard (New York) |date=July 4, 2009 |title=Michael Jackson's music had impact around the globe |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-jackson-global-sb/michael-jacksons-music-had-impact-around-the-globe-idUSTRE5624OT20090704 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191007063232/https://www.reuters.com/article/us-jackson-global-sb/michael-jacksons-music-had-impact-around-the-globe-idUSTRE5624OT20090704 |archive-date=October 7, 2019 |access-date=January 6, 2020 |work=[[Reuters]]}}</ref> South African R&B artist Loyiso Bala, of [[the Bala Brothers]], likened Jackson's impact to Mandela and said that as a black boy growing up in a township, "you either wanted to be Michael Jackson or a [[freedom fighter]]".<ref name="auto12" /> [[NPR]]'s Africa correspondent, [[Ofeibea Quist-Arcton]] said: <blockquote>Africans all across the continent are mourning the loss. [...] A [[Kenya]]n woman, with a tremor in her voice, asked how a legend dared die, because they were not supposed to die. [...] Across the continent in [[West Africa]], a [[Nigerians|Nigerian]] radio host reportedly broke down live on air. [...] But, mostly, Africans are mourning the man whose dazzling dance steps and musical genius are likely to be Jackson's lasting legacy.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Quist-Arcton |first=Ofeibea |date=2009-06-26 |title=Africa Mourns The King Of Pop |url=https://www.npr.org/2009/06/26/105959193/africa-mourns-the-king-of-pop |website=NPR}}</ref></blockquote> Michael is said to remain a consistently popular figure in Africa with stories of his fame spread across the continent.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Michael Jackson continua vivo em São Tomé e Príncipe |url=https://www.uol.com.br/carnaval/videos/?id=michael-jackson-continua-vivo-em-sao-tome-e-principe-0402CC1C3764C8892326 |access-date=2024-07-25 |website=www.uol.com.br |language=pt-br |quote=The 'King of Pop' is still alive in a remote and unknown location on the planet: on the small African islands of São Tomé and Príncipe. Damião Mendes Marques is known only as 'Michael' and became a local celebrity. Always dressed as his idol, he attracts crowds with his performance and dreams one day of being able to meet the Jackson family.}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last1=Voice |first1=Malawi |date=2023-05-10 |title=Micheal Jackson Tombstone 'For Sale' in Malawi |url=https://www.malawivoice.com/2023/05/10/micheal-jackson-tombstone-for-sale-in-malawi/ |access-date=2024-07-25 |website=Malawi Voice |language=en-US |quote=Years after his burial an identified Malawian man has crafted a tombstone with Micheal Jackson's biography.}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-08-04 |title=Experiencing Beira, Mozambique |url=https://serve.ie/experiencing-beira-mozambique/ |access-date=2024-07-25 |website=SERVE |language=en-GB |quote=While staying at Young Africa I've met some of the most genuine, welcoming and all around amazing people. From the moment we started work here we've been welcomed with a smile and friendly "Hola", we've been invited into English classes and been taught Portuguese, we've shared stories and jokes with the students through their broken English and our broken Portuguese and I've even been taught to dance to Michael Jackson's "Thriller".}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Uganda Dance Troupe Dances To MJ's 'Smooth Criminal' |url=https://www.michaeljackson.com/en-ca/news/uganda-dance-troupe-dances-to-mjs-smooth-criminal/ |access-date=2024-07-25 |website=Michael Jackson Official Site |language=en-ca}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2017-11-13 |title=A Madagascar, la réincarnation de Michael Jackson provoque une vague de nostalgie - Tiasy "Pourquoi tu ne suis pas les rangs comme tout le monde?" |url=https://tiasyraconte.mondoblog.org/2017/11/13/a-madagascar-reincarnation-michael-jackson-nostalgie/ |access-date=2024-07-25 |website=Tiasy |language=fr-FR |quote=It's like we're watching a real live of the late singer -- although I've never seen live live, but only on television. The audience, made up of both young and adult, was literally in a trance, especially during the interpretation of Thriller"}}</ref> In 2016, [[John Mahama]], then President of [[Ghana]], referred to "Heal the World" in a speech at the [[United Nations General Assembly]] to encourage globalization and acceptance of refugees, and to denounce [[xenophobia]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Irish |first=John |date=September 21, 2016 |title=Ghana leader tells U.N. to dance to tune of Michael Jackson |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/us-un-assembly-ghana-idUSKCN11R2X6 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191003233058/https://www.reuters.com/article/us-un-assembly-ghana-idUSKCN11R2X6 |archive-date=October 3, 2019 |access-date=October 3, 2019 |work=Reuters}}</ref>
=== South America === [[File:From_Venezuela_(3666773302).jpg|left|thumb|Note to Jackson from a Venezuelan fan]] Before the existence of Brazilian music certification in 1990, Jackson is among [[List of best-selling albums in Brazil#By claimed sales|Brazil's best selling albums by international artists]] with only Jackson and singer [[Julio Iglesias]] holding claimed sales over 2 million.<ref name="BrazilSalesMJ">{{cite journal |last1=Fernandez |first1=Alexandre |date=August 29, 2008 |title=Michael Jackson: 50 anos |url=http://memoria.bn.gov.br/DocReader/893978/94473 |journal=Expresso Popular |language=pt |edition=2.321 |page=24 |access-date=July 8, 2024}}</ref> In 1996, Jackson visited [[Favela Santa Marta|Santa Marta]] in [[Rio de Janeiro|Rio de Janeiro, Brazil]] to film with director [[Spike Lee]] one of the videos for "[[They Don't Care About Us]]". It was intended to bringing global attention to the social issues faced by residents of these [[Poverty|impoverished communities]].<ref>{{Cite web |title="Michael, Eles não ligam pra gente" |url=https://tiltingfutures.org/updates/michael-eles-nao-ligam-pra-gente/ |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=Tilting Futures |language=en-US}}</ref> Initially, Rio's local government was concerned that Jackson would show the world an unflattering picture of poverty, which might affect [[tourism]], and accused Jackson of exploiting the poor. Others supported Jackson's wish to bring public awareness to problems of the region, arguing that the government was embarrassed by its own failings.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Deadspin {{!}} I Found Michael Jackson In A Brazilian Favela |url=https://deadspin.com/i-found-michael-jackson-in-a-brazilian-favela-1586673299/ |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=deadspin.com |language=en}}</ref> A judge [[Ban (law)|banned]] all filming but this ruling was overturned by an [[injunction]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Reuters Archive Licensing |url=https://reuters.screenocean.com/record/385197 |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=Reuters Archive Licensing |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":31">{{Cite web |last=Bylund |first=Lynnea |date=2013-06-28 |title=They Don't Care About Us: Ten Years Later |url=https://www.catalysthouse.net/they-dont-care-about-us-ten-years-later/ |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=Catalyst House |language=en}}</ref> Jackson performed with the [[Olodum]], which runs an inner-city school for Salvador's underprivileged children centered to build self-esteem and encourage economic ascension.<ref name=":31" /> Speaking of the music video in ''The New Brazilian Cinema'', Lúcia Nagib commented: <blockquote>He used the [[favela]] people as extras in a visual super-spectacle [...]. The interesting aspect of Michael Jackson's strategy is the efficiency with which it gives visibility to poverty and social problems in countries like Brazil without resorting to traditional political discourse. The problematic aspect is that it does not entail a real intervention in that poverty.<ref>{{cite book |last=Nagib |first=Lúcia |url=https://archive.org/details/newbraziliancine00nagi_659 |title=The New Brazilian Cinema |publisher=I.B.Tauris |year=2003 |isbn=1-86064-928-9 |page=[https://archive.org/details/newbraziliancine00nagi_659/page/n151 123] |url-access=limited}}</ref></blockquote> In 2009, [[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]] described the area as "now a model for social development" and claimed that Jackson's influence was partially responsible for this improvement.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=485}} Because of his immense popularity, he has received both governmental and fan tributes in [[Spanish language|Spanish]]-speaking countries such as [[Chile]], who gave him virtual presidential honors on his 1993 visit to the country<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dangerous' tour arrives in Chile - UPI Archives |url=https://www.upi.com/Archives/1993/10/18/Dangerous-tour-arrives-in-Chile/5765750916800/ |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=UPI |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-11-03 |title=Hundreds of Michael Jackson fans celebrate with a massive choreography in Chile |url=https://ca.news.yahoo.com/hundreds-michael-jackson-fans-celebrate-124147956.html |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=Yahoo News |language=en-CA}}</ref><ref>{{Cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GElZISSVYtA |title=Hundreds of Michael Jackson fans celebrate with a massive choreography in Chile |date=2024-11-07 |last=AP Archive |access-date=2025-01-13 |via=YouTube}}</ref> [[Argentina]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=BIllboard - El día que Michael Jackson conquistó Argentina en 1993 |url=https://www.billboard.ar/information/post/el-dia-que-michael-jackson-conquisto-argentina-en-1993 |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=www.billboard.ar |date=June 25, 2024 |language=es}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last1=Nicas |first1=Jack |last2=Serra |first2=Anita Pouchard |date=2022-09-22 |title=2 Michael Jackson Impersonators Look Identical. The Resemblance Ends There. |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2022/09/22/world/americas/michael-jackson-impersonators-buenos-aires.html |access-date=2025-01-13 |work=The New York Times |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}}</ref> and [[Peru]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=World Lens: The World Reacts to M.J.'s Death |url=https://www.bet.com/photo-gallery/qpoa28/world-lens-the-world-reacts-to-m-j-s-death/0brt77 |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=BET |language=en}}</ref> For Spanish fans, he then released the single [[I Just Can't Stop Loving You|Todo mi amor eres tú]]' (You are all my love)." In 2015, a Latin project was released for Michael Jackson, ''[[Unity: The Latin Tribute to Michael Jackson]]''.<ref>Unity, [http://www.unitylatintribute.com/blog/news Newsroom] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130605163510/http://www.unitylatintribute.com/blog/news |date=June 5, 2013 }}. Retrieved June 4, 2013.</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=October 7, 2015 |title=Unity - the Latin Tribute to Michael Jackson |url=https://www.kpbs.org/news/2015/oct/07/unity-latin-tribute-michael-jackson/}}</ref>
=== India === {{Blockquote|text=Rock may have smashed the iron curtain, but it was just the moonwalk that did it for India [...] Michael Jackson was not just a pop star for us; he represented the world beyond India we had only heard about as well as the possibility of catching up with it. Michael Jackson was the first symbol of aspiration for a generation that went from denial to obsession about it almost overnight. In the 1980s, bootleg VHS copies of Thriller went from home to home, even as we sought to work hard and study and buy into the first signs of consumerism that had started to appear. By the 1990s, with economic liberalization and the rise of satellite television channels like MTV India and Channel V, Michael Jackson, his music, image, and charisma all became a part of India, like globalization itself.|author=[[Vamsee Juluri]], Professor at [[University of San Francisco]]|title=''The Dawn of Global India''|source=[[Huffington Post]]<ref name=india/>|style=width: 80%; font-size:small; background-color: #E8E9EB}}By 1983, Jackson became the representative global music star of [[India]].<ref>{{Cite web |last= |first= |title=India New England Aug 2006 |url=https://www.umassd.edu/indic/faculty-and-staff/director/india-new-england-aug-2006/ |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=www.umassd.edu |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Listen: Dance Professor Pallabi Chakravorty on Indian Reality Show and Bollywood Dancers :: News & Events :: Swarthmore College |url=https://www.swarthmore.edu/news-events/listen-dance-professor-pallabi-chakravorty-indian-reality-show-and-bollywood-dancers |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=www.swarthmore.edu}}</ref> Culture journalists such as Vamsee Juluri,<ref name=india>{{Cite web |last=Juluri |first=Vamsee |date=2009-07-28 |title=Michael Jackson and the Dawn of Global India |url=https://www.huffpost.com/entry/michael-jackson-and-the-d_b_221853 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240707073838/https://www.huffpost.com/entry/michael-jackson-and-the-d_b_221853 |archive-date=2024-07-07 |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=HuffPost |language=en}}</ref> and [[Kamla Bhatt]] have agreed he was a "huge influence on [[Hindi cinema|Bollywood]] and Indian film industry."<ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-06-26 |title=India: Michael Jackson's Influence On Bollywood |url=https://globalvoices.org/2009/06/26/india-michael-jacksons-influence-on-bollywood/ |access-date=2025-01-13 |website=Global Voices |language=en}}</ref> ''Billboard'' noted, <blockquote>For many people in India — a market where international repertoire accounts for just 5% of physical music sales — Michael Jackson is [[Western world|Western pop]]. Alone among Western artists, his popularity isn't confined to English-speaking urban Indians. Among the country's rural youth his celebrity competes with Bollywood stars for one reason: his trademark dance moves.{{sfn|Romanowski|George-Warren|1995|p=485}}</blockquote> A country drawing influence from Jackson, several movies have been inspired directly by him or have him as a central theme such as ''[[Munna Michael]]'', ''[[ABCD: Any Body Can Dance]]'' and ''[[Donga (film)|Donga]]''.
India's [[entertainment tax]] was [[Tax withholding|waived]] by the ruling [[Shiv Sena]] just for Jackson to perform in the country.<ref name="bbcindia">{{Cite news |date=2021-01-08 |title=A Michael Jackson concert in India that's now tax-free |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-india-55556526 |access-date=2025-01-17 |language=en-GB}}</ref> Jackson visited India in 1996 during his [[HIStory World Tour|History World Tour]] where he was greeted by thousands of fans with the media noting that his security force larger than that of the [[Prime minister|Prime Minister]].<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=SiRUAAAAIBAJ&dq=michael+jackson+india+thousands&pg=PA2&article_id=5118,343799 |title=Thousands of Police guard Michael Jackson's concert |date=2 Nov 1996 |publisher=The News |language=en |quote=Surrounded by more Police officers (6,500+) than India's prime minister, Michael Jackson worked the crowd into a frenzy.}}</ref> A journalist told ''[[Condé Nast Traveler]]'', "These were the days when noise control norms were still lax, and one could hear his music for miles. People were going nuts - they were singing and dancing in the streets."<ref name="bbcindia" /> His show in [[Mumbai]] was performed in front of a sold out audience of 70,000 in an effort to raise funds to provide jobs for young people in the [[Maharashtra|state of Maharashtra]].<ref name="auto12" /> Upon his arrival, a unprecedented frenzy as 15 km of [[Mumbai|Bombay]]'s streets were filled with fans hoping to catch a glimpse of Michael Jackson who was escorted to his hotel by a 30-car police motorcade.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Pal |first=Sanchari |date=2018-01-18 |title=When Michael Jackson's Music and Moonwalk Rocked Mumbai in 1996 |url=https://www.thebetterindia.com/128269/michael-jackson-mumbai-concert-india-king-of-pop/ |access-date=2024-05-23 |website=The Better India |language=en-US}}</ref> BBC reported this brew tensions and separations within the Shiv Sena party, as oppositions asked how a Hindu [[Right-wing politics|right-wing]] party could back a pop concert which "represented Western values". [[Suketu Mehta]] summed it up, "And well, what is culture? He represents certain values in America which India should not have any qualms in accepting. We should like to accept that part of America that is represented by Jackson." Sony Music Entertainment India stated that millions of Jackson's records had been distributed in the country with Jackson accounting for 26% of all music downloads in India following his death.<ref name="auto12" /><ref>{{Cite web |last=Wilkinson |first=Matt |date=2010-08-30 |title=Michael Jackson tops global download sales list |url=https://www.nme.com/news/music/michael-jackson-203-1287910 |access-date=2024-05-23 |website=NME |language=en-GB}}</ref> British-born hip-hop star [[Hard Kaur]] explained, "Actors, established choreographers, aspiring composers, kids in dance shows [...] Go to any village, any corner in India and you'll find everyone is familiar with the name Michael Jackson."<ref name="auto12" /> Top Bollywood actor [[Shah Rukh Khan]] stated that he was a huge fan of Jackson with Khan later brought Bollywood together two months later in order to pay tribute to Michael Jackson which was televised to Indian audiences.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2009-08-28 |title=SRK ropes in Bollywood stars for MJ tribute |url=https://www.thehindu.com/features/friday-review/music/SRK-ropes-in-Bollywood-stars-for-MJ-tribute/article16877421.ece |access-date=2024-05-23 |work=The Hindu |language=en-IN |issn=0971-751X}}</ref>
In 2011, the [[Bombay High Court]] opened a case against the 1996 tax waive, which named [[Raj Thackeray]], President of [[Maharashtra Navnirman Sena]] (MNS), Wizcraft International and Shiv Sena's employment wing, Shiv Udyog Sena as brainchilds for the event. For ten years, it was transferred to multiple courts including the [[Supreme Court of India]], until 2021 when the [[Maharashtra Council of Ministers|Cabinet]] granted its approval.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-01-07 |title=Michael Jackson concert: Maharashtra govt reaffirms duty waiver, firm to get Rs 3.36 crore |url=https://indianexpress.com/article/cities/mumbai/michael-jackson-concert-maharashtra-govt-reaffirms-duty-waiver-firm-to-get-rs-3-36-crore-7135887/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=The Indian Express |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=After 25 years, Ent Tax waiver granted to Michael Jackson's Mumbai concert |url=https://www.daijiworld.com/news/newsDisplay.aspx?newsID=788688 |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=www.daijiworld.com |language=en}}</ref>
=== Romania === Upon arrival in [[Romania]] in 1992 during the [[Dangerous World Tour]], Jackson was greeted by thousands of fans in what was described at the time as "the biggest-ever performance by a Western pop star in the country that until 1989 was isolated by [[Nicolae Ceaușescu|Ceausescu]]'s [[Stalinism|Stalinist]] regime".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sclavone |first=Ionel |date=2022-10-23 |title=Un eveniment care a rămas în istoria României. Cum a câștigat Ion Iliescu popularitate cu ajutorul lui Michael Jackson |url=https://evz.ro/un-eveniment-care-a-ramas-in-istoria-romaniei-cum-a-castigat-ion-iliescu-popularitate-cu-ajutorul-lui-michael-jackson.html |access-date=2024-07-25 |website=Evenimentul Zilei |language=ro}}</ref> Likened to that of a head of state official visit, he meet with then President [[Ion Iliescu]] at the [[Royal Palace of Bucharest|Royal Palace]] and proceeded to stop by orphanages prior to his concert in front of 70,000 people at Bucharest. ''[[The Seattle Times]]'' confirmed all concert proceeds are to benefit 100,000 orphans housed in squalid state-run institutions.<ref name=":33" /> Over 22,000 security officers were assigned to protect Jackson during the proceedings.<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EplfAAAAIBAJ&dq=michael+jackson+romania+22%2C000&pg=PA2&article_id=4960,4569192 |title=Jackson to visit Dracula's Castles |date=30 Sep 1992 |publisher=Lewiston Morning Tribune |language=en |quote=Michael Jackson was in seclusion on Tuesday, staying at the Estate that belonged to Communist Dictator Nicolae Ceaușescu, but reports of his visit were vying with news of the crucial election.}}</ref> Mass hysteria exploded after the concert, as approximately a thousand young fans of Jackson protest over Jackson's deserving of "the [[Nobel Peace Prize]] because he did more to help children than any politician".<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=L15WAAAAIBAJ&dq=romania+michael+jackson+hysteria&pg=PA79&article_id=6896,6744514 |title=Romanian Teens Crazy about Michael Jackson |date=17 Dec 1992 |publisher=The Spokesman-Review |language=en |quote=Teenage Hysteria exploded in Romania this week as Hundreds of young Michael Jackson fans marched through Bucharest asking for the Nobel Peace Prize to be awarded to their idol.}}</ref> Newspaper [[România liberă|Romania Libera]]'s controversially claimed President Iliescu staged their meeting with Jackson during a financial loss to help him get re-elected, to which his foreign minister [[Adrian Năstase]] denied.<ref>{{Cite web |date=1992-10-11 |title=Jackson stirs Romanian controversy |url=https://www.the-independent.com/news/world/europe/jackson-stirs-romanian-controversy-1556900.html |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=The Independent |language=en}}</ref> A live special broadcast special of the event was released years later ''[[Live in Bucharest: The Dangerous Tour]]'' garnering the highest ratings for HBO in the history of the channel up until that point.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Michael Jackson - Live in Bucharest: The Dangerous Tour |url=https://music.apple.com/mx/music-movie/michael-jackson-live-in-bucharest-the-dangerous-tour/646059478 |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=Apple Music |language=es-MX}}</ref> Years later, a continuous cultural figure in their country, Romanian fans erected a statue for him in Bucharest after his death.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-07-07 |title=Monument dedicated to Michael Jackson in Herastrau Park, Bucharest - Travel Moments In Time - travel itineraries, travel guides, travel tips and recommendations |url=https://travel.prwave.ro/monument-dedicated-to-michael-jackson-in-herastrau-park-bucharest/ |access-date=2024-07-25 |website=travel.prwave.ro |language=en-US}}</ref>
=== China === According to the [[List of best-selling albums in China#Best-selling physical albums|list of best-selling physical albums in China]], Jackson is the best-selling English musician after ''The Lion King'' and ''Titanic'' soundtracks in [[China]].<ref name="sales">{{cite web |last=Becker |first=Jasper |date=December 10, 1995 |title=The last rock star in China |url=https://www.scmp.com/article/142245/last-rock-star-china |url-access=subscription |access-date=June 9, 2024 |work=[[South China Morning Post]] |quote=The best-selling album in China was Michael Jackson's Bad and that only sold 500,000 copies in a country of 1.2 billion}}</ref> [[MinnPost]] cited his appeal largely comes from his association with China's [[Open Door Policy]] and [[Chinese economic reform|economic reform]], which kicked into gear in the 1980s,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gardner |first=Dinah |date=2009-10-28 |title=Why the Chinese are obsessed with the late Michael Jackson |url=https://www.minnpost.com/global-post/2009/10/why-chinese-are-obsessed-late-michael-jackson/ |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=MinnPost |language=en-US}}</ref> after American established diplomatic relations with China the previous year (1979 hegemony versus previously state-controlled radio).<ref>{{Cite web |last=Carter |first=Jimmy |date=1978-12-15 |title=Foreign Relations of the United States, 1977–1980, Volume I, Foundations of Foreign Policy - 104. Address by President Carter to the Nation |url=https://history.state.gov/historicaldocuments/frus1977-80v01/d104 |access-date=2025-01-16 |website=Office of the Historian, Foreign Service Institute - United States Department of State - history.state.gov}}</ref> Due to his popularity, China currently is the country with the largest number of [[List of monuments and memorials to Michael Jackson|statues dedicated to Jackson]], with 10 statues in cities such as [[Guangzhou]], [[Chengdu]], [[Nanning]], [[Xi'an]], [[Zhengzhou]] and [[Wuhan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=10 Interesting MJ Statues You Won't Believe |url=https://www.mjworld.net/news/2015/01/14/10-interesting-mj-statues-you-wont-believe/ |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=www.mjworld.net |language=en-US}}</ref> He has been referenced or had songs used in many Chinese-language films such as ''[[Detective Chinatown 3]]'', ''[[Gong Shou Dao]]'', ''[[Art College 1994]]'', ''[[Old Boys (2010 film)|Old Boys]]'', ''[[Police Story (1985 film)|Police Story]]'', ''[[City Hunter (film)|City Hunter]]'' and ''[[Shaolin Soccer]]''.<ref>{{Cite web |last= |date=2017-10-31 |title=Alibaba's Jack Ma stars in kung fu movie because he's awesome |url=https://thenextweb.com/news/alibaba-jack-ma-jet-li-movie |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=TNW {{!}} Science |language=en}}</ref> According to [[Adam Tsuei]], President of [[Sony Music|Sony Music Entertainment]] Greater China division, Jackson's sales in Asia have continued to be strong despite rampant [[piracy]].<ref name=":34" /> Until today, there are various impersonators scattered across China.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Michael Jackson and his poems remembered in China - People's Daily Online |url=http://en.people.cn/n3/2016/0630/c90782-9079655.html |access-date=2025-01-14 |website=en.people.cn}}</ref>
=== Mexico === [[File:People_Dancing_Thriller.jpg|thumb|13,000 people dancing to Thriller in Mexico City]] Jackson's ''Thriller'' holds the all-time record for [[List of best-selling albums in Mexico|best-selling album in Mexico]], based on [[Asociación Mexicana de Productores de Fonogramas y Videogramas]].<ref name="mexico">{{cite web |title=Certificaciones Encontradas: Michael Jackson |url=http://www.amprofon.com.mx/certificaciones-amprofon.php?disquera=&anio=&tipoAlbum=&tipoCertificacion=&aniocer=&key=michael+jackson&action=Buscar |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160120180113/http://www.amprofon.com.mx/certificaciones-amprofon.php?disquera=&anio=&tipoAlbum=&tipoCertificacion=&aniocer=&key=michael+jackson&action=Buscar |archive-date=20 January 2016 |access-date=12 November 2017 |publisher=[[Asociación Mexicana de Productores de Fonogramas y Videogramas]] (AMPROFON)}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |title=Best-selling album (Mexico) |url=https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/692100-best-selling-album-mexico |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231121075709/https://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/world-records/692100-best-selling-album-mexico |archive-date=November 21, 2023 |access-date=2025-01-16 |work=Guinness World Records |language=en-GB |url-status=live }}</ref> During his 1993 [[Dangerous World Tour]], he gave five concerts at the largest stadium in Latin America, [[Estadio Azteca]] in [[Mexico]] for 550,000 people, breaking an attendance record for greatest number of fans across a run of shows at the stadium.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Mexico: Azteca's birthday in protests' shadow - turmoil before World Cup |url=https://stadiumdb.com/news/2023/06/mexico_aztecas_birthday_in_protests_shadow_turmoil_before_world_cup |access-date=2025-01-16 |website=stadiumdb.com |language=en}}</ref> Through his concert run, he met with former president, [[Carlos Salinas de Gortari]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Michael Jackson "festeja" el 5 de mayo con Carlos Salinas de Gortari - ViveUSA |url=https://www.viveusa.mx/showbiz/michael-jackson-festeja-el-5-de-mayo-con-carlos-salinas-de-gortari/ |access-date=2025-01-16 |website=ViveUSA.mx |language=es}}</ref> There is an active fan community in Mexico. On his birth date following his death, 13,000 fans gathered at the [[Monumento a la Revolución]] for a ''Thriller'' flash mob, which at one point held a Guinness World Record.<ref name=":36">{{Cite web |title=MEXICO: Mexican fans of Michael Jackson break world record to mark singer's birthday |url=https://reuters.screenocean.com/record/300980 |access-date=2025-01-16 |website=Reuters Archive Licensing - Record ID: 300980 |language=en}}</ref> Mexico's Tourism Secretary Alejandro Rojas released a statement, "We are very happy because the initiative came from the people."<ref name=":36" />
=== Czech Republic === To celebrate Jackson's [[HIStory World Tour]], a large statue of the singer was erected in [[Letná Park]], the place once occupied by [[Joseph Stalin]].<ref name=":02">{{Cite web |last=office |first=Kafkadesk Prague |date=2021-09-07 |title=On this Day, in 1996: Michael Jackson kicked off his HIStory World Tour in Prague |url=https://kafkadesk.org/2021/09/07/on-this-day-in-1996-michael-jackson-kicked-off-his-history-world-tour-in-prague/ |access-date=2024-08-07 |website=Kafkadesk |language=en-GB}}</ref> Jackson's airport arrival in [[Prague|Prague, Czech Republic]] was described by a merchant civilian as equivalent to "[[Yuri Gagarin]] returning home after landing on the Moon".<ref>{{Cite web |title=Pollstar Magazine: The Concert Hotwire - Between the Dates section - pg. 17 |url=https://www.worldradiohistory.com/Archive-All-Music/Pollstar/90s/96/Pollstar-1996-09-09.pdf |access-date=2025-01-16 |website=Pollstar Magazine 1996-09-09 via World Radio History |quote=It's just like the arrival of Yuri Gagarin many years ago," said a Prague street merchant, comparing the first man in space with last week's visit by MICHAEL JACKSON to the Czech Republic's capital city.}}</ref> Other people climbed on trees and upon the roofs of buildings in effort to get a glimpse of Jackson, whose security force and associates caused multiple traffic jams and took up 5 floors of the [[InterContinental|InterContinental hotel]] with a crowd of over 5,000 people blocking the streets waiting to see Jackson from his Hotel suite.<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wAwzAAAAIBAJ&dq=pope+prague&pg=PA2&article_id=2276,681181 |title=Michael Jackson outdraws the Pope |date=4 Sep 1996 |publisher=Star-News |language=en |quote=Michael Jackson is bigger than the Pope here, thousands of screeching fans welcomed the King of Pop at the Airport Tuesday as he arrived to kick off his History World Tour. The Crowd was far bigger than the one received by the Pope during his recent visit according to locals.}}</ref><ref name=":02" /> Jackson's concert at [[Prague]] is one of the [[List of most-attended concerts|largest in history]] with an estimated 125,000 in attendance and thousands more outside the fences.<ref>{{Cite web |last= |first= |date=2021-09-07 |title=On this Day, in 1996: Michael Jackson kicked off his HIStory World Tour in Prague |url=https://kafkadesk.org/2021/09/07/on-this-day-in-1996-michael-jackson-kicked-off-his-history-world-tour-in-prague/ |access-date=2025-01-16 |website=Kafkadesk |language=en-GB}}</ref> Before his performance, Jackson was welcomed to [[Prague Castle]] for a meeting with President [[Václav Havel]], and toured the capital city as well as children's home in [[Zbraslav]] and the traumatology centre in Prague's [[Motol University Hospital]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-09-03 |title=25 years since Michael Jackson enchanted Prague |url=https://english.radio.cz/25-years-michael-jackson-enchanted-prague-8727540 |access-date=2025-01-16 |website=Radio Prague International |language=en}}</ref> After his concert, the statue was moved to [[Eindhoven]].<ref>{{Cite web |title="The Jackson Statues"1995 {{!}} The Pop History Dig |url=https://pophistorydig.com/topics/the-jackson-statues-1995/ |access-date=2025-01-18 |website=pophistorydig.com}}</ref>
=== Pakistan === Following his 2009 death, many local television channels in [[Pakistan]] shifted their focus to cover the news, temporarily setting aside the ongoing military campaign against [[Taliban]] militants. This change in programming highlights the global impact of Jackson whom at the time was regarded "internationally acclaimed" by [[Pakistan People's Party]] (PPP) Salim Qureshi Khokhar who asked the House to observe a minute of silence "as he was an Islam".<ref name=":310">{{Cite web |date=2009-11-02 |title=Michael Jackson Made his Mark in Pakistan |url=https://www.voanews.com/a/a-13-2009-06-26-voa19-68818302/413264.html |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=[[Voice of America]] |language=en}}</ref> Minister for Local Government [[Shazia Marri]] interjected the claims, "Jackson's brother may have been a Muslim but there's no confirmation about Jackson having converted to Islam".<ref name=":15">{{Cite web |date=2009-06-29 |title=PAKISTAN: An Unexpected Tribute to MJ |url=https://www.globalissues.org/news/2009/06/29/1975 |access-date=2025-01-24 |website=www.globalissues.org |language=en-gb}}</ref> Professor of International Relations at [[Boston University]] Adil Najam recalled Jackson's cultural relevance in the country, which inspired vast television and music in Pakistan, "There was a time when - irrespective of your economic and social class - the way to be 'tich' was to be like Michael Jackson [...] And no stage show from [[Peshawar]] to [[Karachi]] would ever be complete without the 'performance' of a Michael Jackson clone."<ref name=":15" />
=== Saudi Arabia === {{Blockquote|text=Even inside the most conservative families' households. There was just something about him and his songs that rang true. When we were teenagers, we would often smuggle music by MJ into our school in Saudi Arabia and share it among us by putting the cassettes into generic plastic covers to hide the fact that we were listening to his music. There were fears among the [[Committee for the Promotion of Virtue and the Prevention of Vice (Saudi Arabia)|religious police]] about his "influence" on the young mind, particularly as songs such as Bad and Beat It were copied and sung, and even dubbed into [[Arabic]], by the young and the rebellious. We didn't care about his personal life, it didn't matter. What was important were the songs. We identified with the themes of loneliness and rejection in his lyrics. After the first [[Gulf War]], the young in countries such as [[Saudi Arabia]], [[Iraq]] and [[Kuwait]] listened to his songs for strength and inspiration. I know I did - even if I didn't understand all of the words back then. In many ways - and despite reservations about [[United States foreign policy in the Middle East#Iran–Iraq War (1980–1988)|Washington's recent foreign policy]] - he was a symbol of America as a land of opportunity, especially for a generation of Arabs that had grown up in conflict.|author=Journalist Rym Ghazal|title=''He meant so much to Arabs''|source=[[The National (Abu Dhabi)]]<ref name=arab/>|style=width: 80%; font-size:small; background-color: #E8E9EB}}Michael Jackson's influence in [[Saudi Arabia]] went beyond just being a pop icon; he became a symbol of cultural transcendence and inspiration for large population of Saudis.<ref name="arab">{{Cite web |last=Ghazal |first=Rym |title='He meant so much to Arabs' |url=https://www.thenationalnews.com/world/mena/he-meant-so-much-to-arabs-1.541703 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241207110450/https://www.thenationalnews.com/world/mena/he-meant-so-much-to-arabs-1.541703 |archive-date=2024-12-07 |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=The National (Abu Dhabi) |language=en}}</ref> In the 1980s, public displays of music and dance were traditionally limited and mostly [[banned]] and debunked by the [[Committee for the Promotion of Virtue and the Prevention of Vice (Saudi Arabia)|Committee for the Promotion of Virtue and the Prevention of Vice]], Michael Jackson's music and dance moves provided a unique outlet for self-expression.<ref name=":110">{{Cite web |date=2022-07-15 |title=Michael Jackson broke the colour barrier {{!}} CBC News |url=https://www.cbc.ca/news/entertainment/michael-jackson-broke-the-colour-barrier-1.833673 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220715054753/https://www.cbc.ca/news/entertainment/michael-jackson-broke-the-colour-barrier-1.833673 |archive-date=July 15, 2022 |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=[[Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|CBC]]}}</ref> Despite his music not being openly distributed, fans listened to Jackson's music through illegal pirated copies and bootlegs made on the [[black market]].<ref name="huffpost.com2">{{Cite web |date=2009-07-27 |title=Michael Jackson... The Man Who Rocked The Desert |url=https://www.huffpost.com/entry/michael-jackson-the-man-w_b_221621 |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=HuffPost |language=en}}</ref> Notably, Jackson's unique connection with Prince [[Al Waleed bin Talal Al Saud]] saw potential in Jackson, and was instrumental in Jackson's continued success and influence in the Middle East.<ref name=":40">{{Cite magazine |last=Macleod |first=Scott |date=1997-12-01 |title=MICHAEL JACKSON'S ADVENTURES IN THE ARABIAN MAGIC KINGDOM |url=https://time.com/archive/6731893/michael-jacksons-adventures-in-the-arabian-magic-kingdom/ |access-date=2024-12-30 |magazine=TIME |language=en}}</ref> The two formed a short-lived joint venture called Kingdom Entertainment.<ref>{{Cite web |date=1996-03-19 |title=FRANCE: Saudi Arabian Prince and Michael Jackson announce show business joint venture, Kingdom Entertainment |url=https://reuters.screenocean.com/record/226988 |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=Reuters Archive Licensing |language=en}}</ref> In recent years, Saudi Arabia has continued to honor Michael Jackson's legacy through various tribute shows and events.<ref>{{Cite web |title=THRILLER LIVE! OPENS AT RIYADH SEASON, KSA |url=https://www.broadwayentertainmentgroup.com/BEGNews/thriller-live!-opens-at-riyadh-season,-ksa#:~:text=Michael's%2045%20year%20musical%20history.%20Over%20two,homage%20to%20Jackson's%20legendary%20live%20performances%20and |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=Broadway_Ent_Group |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Abid |first=Ameera |date=July 1, 2018 |title=Saudi fans pay tribute to Michael Jackson |url=https://www.zawya.com/en/life/saudi-fans-pay-tribute-to-michael-jackson-ed65q63e |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=www.zawya.com |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-07-07 |title=Arab World: Good Bye Michael Jackson |url=https://globalvoices.org/2009/07/07/arab-world-good-bye-michael-jackson/ |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=Global Voices |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-07-01 |title=Despite 'Leaving Neverland,' Michael Jackson's Star Undiminished In Middle East |url=https://www.eurasiareview.com/01072019-despite-leaving-neverland-michael-jacksons-star-undiminished-in-middle-east/ |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=Eurasia Review |language=en-US}}</ref> For instance, during the [[Riyadh Season]], a major cultural festival in the capital, Jackson's montages were shown on giant screens every hour from 4:30 PM to midnight, a notable testament to his impact on Saudi people.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Team |first=Admin |date=2021-11-20 |title=Riyadh Season pays tribute to Michael Jackson |url=https://www.mjvibe.com/riyadh-season-pays-tribute-to-michael-jackson/ |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=MJVibe |language=en-US}}</ref>
==== Other Arabian countries ==== Egyptians refer to Jackson as a common cultural icon, with camels being after him,<ref>{{Cite web |title=A week in Egypt with Michael Jackson, the pyramids and unrelenting beauty|url=https://archive.seattletimes.com/archive/20060602/webhaley08/a-week-in-egypt-with-michael-jackson-the-pyramids-and-unrelenting-beauty |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=archive.seattletimes.com}}</ref> and often part of a longer list of films depicting his relevance in the country.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Egypt's film inspired by Michael Jackson attracts international distribution - Film - Arts & Culture |url=https://english.ahram.org.eg/NewsContent/5/32/269276/Arts--Culture/Film/Egypt%E2%80%99s-film-inspired-by-Michael-Jackson-attracts-.aspx |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220128202851/https://english.ahram.org.eg/NewsContent/5/32/269276/Arts--Culture/Film/Egypt%E2%80%99s-film-inspired-by-Michael-Jackson-attracts-.aspx |archive-date=January 28, 2022 |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=Ahram Online |url-status=live }}</ref> ''[[Sheikh Jackson]]'' in particular is based on the country's emotions stirred by the pop star's death, sparking a series of existential questions within the [[Sheikh]] community. In Lebanon, he also caused major mourning tributes,<ref name=":210">{{Cite web |last=America |first=Arab |date=2009-06-26 |title=Lebanon's Teens Mourn Michael Jackson |url=https://www.arabamerica.com/lebanons-teens-mourn-michael-jackson/ |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=Arab America |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-10-30 |title=Michael Jackson movie earns $20 million on first day |url=https://timesofmalta.com/article/michael-jackson-movie-earns-20-million-on-first-day.279568 |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=Times of Malta (Reuters) |language=en-gb}}</ref> to which Lebanese Minister of Information, Walid al-Daouk, remarked, "I'm attending this just to confirm that Beirut is not just tears in the middle of the streets. Lebanon is culture. It is what we see. It's the joy of living."<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-08-26 |title=Man in the Mirror - The Michael Jackson Show' is a huge hit in Beirut |url=http://www.albawaba.com/video/Man-in-the-Mirror-in-Beirut-(source:-AlArabiya)-m4GeBrO8bp0 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120826011815/http://www.albawaba.com/video/Man-in-the-Mirror-in-Beirut-(source:-AlArabiya)-m4GeBrO8bp0 |archive-date=2012-08-26 |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=[[albawaba]]}}</ref> Years later, [[They Don't Care About Us]] single in 2017 was used to protest against the Lebanese government regarding the waste issue which had turned to a health crisis.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-10-29 |title=Une chanteuse libanaise reprend un tube de Michael Jackson pour dénoncer la crise des déchets |url=https://www.france24.com/fr/20151029-liban-crise-dechets-ordures-chanteuse-libanaise-xriss-jor-clip-michael-jackson |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=France 24 |language=fr}}</ref> In the United Arab Emirates, Jackson's visit included several surprise public appearances, which thrilled fans and locals alike, prior to Jackson's visit he was the honored guest of [[Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan|Sheik Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan]] in [[Bahrain]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2005-08-30 |title=Michael Jackson Relaxes In Dubai - CBS News |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/michael-jackson-relaxes-in-dubai/ |access-date=2024-12-30 |website=www.cbsnews.com |language=en-US}}</ref>
=== Japan === [[File:L.A,_June_2014_(14633184011).jpg|left|thumb|Japanese fan leaves a wreath at his memorial site with the message, "Your songs are the best joy of my life"]] Indicative of Japan's devotion to Jackson was the hysteria that his visits caused.<ref>{{Cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AIp-InPZalI |title=Michael Jackson fans go crazy upon his arrival in Japan [1987 'Bad' Concert Tour] |date=2023-05-14 |last=RestlessRick |access-date=2025-01-18 |via=YouTube}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-06-26 |title=Michael Jackson had loyal, generous fans in Japan |url=https://www.sandiegouniontribune.com/2009/06/26/michael-jackson-had-loyal-generous-fans-in-japan/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=San Diego Union-Tribune |language=en-US}}</ref> Japan's music market is [[List of largest recorded music markets|second in the world]] with Jackson being the country's [[List of best-selling music artists in Japan#Best-selling Western acts|highest certified Western male soloist]], even though the majority of the country's sales are not traceable before Japan's music certification [[Recording Industry Association of Japan|RIAJ]] established in 1989.<ref name="RIAJ certifications1">{{cite web |title=RIAJ: Gold Disc Certifications |url=http://www.riaj.or.jp/f/data/cert/gd.html |access-date=November 8, 2015 |publisher=[[Recording Industry Association of Japan]] |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="RIAJ Digital certifications">{{cite web |title=RIAJ: Digital Certifications |url=http://www.riaj.or.jp/f/data/cert/hs.html |access-date=November 8, 2015 |publisher=Recording Industry Association of Japan |language=ja}}</ref> Defense Minister [[Yasukazu Hamada]] has credited him with building a generation with his music.<ref name=":422">{{Cite magazine |last=Masters |first=Coco |date=2009-06-26 |title=Big In Japan: Tokyo Mourns Jackson's Death |url=https://time.com/archive/6947152/big-in-japan-tokyo-mourns-jacksons-death/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |magazine=TIME |language=en}}</ref> Because of his immense popularity starting in the 1970s, he had several advertising deals with [[Suzuki]]'s Love Scooter.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Michael Jackson's unearthed Suzuki commercial from 1979 might be the coolest ever - video |url=https://www.smoothradio.com/artists/michael-jackson/suzuki-commercial-wink-video/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=Smooth |language=en}}</ref> For the initial leg of his first solo tour 1987's [[Bad (tour)|''Bad'']], he told the [[Associated Press]] he chose Japan as his first leg of his tour, "I love Japan [...] It is one of my favorite places in the entire world."<ref>{{Cite web |date=2007-03-05 |title=Japan Welcomes Michael Jackson - CBS News |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/japan-welcomes-michael-jackson/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=www.cbsnews.com |language=en-US}}</ref> Editor Sam Jameson of [[LA Times]] released sale measurements, "No singer, foreign or Japanese, has matched that record (at that point). Nearly 400,000 tickets were sold in advance." The 9-show tour expanded to 14 performances in [[Tokyo]], [[Osaka]] and [[Yokohama]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jameson |first=Sam |date=1987-09-14 |title=JACKSON'S PERFORMANCE CAPTIVATES THE JAPANESE |url=https://www.latimes.com/archives/la-xpm-1987-09-14-ca-4980-story.html |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=Los Angeles Times |language=en-US}}</ref> His infamous note left at Capitol Hotel Tokyu was reprinted on People magazine's October 1987 release showed his handwriting, "I love every race on planet earth". [[MTV Video Music Awards Japan]] anointed him their [[2006 MTV Video Music Awards Japan|Legend Award]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=『MTV Japan Legend Award 2006』 |url=https://ameblo.jp/love-michael/entry-11633607441.html |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=Michael Jackson を語らせて！ |language=ja}}</ref> While visiting for the awards ceremony, he held a Japanese banquet and invited 100 orphans and handicapped children for free.<ref name="recordonline">{{Cite web |last=Talmadge |first=Eric |title=Michael Jackson says he's happy with career, despite 'attempts to hurt me' |url=https://www.recordonline.com/story/entertainment/local/2007/03/08/michael-jackson-says-he-s/52803871007/ |access-date=2025-01-18 |website=Times Herald-Record |language=en-US}}</ref>
His passing had a major cultural effect on Japan, with multi-day coverage of various television tributes and news updates.<ref name=":422" /> Health Minister [[Yōichi Masuzoe]] added, "He was a superstar. It is an extremely tragic loss. But it is fantastic he was able to give so many dreams and so much hope to the people of the world."<ref name=":422" /> A part of Japanese culture is doppelganger industry, related to impersonators, with Jackson being a popular choice to emulate.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Mckirdy |first=Andrew |date=2019-07-04 |title=The life of a professional Japanese doppelganger |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/culture/2019/07/04/stage/life-professional-doppelganger/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=The Japan Times |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-06-27 |title=CHINA/JAPAN: Michael Jackson impersonators lose their inspirational idol |url=https://reuters.screenocean.com/record/464603 |access-date=2025-01-18 |website=Reuters Archive Licensing |language=en}}</ref><ref name="recordonline" />
=== South Korea === Founders of the first largest K-pop record companies [[Lee Soo-man]] ([[SM Entertainment]]) and [[Park Jin-young]] ([[JYP Entertainment]]) have named Michael Jackson's influence in their venture into music, with Park stating his journey in music began particularly when he first heard Jackson's ''Off the Wall'' album<ref>{{Cite web |last=Coley |first=Denise |date=2019-01-14 |title=Korea's Music Mogul Park Jin-young (a.k.a. JYP) |url=https://www.kcrush.com/koreas-music-mogul-park-jin-young-a-k-a-jyp/ |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=K Crush America Magazine |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Local talent, global goals: K-pop agencies look to "glocalize" the genre |url=https://english.hani.co.kr/arti/english_edition/e_entertainment/1100886.html |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=Hankyoreh |language=ko}}</ref> and Lee's business desire began seeing the "meteoric rise" of Jackson while studying in the states.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Lee Soo Man: Taking Korean Pop Culture Global |url=https://www.gsb.stanford.edu/insights/lee-soo-man-taking-korean-pop-culture-global |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=Stanford Graduate School of Business |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=K-Pop Factory Boss Lee Soo ManBy wchung {{!}} 20 Jan, 2025 {{!}} GOLDSEA {{!}} Asian American Supersite |url=https://goldsea.com/article_details/k-pop-factory-boss-lee-soo-man |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=goldsea.com}}</ref> Jackson has been honored in several shows,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Albert |first=Michael |date=2022-01-25 |title=7 K-Pop Idols Who Have Covered Michael Jackson |url=https://trends.kpopmap.com/7-kpop-idols-who-have-covered-michael-jackson/ |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=Trends - In-Depth K-Pop & K-Culture Insights {{!}} Kpopmap |language=en}}</ref> thematically reference in songs,<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-11-16 |title=Michael Jackson: K-Pop's Biggest Idol? |url=https://seoulbeats.com/2014/11/michael-jackson-k-pops-biggest-idol/ |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=Seoulbeats |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-09-21 |title=Netizens uncover the secret behind Red Velvet's "Dumb Dumb" lyrics |url=https://www.koreaboo.com/article/netizens-uncover-the-secret-behind-red-velvets-dumb-dumb-lyrics/ |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=Koreaboo |language=en-US}}</ref> and cited as a major influence to the biggest K-pop solo acts in the country such as [[BoA]], [[Rain (entertainer)|Rain]], [[Jay Park]], [[Hyuna]], [[Chung Ha]], [[Yunho]], [[Kim Junsu]], [[Taemin]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-05-31 |title=아시아의 별 보아, 마이클 잭슨으로 완벽 변신 |url=https://m.ekn.kr/view.php?key=137894 |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=EKN |language=ko |trans-quote=In the last part of the broadcast that day, BoA revealed a performance of Michael Jackson's Billie Jean, which she specifically revealed as her role model}}</ref><ref>{{Cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4bwxyt44b04 |title=Bi Rain on CNN Michael Jackson was my idol |date=2009-09-07 |last= |access-date=2025-01-19 |via=YouTube}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Jay Park shares his inspiration from Michael Jackson, growing interest in female idols, and more during his 'hashtag' interview |url=https://www.allkpop.com/article/2014/09/jay-park-shares-his-inspiration-from-michael-jackson-growing-interest-in-female-idols-and-more-during-his-hashtag-interview |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=allkpop |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=조선일보 |date=2020-07-19 |title=트리플H 현아 "故마이클 잭슨 무대영상 도움 받았다" |trans-title=Hyuna: "I received inspiration from the late Michael Jackson's stage video" |url=https://www.chosun.com/site/data/html_dir/2018/07/18/2018071802109.html |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=조선일보 |language=ko}}</ref><ref>{{Cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JeeUydwthoc |title=(CC) Can a cat-owner & dog-owner be friends? {{!}} Friend Request {{!}} TEN & CHUNG HA |date=2024-02-20 |last= |minutes=12:11 |time-caption=If you can invite one person, dead or alive, for dinner, who would you pick? Chung-ha: Michael Jackson |access-date=2025-01-19 |trans-quote=If you can invite one person, dead or alive, for dinner, who would you pick? Chung-ha: Michael Jackson |via=YouTube}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-06-12 |title=TVXQ's Uknow Yunho Reveals When He Plans To Retire From The Entertainment Industry |url=https://www.koreaboo.com/news/tvxq-uknow-yunho-retirement-plans/ |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=Koreaboo |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Kim Junsu's playlist - Weverse Magazine |url=https://magazine.weverse.io/article/view/358?lang=en |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=magazine.weverse.io |language=en |quote=Junsu: "Michael Jackson's performance made me dream of being on stage, and I actually performed this several times on stage, too. You asked me which songs have influenced me as a singer, musical actor, and in everything else I've done. Well, I think I could say this is the song that was the origin of my dream of being on stage. This song was an extremely big influence on me."}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last= |date=2017-08-11 |title=SHINee's Taemin Talks About His Hopes As An Artist |url=https://www.soompi.com/article/1025829wpp/shinees-taemin-talks-hopes-artist |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=Soompi |language=en-US |quote=Taemin: "I came across his 'Smooth Criminal' music video by chance, and I saw his 'lean' move. I started dancing because I was so amazed by it."}}</ref> as well as several idol members from [[Exo]], [[BTS]], [[Super Junior]], [[NCT (group)|NCT]], [[Seventeen (South Korean band)|Seventeen]], among others.<ref>{{Cite web |last=전여울 |date=2023-02-23 |title=Days With You, Baekhyun from EXO |url=https://www.wkorea.com/2023/02/23/eng-days-with-baekhyun/ |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=W korea |language=ko-KR |quote=Baekhyun: There isn't an artist who can beat Michael Jackson.}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=권은경 |date=2023-01-20 |title=Jimin(BTS) in Full Bloom |url=https://www.wkorea.com/2023/01/20/in-fullbloom-jimin/ |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=W korea |language=ko-KR |quote=Jimin: I have to say Michael Jackson for me too. I really wish I could have seen him while he was still alive.}}</ref><ref>{{Cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9GgVK6epy1E |title=SUPER JUNIOR-D&E Takes The BFF Test |date=2021-11-09 |last=BuzzFeed Celeb |minutes=2:45 |access-date=2025-01-20 |via=YouTube}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-06-13 |title=Haechan of NCT and NCT 127: Confident, lovable and full of mischief |url=https://www.scmp.com/lifestyle/entertainment/article/3088769/haechan-nct-and-nct-127-talented-and-confident-performer |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=South China Morning Post |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-08-29 |title=9 Kpop Idols Inspired by Michael Jackson for Their Art: NCT's Haechan, SEVENTEEN's Dino and Others {{!}} Leisurebyte |url=https://www.leisurebyte.com/kpop-idols-inspired-by-michael-jackson/ |access-date=2025-01-20 |language=en-US}}</ref> In Seoul, fashion wholesaler Shin Ji-hyun said Jackson was the most popular foreign superstar in the country.<ref>{{Cite web |title=In Asia, Jackson recalled as iconic, troubled |url=https://www.stripes.com/migration/in-asia-jackson-recalled-as-iconic-troubled-1.92909 |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=Stars and Stripes |language=en}}</ref> Additionally, choreographers such as [[:ko:리아킴 (안무가)|Lia Kim]], [[Rino Nakasone]] and Rie Hata have cited him as their main influence.<ref name=":322">{{Cite web |title="마이클 잭슨처럼"…'대화의 희열' 리아킴 밝힌 #가시나 #백업댄서 #K댄스(종합)[Oh!쎈 리뷰]: 네이트 연예 |url=https://news.nate.com/view/20190414n10700 |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=네이트 뉴스 |language=ko}}</ref><ref name=":45">{{Cite web |title=[Exclusive] Interview With Rino Nakasone (@RinOkinawa) |url=https://www.soshified.com/2011/10/exclusive-interview-with-rino-nakasone-rinokinawa |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=www.soshified.com}}</ref><ref name=":46">{{Cite web |date=2022-04-12 |title=7 K-pop choreographers you need to know - Rie Hata, Bada Lee, Sienna Lalau, Spella, and more |url=https://www.bandwagon.asia/articles/7-k-pop-choreographers-the-music-industry-rie-hata-bada-lee-sienna-lalau-spella-bts-bieber-aespa-taeyong-txt-red-velvet-#:~:text=Inspired%20by%20the%20likes%20of,aspiring%20dancers%20look%20up%20to. |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=7 K-pop choreographers you need to know - Rie Hata, Bada Lee, Sienna Lalau, Spella, and more {{!}} Bandwagon {{!}} Music media championing and spotlighting music in Asia. |language=en}}</ref>
In late 1996, Jackson performed two nights at [[Seoul Olympic Stadium]] to an audience of 100,000.<ref name=":16">{{Cite web |date=2020-04-24 |title=Michael Jackson's many connections to Korea |url=https://www.koreatimes.co.kr/www/opinion/2025/01/137_288162.html |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=The Korea Times |language=en}}</ref> He had spoken positively of the culture in Korea,<ref>{{Cite news |last=Glain |first=Steve |date=1997-12-16 |title=In This Michael Jackson Video, He Sings South Korea's Praises |url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/SB882223934979603000 |access-date=2025-01-20 |work=Wall Street Journal |language=en-US |issn=0099-9660}}</ref> and had taken a particular liking to [[Bibimbap]].<ref>{{Cite web |last= |first= |date=2021-11-15 |title=The History of Bibimbap |url=https://sweekly.co.kr/the-history-of-bibimbap/ |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=Scholars Weekly |language=en-US}}</ref> The following year, he began a friendship with President-elect [[Kim Dae-jung]].<ref name=":16" /> Jackson, who rarely gets involved with politics, took part in an international video conference supporting Kim's candidacy for the [[1997 South Korean presidential election]], stating "Korea is a country of warmth, love, sincerity and complete innocence." He attended Kim's inaugural ceremony.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Reuters Archive Licensing |url=https://reuters.screenocean.com/record/992221 |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=Reuters Archive Licensing |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":43">{{Cite web |date=2009-06-26 |title=DJ Expresses Condolence for Jackson's Death |url=https://www.koreatimes.co.kr/www/nation/2025/01/113_47531.html |access-date=2025-01-20 |website=The Korea Times |language=en}}</ref> Around the same time, Jackson had met a Korean adviser named Myung Ho Lee. Lee would eventually emerged as a central figure in the performer's [[debt]] binge.<ref name=":44">{{Cite news |last=O'Brien |first=Timothy L. |date=2006-05-12 |title=Jackson faces demise of a financial Neverland |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2006/05/12/business/worldbusiness/12iht-michael-web.html |access-date=2025-01-19 |work=The New York Times |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}}</ref> Another former financial adviser Alvin Malnik estimates that some of Jackson's advisers squandered $50 million to $100 million on deals that never panned out. It was eventually settled out of court.<ref name=":44" /> In 1999, he held ''[[MJ & Friends]]'' in Seoul. In the year of his death, tributes formed on television and memorials, with Kim Dae-jung releasing a statement, "Today, we lost a hero and a dear friend of Korea who supported our country's cause for reunification and peace."<ref name=":43" />
=== Poland === In front of a crowd of over 120,000 people, Michael Jackson performed his concert at the [[Warsaw Babice Airport|Bemowo airport]], which makes it one of the [[List of most-attended concerts|largest concerts of all time]] and, to date, the biggest concert in [[Poland]]'s history.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Jackson received by Polish president - UPI Archives |url=https://www.upi.com/Archives/1996/09/18/Jackson-received-by-Polish-president/6019843019200/ |access-date=2024-11-20 |website=UPI |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":37">{{Cite web |date=2023-09-20 |title=Michael Jackson's Landmark Performance in Poland Celebrates 27th Anniversary |url=https://beautifulwarszawa.home.blog/2023/09/20/michael-jackson/ |access-date=2024-11-20 |website=Beautiful Warszawa |language=en}}</ref> Jackson arrived in Poland on September 18, 1996 at [[Warsaw Chopin Airport|Okęcie International Airport]] and a mini ceremony welcomed by children dressed in traditional Polish attire presented him with bouquets of flowers.<ref name=":38">{{Cite web |date=2021-09-20 |title=OTD – 25 years ago, Michael Jackson performed for the first and last time in Warsaw! |url=https://beautifulwarszawa.home.blog/2021/09/20/otd-25-years-ago-michael-jackson-performed-for-the-first-and-last-time-in-warsaw/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=Beautiful Warszawa |language=en}}</ref> As he traveled to his hotel, a police motorcade escorted him, and thousands of fans lined the streets, cheering and waving with excitement.<ref name=":38" /> In return, Jackson frequently interacted with them and engaging in playful conversations. Before the concert, Jackson was welcomed to the [[Presidential Palace, Warsaw|Presidential Palace]] where he had a scheduled meeting with then President [[Aleksander Kwaśniewski|Aleksander Kwasniewski]] who welcomed him with his wife [[Jolanta Kwaśniewska]].<ref name=":39">{{Cite web |title=Polskie Radio Esperanto - Polish fans mourn King of Pop |url=https://www2.polskieradio.pl/eo/dokument.aspx?iid=110879 |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=www2.polskieradio.pl}}</ref> After a meeting with Warsaw mayor [[Marcin Święcicki]], he visited an orphanage on No. 4 Rakowiecka Street in the north of [[Mokotów]] to where chorus children gathered to sing [[Heal the World]].<ref name=":37" /> Jackson had a positive experience in Poland, stating he considered to "buy a castle in the beautiful country".<ref name=":39" /> By 1997 he met with business partners with plans to open an amusement park; learning at the time there were no major theme parks in the Warsaw area.<ref>{{Cite web |date=1998-06-30 |title=Michael Jackson wants to build park in Poland |url=https://www.deseret.com/1998/6/30/19388734/michael-jackson-wants-to-build-park-in-poland/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=Deseret News |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=The Unfinished 'Amusement and Sports Park' Project in Warsaw from 1921the First Vision for an Olympic District in the Capital City of the Second Polish Republic? {{!}} Playing Pasts |url=https://www.playingpasts.co.uk/articles/olympics/the-unfinished-amusement-and-sports-park-project-in-warsaw-from-1921the-first-vision-for-an-olympic-district-in-the-capital-city-of-the-second-polish-republic/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=1997-07-13 |title=Jackson may build amusement park in Poland |url=https://www.deseret.com/1997/7/13/19323432/jackson-may-build-amusement-park-in-poland/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |website=Deseret News |language=en}}</ref> Kwasniewski released a statement on Jackson, "He was a king, an extraordinary talent but at the same time a very sensitive person".<ref name=":39" /> His theme park plan was still in talks years later, though had never came to fruition after the years-long proposal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-09-21 |title=Polish parks – theme or dream? - Park World Online - Theme Park, Amusement Park and Attractions Industry News |url=https://parkworld-online.com/polish-parks-theme-or-dream/ |access-date=2025-01-17 |language=en-GB}}</ref>
=== Soviet Union/Russia === Less than a decade before the 1991 [[Transition period and cessation of the existence of the Soviet Union|cessation of the Soviet Union]], Western music was particularly banned in the country. In 1984, New York Times confirmed [[Kultura (newspaper)|Sovetskaya Kultura]]'s denouncing stance of ''Thriller'', proliferating the news <nowiki>''</nowiki>The great show-biz swindle [...] given rise to a kind of euphoria.<nowiki>''</nowiki> The article also accused Jackson of mesmerizing Americans and keeping them from thinking about political topics, such as violence in Miami or Lebanon, that would make the White House uncomfortable.<ref>{{Cite news |last= |date=1984-06-21 |title=Russians Denounce Michael Jackson |url=https://www.nytimes.com/1984/06/21/arts/russians-denounce-michael-jackson.html |access-date=2025-01-18 |work=The New York Times via UPI |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}}</ref> After the Soviet Union became Russia, publications described Michael Jackson was "perhaps, the focus of all those contradictions between Soviet and later Russian society and the world of Western show business."<ref name="russkiy">{{Cite web |title=Michael Jackson and Contemporary Russian History |url=https://russkiymir.ru/en/publications/139544/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150609203954/https://russkiymir.ru/en/publications/139544/ |archive-date=2015-06-09 |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=[[Russkiy Mir Foundation]] russkiymir.ru |language=en}}</ref> Prior to his 1993 concert during the Dangerous World Tour, Michael Jackson's first appearance in the [[Soviet Union]] actually came during a promotional campaign with the [[PepsiCo|Pepsi Corporation]], where Jackson was starred in the first ever American advertisements shown on Soviet television. The TV spots were aired to a estimated 150,000,000 viewers within the Soviet Union making it one of the most watched broadcasts of all time.<ref>{{Cite web |date=May 4, 1988 |title=Pepsi-Cola to advertise on Soviet TV - UPI Archives |url=https://www.upi.com/Archives/1988/05/04/Pepsi-Cola-to-advertise-on-Soviet-TV/2747578721600/ |access-date=2025-01-27 |website=[[UPI]] |language=en |quote=Aired before an estimated 150 million Soviet viewers, was co-produced by Gostelradio and KING-TV of Seattle and taped in that city.}}</ref>
A documentary ''Michael Jackson - Moscow Case 1993: When The King Of Pop Met The Soviets'' detail events after the [[1993 Michael Jackson sexual abuse allegations]], leading up to his controversial 1993 [[Dangerous World Tour]] [[Moscow, Russia]] concert, six days before the [[1993 Russian constitutional crisis]] arose.<ref>{{Cite web |date=1993-01-01 |title=Michael Jackson - Moscow Case 1993: When The King Of Pop Met The Soviets - Apple TV |url=https://tv.apple.com/us/movie/michael-jackson---moscow-case-1993-when-the-king-of-pop-met-the-soviets/umc.cmc.66jxlpvnz37qf4h9o4uacj5l1 |access-date=2025-01-18 |website=Apple TV |language=en-US}}</ref> Jackson had been escorted by the [[Police of Russia|Russian police]],<ref>{{Cite news |title=Moscow Likes Michael |url=https://www.csmonitor.com/1993/0923/23131.html |access-date=2025-01-19 |work=Christian Science Monitor |issn=0882-7729}}</ref> and met with Moscow's first-standing mayor [[Yury Luzhkov|Yuri Luzhkov]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=MICHAEL JACKSON TOURS MOSCOW. |url=https://jamestown.org/program/michael-jackson-tours-moscow/ |access-date=2025-01-18 |website=jamestown.org |language=en-US}}</ref> The early morning of the concert, several confusing events were reported: ticket sales stagnated at 50,000 of total 70,000 seats, though the booths had a "sold out" sign and office was closed for further sale.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Lally |first=Kathy |date=1993-09-16 |title=Michael Jackson heats up rainy night in Moscow |url=https://www.baltimoresun.com/1993/09/16/michael-jackson-heats-up-rainy-night-in-moscow/ |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=Baltimore Sun |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name=":28">{{Cite web |last=Andreeva |first=Olga |date=2023-09-15 |title=Exactly 30 years ago, Michael Jackson became the first world pop star to perform at Luzhniki (Ровно 30 лет назад Майкл Джексон первым из мировых поп-звезд выступил в "Лужниках" - Москвич Mag) |url=https://moskvichmag.ru/lyudi/rovno-30-let-nazad-majkl-dzhekson-pervym-iz-mirovyh-pop-zvezd-vystupil-v-luzhnikah/ |access-date=2025-01-19 |website=Moskvich Mag |language=ru-RU}}</ref> It had been raining and following reports of a [[bomb threat]] as well as news Michael's team brought drugs, had police officers stop activities to search the premises, such that intelligence agents dragged a police officer who was found under the stage stairs. No drugs or bomb were found.<ref name=":28" /><ref>{{Cite news |title=15 сентября 1993 года: первый концерт Майкла Джексона в Москве |url=https://spletnik.ru/25609-15-sentyabrya-1993-goda-pervyj-koncert-majkla-dzheksona-v-moskve-130488 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240613122933/https://spletnik.ru/25609-15-sentyabrya-1993-goda-pervyj-koncert-majkla-dzheksona-v-moskve-130488 |archive-date=June 13, 2024 |access-date=2025-01-19 |language=ru-RU |url-status=live }}</ref> Some ticketless audience that had gathered around the stadium were allowed entry despite organizers having lost money.<ref name=":28" /> As it was an open-air concert, spectators were drenched, and also reportedly drunk.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Tribune |first=Chicago |date=1993-09-16 |title=JACKSON NO THRILLER IN RAINY MOSCOW CONCERT |url=https://www.chicagotribune.com/1993/09/16/jackson-no-thriller-in-rainy-moscow-concert/ |access-date=2025-01-18 |website=Chicago Tribune |language=en-US}}</ref>{{Blockquote|text=Indeed, Jackson's ascent to the heights of fame occurred during an era of critical change in the Soviet Union (and all those governments that arose in its place). This era was also life-changing on the question of how Soviet popular culture related to global trends and developments. One can speak of "progress" in this case without any [[irony]]. [...] Indeed, six days later, [[Boris Yeltsin]] would issue Decree No. 1400 on the Dissolution of the Supreme Soviet, thus laying the foundation for the bloody events of October 3–4. It was precisely this time that most clearly separated the Soviet period from the history of post-Soviet Russia. And in the fact that the last days of Soviet history culminated in a carnival involving Michael Jackson, one can see some kind of sad irony, not to mention in the fact that the concert actually ended in failure: the stage at [[Luzhniki Stadium]] failed to operate at full capacity under the heavy rain, and the public was drenched under the old and still unequipped roof. As a parenthetical note, this concert was one of the last non-sports events that took place before the arena was closed for reconstruction. It was from this point that the "Era of Luzhkov" began.|author=|title=''Michael Jackson and Contemporary Russian History''|source=[[Russkiy Mir Foundation]]<ref name="russkiy"/>|style=width: 80%; font-size:small; background-color: #E8E9EB}}The events in the past months up to September 1993 and the concert greatly impacted Jackson's health and led to the cancellation of the rest of his tour by November. It would also inspire his song "[[Stranger in Moscow]]".<ref name=":28" />
==Tabloid media== {{See also|Health and appearance of Michael Jackson}} [[File:King_of_Propotol_(6011558404).jpg|thumb|One of a few "King of Propofol" graffities of Jackson in [[Porto]]]] {{Blockquote|quote=The Michael Jackson cacophony is fascinating in that it is not about Jackson at all. I hope he has the good sense to know it and the good fortune to snatch his life out of the jaws of a carnivorous success. He will not swiftly be forgiven for having turned so many tables, for he damn sure grabbed the [[brass ring]], and the man who broke the bank at [[Monte Carlo Casino|Monte Carlo]] has nothing on Michael. All that noise is about America, as the dishonest custodian of black life and wealth; the blacks, especially males, in America; and the burning, buried American guilt; and sex and sexual roles and sexual panic; money, success, and despair—to all of which may now be added the bitter need to find a head on which to place the crown of Miss America.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cusd80.com/cms/lib/AZ01001175/Centricity/Domain/1073/Full%20Text%20Here-be-Dragons-James-Baldwin.pdf|title=Here Be Dragons|last=Baldwin|first=James}}</ref><ref>Baldwin, James. "Freaks and the American Ideal of Manhood". ''Collected Essays''. 828. 1998.</ref>|author=[[James Baldwin]]|source="Freaks and the American Ideal of Manhood" (1985) }}
At the height of his fame, during the 1980s, Jackson began to embrace and perpetuate the public perception of his strangeness. Jackson (and his publicity team) and the media worked in tandem to cultivate this image.<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 2679.</ref> Early tabloid stories of his being obsessed with the [[Joseph Merrick|Elephant Man]]'s bones and sleeping in an "oxygen chamber" were possibly [[publicity stunt]]s. Around this time, the tabloid newspaper ''[[The Sun (United Kingdom)|The Sun]]'' began nicknaming Jackson "Wacko Jacko", a name he came to despise. Other tabloids and media outlets soon followed. The nickname stayed with Jackson for the rest of his career. Stories about him gradually turned negative. In Vogel's description: "Critics maligned him for buying the Beatles catalog [in 1985], mocked his changing appearance, called him a sissy, questioned whether he actually wrote his songs, reduced his art to commercial ephemera."<ref>Vogel, (Kindle location) 2729.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theatlantic.com/entertainment/archive/2012/09/how-michael-jackson-made-bad/262162/|title=How Michael Jackson Made 'Bad'|first=Joseph|last=Vogel|date=September 10, 2012|website=The Atlantic|access-date=October 31, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161218073140/http://www.theatlantic.com/entertainment/archive/2012/09/how-michael-jackson-made-bad/262162/|archive-date=December 18, 2016|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.washingtonpost.com/wp-dyn/content/article/2007/08/31/AR2007083101466.html|title=The Michael Vicks of Yore|via=www.washingtonpost.com|access-date=October 31, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191031155940/http://www.washingtonpost.com/wp-dyn/content/article/2007/08/31/AR2007083101466.html|archive-date=October 31, 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> His marriage to [[Lisa Marie Presley]] and rumours of him undergoing skin-whitening and other cosmetic surgery furthered the controversy surrounding Jackson's image and made him the subject of sensationalist biographies.{{sfn|Kooijman|2006|pp=119–20}}
Writing in British ''Vogue'' in 1987, [[Barney Hoskyns]] said that Jackson occupied a "superstar stratosphere of his own" and part of the public's misunderstanding of the star was "because we so ''want'' to know him – as we want to know anyone that famous". Hoskyns described the tabloid image of Jackson as "despicable ... distortions", but nevertheless opined: "Michael Jackson represents a terrible, pitiful corruption of what a twenty-nine-year-old human being really should be ... His neurotic obsession with perfecting his physical appearance apparently knows no bounds.
